rows.description:
Hello team!


a presentation for a governmental gathering. This presentation will be displayed on a screen, with the presenters standing in front of it. The dimensions are already defined.

We'll start by animating specific slides: 1, 4, and 7.


Slide 1:

Our aim is to create a smooth and engaging animation. Each flag will be animated individually, gradually converging into circles aligned in a square, with the UAE flag at the center. This motion signifies the UAE's central role in bringing various groups together.

We'll also include subtle, fading-in-and-out circles in the background to represent the countries. Each flag will have a corresponding title, synchronized with the PowerPoint timing for ease of editing.


Slide 4:

It addresses climate change and global warming. We will incorporate footage of the natural disasters mentioned below the video, animating them in a way to align with the globe footage. We may explore alternative background footage options to convey global warming in a more subtle manner.


Slide 7:

It will feature a similar animation to create a UAE map using mini logos which will be animated together. We look forward to receiving the logo choices from you by tomorrow.

Regarding color selection and other design choices, we have the flexibility to decide what best suits the presentation.


Thanks!

Hello team 👋,


Background:


We have a new client, Richemont, the owner of many well-known Maisons renowned for their excellence in jewelry, watches, fashion, and accessories, including brands like Cartier, Van Cleef and Arpels, Piaget, and Mont Blanc.


Objective:


Richemont is hosting their annual MEIA Regional Finance townhall and seeks our assistance in redesigning their presentation. They found last year's presentation too professional and dull and aim to make this year's session more engaging, interactive, and enjoyable. With the event being held online, their primary challenge lies in ensuring active participation and engagement from all attendees. The townhall will primarily focus on summarizing key highlights from 2024 and outlining focus areas for 2025.


Scope:

  • We will amplify up to 25 slides with subtle transitions and animations
  • Create two concept options
  • Develop a session itinerary
  • Design a background for the virtual call (providing two design options).

Design:


We have flexibility to add creativity into both the presentation format and design.


Milestones and Deadline:


Wed 3 - Thurs 4 Apr: Receive first draft of content.

Wed 3 - Thurs 4 Apr: Begin seeking inspiration.

Fri 5 Apr: Kick-off call with Richemont.

Mon 8 - Fri 12 Apr: Eid holiday.

Mon 15 - Tue 16 Apr: Concept creation.

Wed 17 Apr - Tue 23 Apr: Design the presentation, session itinerary, and call background.


Assets and Links:


2023 Finance regional townhall presentation - [link]

2024 Finance regional townhall agenda - [link]

Richemont Template - [link]

Richemont guides - [link]


Thank you! 🌟

Helloooooo team, 


We just landed our first project from Innovethics. Nailing this one could pave the way for numerous future exciting projects!


About Innovethics:


Innovethics is a boutique consultancy firm specializing in financial advisory services and solutions.


Project Overview:


Innovethics require our assistance in amplifying and optimizing their presentation by restructuring the content.


Milestones:


-Content optimization 

- Two Concept options 

-Amplification of the remaining slides after finalizing the content (~15 slides)


Assets:

  • Incorporate more details from their LinkedIn page for point 1.
  • Rewrite the content based on additional information provided by Muhannad in slides 10 to 12 for point 6.

Additionally, the client seeks our guidance on restructuring the presentation, determining what to include and remove.


For example:

  • Slide 13: Advise on whether to include all the leadership members and what information should be added or removed.
  • Slides 7 and 8: Advise on whether to include testimonials from clients.
  • The client is also open to our suggestions on whether to keep the vision and mission at the end of the presentation or make any adjustments.

Design Brief:


  • Brand guidelines are embedded in the presentation through the colors, font, logo and elements. The presentation could adhere to the client's brand guidelines in terms of colors, elements, and logo, however, slight deviations are acceptable.
  • The client likes the cover and prefers a similar style for the separator on slide 9. For slides 7 & 8, he prefers the clients to be organized by sectors but welcomes our direction on how to present them.

In summary, the client is open to suggestions and guidance from us.


Plan:


- Kick off content work on Monday, May 20th (Amani E)

- Kick off concept work on Monday, May 20th (TBD)

- After concept and content approval, design the finalized file (TBD)


Deliverables:


- Content Presentation on Thursday, May 23rd

- Concepts Presentation on Thursday, May 23rd 

- Complete the full file by Tuesday, May 28


Thank you and let me know if you have any questions!

Objective

Prepare a corporate profile for Saal that effectively targets the right audience with the right message.


Target Audience

C-suite executives of large clients.


Tone

Professional and formal.


Brand Guidelines

Adhere to Saal's brand guidelines, including colors and fonts from the existing presentation. Ensure it is consistent with the other slides that we won't be designing while introducing a new concept to enhance visual appeal, as the current presentation lacks visuals. Maintain the existing layout as much as possible for consistency.


Final Output

An editable PowerPoint presentation designed for a regular laptop display.


Images

Incorporate diverse images of objects related to tech, data, AI, and software. Avoid using images of people. 


Theme

Opt for a light theme.


Slide-by-Slide Updates


Slide 5:

Define the purpose and problem being addressed, making the slide more engaging to deliver the right message.


Slide 7:

People related slide, enhance with more visuals to make it appealing and engaging.


Slide 8:

Showcase the core AI product's strengths, capabilities, and benefits in a more engaging manner.


Slide 9:

The remaining products, increase visual elements to complement the text and logo.


Slide 11:

Shows the market and the industry, the landscape, some key data points. We can keep the same and only add icons or we can create a new design for this slide. 


Slide 12:

Redesign the layout and update the image for a better visual appeal.


Slide 14:

A complex page. This is the process and components to deliver the solution to the client. If we redesigned the layout, we will make sure that the message and process remains intact; otherwise we will keep it as is and only enhance it.


Slide 15:

Needs most work, This is the process to deliver the solution to the client. Visuals are off, and out of context. Showing all the use cases of Saal across the industries, data and AI, verticals, portfolio, etc..The organization of the page is correct, but the logos and everything is out of context.


Slide 18:

Organize the case study components for clarity and ease of replication.

Hello team , I hope you're all doing well! 

 

We've received a presentation from our new client, Dubai International Marine Club (DIMC), with Osama as our PoC.

 

Background:

DIMC, located in Mina Seyahi, initially had a natural slipway for boats and dhows. It was HH Sheikh Ahmed Bin Rashid Al Maktoum's vision that transformed the area into a modern marina for yacht owners.

 

Objectives:

Osama and his manager aim to pitch this presentation to the Sheikh to facilitate changes in land use scope. By discussing this with the Sheikh, they hope to streamline processes, eliminating the need for NoC and making entry and exit smoother.

 

Scope:

2 concept creation

We will amplify up to 15 slides

Content optimization and restructuring of 15 slides

Content writing of 15 slides

 

Brief:

You can find the presentation here: الاستثمار وزيادة الموازنة.pptx 

 

 Content:

  • The attached presentation requires content rewriting and optimization, focusing on headlines, titles, and bullet points rather than lengthy paragraphs. 
  • The aim is to propose initiatives to the Sheikh concisely, considering the limited time available. Four projects are central to this presentation, with Dubai Harbor Mina Seyahi being the primary focus. The second project stems from the first, and the fourth involves accommodation for employees. 
  • The presentation should be cohesive, with a clear storytelling flow, emphasizing impact over financial aspects.

Design:

  • The design should be clean and minimalistic, similar to ADEO, with minimal transitions and animations.

Next Steps:

  • We'll have an internal call today to align on the approach and determine if we need support from Dina Abou Soufeh. 
  • Check who will be responsible for the design.

Timeline:

The final presentation should be ready for sharing by May 31st.

Hello Team!

I hope you're having a great start to your week! We have a new project for Fly Emirates which will kick off between today and tomorrow.

Designers working on this for the concept phase:

  • Rand A
  • Razan Shihab (freelancer), will start on Tuesday
  • Nightshift

Razan Sh will continue with the execution after the approval, and a detailed plan will be shared. Our aim is to deliver the file internally by the 15th, depending on the round of feedback she provides.

Background:

Fly Emirates is the largest airline in the Middle East, operating over 3,600 flights per week from its hub at Terminal 3 of Dubai International Airport. It operates to more than 150 cities in 80 countries across six continents on its fleet of nearly 300 aircraft.

We are going to support them with the "Pilot Roadshow presentation." The aim of this presentation is to be used in an event/roadshow for recruiters to attract talent for first officers and pilots to join Emirates and relocate to Dubai. The presentation includes:

  • The Emirates Story
  • Minimum Requirements for first officers and pilots to join
  • Application Process for applying to Emirates
  • Pay package
  • Benefits of joining Emirates
  • Rostering & Annual Leave planners
  • Living in Dubai (luxury, safe, touristic)

The Ask:

It is the presentation that emirates use in their roadshows to attract and hire Pilots. It is presented face to face most of the time, so its important to take readiblity into consideration. They dont want it too flashy, but to be professionally design. As Samantha puts it, it should be "cool, prestigious, and impressive, making it super glossy."

The client is looking for a creative, simple, bold, and clear presentation to impress the audience and have all the information presented clearly in one presentation. Keywords: travel, aviation, pilots, Dubai, tourism.

Objectives:

  • 3 concept creations: Directions to be discussed with Razan (see references below). Each concept should be up to 6 slides.
  • 2 hours for gathering inspirations: Then meet with the team to align.
  • Proposal: Showcasing the three concepts, with ideas/mood boards if needed.
  • Amplify the presentation: Up to 55 slides.

Scope & Brief:

Here you can find the presentation along with the logo and brand guidelines:

  • Format Size: Typical PPT horizontal format 16:9
  • Color Scheme: The presentation should be light-themed as per Emirates guidelines. It should remain simple and bright, using the primary color of Emirates "Red." We can go for full images or a dark theme on some slides if needed. Additionally, we can use supporting/secondary colors along with the red, such as light sky blue, silver, or goldish but keep it toned down.
    We can also use bright to dark red gradients as per guidelines.
  • Consistency: Ensure the design carries a consistent theme and overall direction/idea. 
  • Fonts: We will use two fonts: the primary Emirates font for big headlines or titles, and Helvetica Neue for body text.
  • Images: Focus on imagery to make the presentation lively, with an emphasis on pilots, cabin crew, people, and destination images. Tourism and aviation images such as planes are also important. Images can be requested from the client once the direction is approved.
  • Level of Information Design: Remember, this is going to be presented at an event. Keep it simple and informative with big, bold numbers and nice layouts. Avoid adding a lot of infographics and complicated charts.
  • Transitions and Animations: Add transitions and animations to ensure the flow of the storytelling. However, interactive clicks are not possible as this presentation will be used on stage with a clicker.
  • Speaker Notes: Make sure to add all speaker notes to the existing slides.

Design Directions:

Concept 1:

  • Parallax effect with imagery and bold colors. Get creative with transitions, moving from one scene to another. For example, start with an airplane moving in the sky, clouds transitioning to mountains and landscapes, moving from one destination to another. Use a lot of image cutouts, maybe a map, and think about take-off and landing.
    - https://www.behance.net/gallery/89453059/Emirates-Discover-your-new-world
    - https://www.behance.net/gallery/110713507/Live-a-new-experience-Fly-Emirates
    - https://www.behance.net/gallery/199464141/Air-Arabia-Social-Media-(Unofficial)
    - STA sample shared with the client that she really liked: STA Immersive 1.pptx 

Concept 2:

  • The pilot shoulder badge. Play around with the idea of adding the levels of a career plan for a pilot. When first officers join, they have three golden stripes on their shoulder badge, and when they get promoted to pilot, they have four stripes. Use the element of stripes as diagonal-shaped graphics and place the images inside the stripes. 

Concept 3: (open for discussion if we have more ideas)

  • Focus more on imagery, illustrations, and bold typography (marketing/agency/pitch presentation style). It should be fun, playful, and BOLD. Maybe use a compass navigating through the slides, representing speed, growth, and movement. A nice idea would be water salutes too.

https://www.behance.net/gallery/199464141/Air-Arabia-Social-Media-(Unofficial)

Things to Avoid:

  • White text on a full red background, unless it's a big title, as it's super hard to read. 

Assets and Links:

  • Odoo link here
  • Drive link here containing all the assets needed
  • ⁠44754_EKFB - Advertising VSG (2).pdf
  • 5507_EK - BTL Collaterals_Mini Guideline (2).pdf
  • 63826_EK - Logo_font_colour_Mini Guideline (2).pdf
  • Final Corperate Presentation_Concept2_PLMB 3.pptx

Timeline & Milestones:

  • Mon 22 - Thurs 25 July: Rand, Razan & night shift to work on 3 concepts
  • Thurs 25th of July: Schedule a call with the client and share the concepts and proposal
  • Thurs 25 - Wed 31st of July: Feedback and edits if any
  • Wed 31st of July - Tue 6th of Aug: WIP up to 20 slides
  • Tue 6th of Aug: share the WIP
  • Room for feedback
  • Thurs 14th of Aug: share the full file
  • Feedback and edits if any 

Super excited for this one!

 

Thank you!

rows.description:
Hello team 

 

I hope you're all having a lovely weeekk!

 

We received a new request from Ne'ma Initiative (Emirates Foundation) - Sara and Kholoud (Kholoud is the face of Ne'ma)

 

 

What is Ne'ma Initiative?

Ne’ma, the National Food Loss and Waste Initiative, is a reflection of UAE President His Highness Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, vision and call for action to address over-consumption and building a UAE-wide social norm around responsible and sustainable consumption, bringing back UAE’s rooted traditional values of social responsibility and good stewardship of national resources.

 

It is a collaboration between the Ministry of Climate Change and Environment and Emirates Foundation, that will promote and align shared action by key national stakeholders including government, private sector, NGOs and communities to address food loss and waste across the whole value chain, from production to consumption, covering farms, producers, distributors, retailers, restaurants and households with the aim to tackle current behaviours and attitudes to build new national social norms around responsible consumption that contribute to food loss and waste reduction.

 

Please read more about it here -> Ne'ma Initiative Website

Emirates Foundation Background:


Emirates Foundation, established by the Government of Abu Dhabi, aims to harness the potential of UAE communities by fostering a resilient and sustainable society that values autonomy, innovation, and cooperation. The foundation provides opportunities to build awareness, overcome adversity, and develop valuable skills for a better future. Through strategic partnerships, well-designed programs, and a commitment to positive change, Emirates Foundation nurtures talent, inspires achievements, and creates a lasting impact beyond national borders.


You can find their website here

Objective of the Project: 

We’ll be working on an annual report that will be presented to leadership, partners, and stakeholders, showcasing the impact and achievements of the Ne'mah initiatives achieved over the past two years.

Scope:

  • Two creative concepts following Ne'mahs brand guidelines
  • English Report up to 80 pages design
  • Proofreading and content optimization for the English content
  • Arabic Report up to 80 pages design
  • Flipping from English to Arabic once the English is finalized
  • Translation to Arabic (up to 20,000 words)
  • Both Arabic and English need to be in one report - we need to suggest a creative way to combine them together.
  • Up to 15 detailed infographics
  • Up to 20 illustartions
  • Print and Digital formats - both in spreads

Brief:

  • The content is nearly finalized and will be shared by the end of this week.
  • Primary focus is to enhance the data visualizations, making data/stats more engaging, easy to read, and quick to understand, effectively highlighting key KPIs and impacts.
  • We will create a concept using their color palette while elevating the brand to make it more lively. This concept will include page size suggestions (a wider version of A4) and packaging options. After receiving  approval on the concept and the optimized content, we will incorporate the approved design approach into the content.
  • The report will be in both Arabic and English. We will provide suggestions on how to better present both versions in one report, possibly using a divider between the two versions.
  • We will provide two versions, one that is digital and one that is for printing purposes. Both versions will be in spreads. 
  • Reference of the type of design direction you liked from the samples we presented during our call. 
  • The expected deadline is end of July - first week of August

Design Preferences:

  • Adhere to the colors and guidelines - we have freedom to be creative as long as people can still tell it's Ne'ma.
  • Visualizing Content is key.
  • When creating and submitting the concept - they want to see packaging options
  • This is a Reference of a sample that they liked 

Next Steps:

  • Get confirmation tomorrow from the client 
  • Who will be handling content and who will be taking ownership for the design

Timeline:

Deadline - End of July/First week of August

Assets and Links:

https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1GCS7W-SSwcY8XQiwHkRnDqSYWZl-EaWL

Here's a brief:

  • Template Design: We’ll create a simple and professional template with up to 5 slides.
  • Content Discussion: Tomorrow morning at 9:30 AM, we’ll brainstorm and discuss the content you’re preparing with our strategy team.
  • Content Optimization: On Wednesday, we’ll dedicate time to refining the draft content you’ll be sharing with us by the end of today.
  • Color Scheme: We’ll use black and white as the primary colors, with our creative team selecting the secondary colors.
  • Slide Design: Thursday will be focused on designing the slides, and we’ll share the final version with you by the end of the day.
  • We will be adding simple animation to the design once done.
  • Audience: Rami's team and leadership. Then it will be presented for a second time in Johannesburg in an event.
  • Look and feel: Clean and Professional.
  • Duration of the presentation will be 10-15 minutes.
  • Add icebreakers within the presentation.
  • First slides, General introduction about previous roles and expertise, to include logos of the previous companies worked with. Lilly, Medtronic, Bausch + Lomb, Merz aesthetics and current company (Galderma)
  • Incorporate the story of the doctor that’s in the draft, to present it as a story.
  • Inverted hierarchy charts are essential to be added.
  • Sentences to add such: The more investment in the sales team   = better results
  • Themes of the content: optimize teamwork, being more efficient and more collaborative. Intercross the regional and global efforts within the company. Aims to do that through workshops, seminars, and to lead by example. 
  • Light design theme preference.
  • Add a family photo in the design.

rows.taskName: MiSK-T12 - Financial Sustainability and Revenue Generation Strategy Slides
rows.description:
Financial Sustainability and Revenue Generation Strategy for Misk Foundation

Introduction: Rationale and Logic for Financial Sustainability

In response to the directive to reduce our operating expenses (OPEX) by 30% by 2026, Misk Foundation is strategically rethinking its financial approach to ensure sustained impact and mission fulfillment. While cost reduction is crucial, it also presents an opportunity to strengthen our financial position by diversifying income streams and leveraging catalytic partnerships.

Diversifying Income Streams is essential for building a resilient financial foundation that reduces reliance on traditional funding sources. By exploring innovative revenue-generating avenues—such as program-related income, strategic sponsorships, and leasing activities—Misk can offset budget constraints while continuing to deliver high-quality programs.

Catalytic Partnerships will be a key component of this strategy. By forming strategic alliances with educational institutions, corporations, and consulting firms, Misk can optimize resources, share costs, and enhance the impact of its initiatives. These partnerships will contribute to both cost efficiency and new funding opportunities, ensuring the foundation’s long-term sustainability.

Approaching this challenge from these multiple lenses will not only help Misk meet its OPEX reduction target but also position the foundation for sustainable growth and increased impact in the years to come.

1. Diversified Revenue Streams: Classified by the Nature of Activity

A. Sponsorships

  • Marketing Sponsorships:
    • Opportunities: Marketing-related sponsorships for major events such as the Misk Global Forum (MGF), Misk Art Week, and the potential "Misk Season" for Misk City’s launch in 2026.
    • Naming Rights: Selling naming rights to major corporations for key assets in Misk City, such as the City Hub and Station M (e.g., "Station M powered by IBM").
  • Philanthropic Sponsorships:
    • Opportunities: Collaboration with philanthropic entities to fund our talent development, leadership, community and art programs.
    • Support for Youth Art: Securing grants or donations to promote young emerging Saudi Artists.

B. Program-Related Revenue

  • Online Training Programs:
    • Offering: Specialized corporate training programs through the Misk Hub LMS, focusing on leadership, Media, Sports, and other skills related to Misk focus sectors
  • Memberships:
    • Startup Community: Paid memberships for startups that did not enroll in Misk's incubators or accelerators.
    • Talent Development Community: Corporate memberships providing access to Misk’s talent pool, including participation in career fairs and recruitment events.
  • Social Entrepreneurship:
    • Equity Stakes: Taking equity in startups emerging from Misk’s Social Entrepreneurship Accelerator.

C. Leasing and Investment Activities

  • Leasing Spaces:
    • Revenue Opportunities: Leasing coworking spaces at Misk City to tech startups and creative entrepreneurs.
    • Art Venue Leasing: Leasing the Prince Faisal bin Fahd Art Center for external exhibitions, attracting local and international artists.
  • Investment Activities:
    • Innovation Investments: Investing in projects within the Innovation Hub or Innovation District, focusing on cutting-edge technologies or entrepreneurial ventures.

2. Catalytic Partnerships Model: Impact on Cost Reduction

Strategic Partnerships and Cost Efficiency

The catalytic partnerships model will optimize resources and reduce program implementation costs, helping Misk enhance its programs while maintaining financial health.

Key Points:

  1. Educational Partnerships:
  • Cost Savings: Reduced training costs through partnerships with universities and tech companies.
  • Technical Contributions: Access to software and platforms at reduced rates or free.

Consulting Partnerships:

  • Leveraged Expertise: Utilizing consulting firms for project execution, reducing the need for permanent staff.
  • Operational Savings: Strategic use of temporary teams for specific projects.

Financial Impact:

  • Cost-Benefit Analysis: Potential to reduce program costs by 30-50%.
  • Revenue Growth: Savings reinvested into new or improved programs.

3. Financial Sustainability of the Innovation Hub

The Innovation Hub’s business model is designed for sustainability by leveraging a combination of revenue streams and strategic investments.

Key Areas Generating Income:

  1. Membership Fees:
  • Tiered Memberships: Offering various access levels from hot-desking to full office spaces, ensuring steady income.
  • Corporate Memberships: Premium fees from larger companies for exclusive services and presence within the hub.

Leasing Spaces:

  • Office and Lab Rentals: Revenue from leasing office spaces, labs, and meeting rooms equipped with high-tech resources.
  • Event Space Rentals: Additional income from leasing facilities for conferences, workshops, and corporate events.

Service-Based Revenue:

  • Consulting and Advisory Services: Providing consulting services, mentorship, and training for additional revenue.
  • Event Hosting: Revenue from hosting industry events, hackathons, and innovation challenges, especially for non-members.

Public-Private Partnerships and Sponsorships:

  • Government Funding: Grants, subsidies, and incentives aligned with economic development goals.
  • Corporate Sponsorships: Financial support from corporations for specific labs or programs.

Equity Stakes:

  • Startup Investments: Taking equity stakes in incubated startups, generating long-term revenue.
  • Co-Development Projects: Partnering with members to co-develop technologies or products, sharing risks and rewards.

How the Model Ensures Sustainability:

  • Diversified Income: Multiple revenue streams reduce dependency on any single source.
  • Reinvestment Strategy: Revenue is reinvested into the hub’s infrastructure and programs, enhancing value for members and attracting more participants.
  • Strategic Partnerships: Collaborations with government, corporations, and research institutions provide additional funding and strengthen the hub’s ecosystem.

Slides Design Instructions

Slide 1: Revenue Diversification Approaches

Title: Revenue Diversification Approaches

Visual Layout:

  • Three Sections: Use three distinct sections or columns for Sponsorships, Program-Related Revenue, and Leasing & Investment Activities.
  • Icons/Graphics: Use relevant icons (e.g., dollar sign for Sponsorships, laptop for Online Programs, buildings for Leasing).

Content Highlights:

  1. Sponsorships: Marketing & Philanthropic Sponsorships, Naming Rights.
  2. Program-Related Revenue: Online Training, Memberships, Social Entrepreneurship Equity.
  3. Leasing & Investment Activities: Office/Lab Rentals, Art Venue Leasing, Innovation Investments.

Slide 2: Catalytic Partnerships – Financial Impact

Title: Financial Impact of Catalytic Partnerships

Visual Layout:

  • Two Columns: Left column for Cost Reduction, right column for Revenue Growth.
  • Icons/Graphics: Use icons to represent cost savings (e.g., downward arrow) and revenue growth (e.g., upward arrow).

Content Highlights:

  1. Cost Reduction: Educational Partnerships, Consulting Partnerships, Technical Contributions.
  2. Revenue Growth: Reinvestment Opportunities, Cost-Benefit Analysis.

Slide 3: Financial Sustainability of the Innovation Hub

Comprehensive Project Brief: Dubai Shopping Festival Presentation


Project Overview: We have an exciting new project opportunity with DET’s PR team, centered around the upcoming Dubai Shopping Festival (DSF). This will involve creating a presentation to be delivered at a press conference aimed at briefing the media about the festival. Key highlights of the project include:

  • Theme: The presentation will revolve around spheres, symbolizing DSF as an out-of-this-world celebration of Dubai, inspired by the "Dubai Kawkab Akhar" song.
  • Significance: This year marks the 30th edition of DSF, a milestone that should be creatively and prominently featured.

Scope:

  • Slides: We will design approximately 50-70 slides , including videos (provided by DET) used as breakers during the event. (This will be increased based on content, skeleton, and structure of the doc)
  • Repetitive slides will be redesigned to ensure they remain engaging and creative throughout the presentation.
  • Key Dates:
    • Press Conference: November 12th.
    • Practice Event: November 5th, 8th, and 11th(all night).

Key Focus Areas:

  1. History Slide: This will need to be visually impactful, celebrating DSF’s rich history and growth over its 30-year span.
  2. Founded by His Highness Slide: A special tribute slide, which will be designed with utmost care and attention to detail.
  3. Bilingual Content: we will add english and arabic content on the two side screens next to the main screen


    Collaboration with PR Agency: DET’s PR team has connected us with their PR agency, and they’ve provided the following essential materials from TODA that we will need to integrate into our design and planning process:
  • Tech Specs for Projectors Layout
    • Please see here all the files required.
  • TODA General Layout & Equipment
    • Please find here for general layout and specifications.
    • To access projection and sound system specifications, please refer to TODA Projection & Sound System Specifications.pdf.
    • Note that the .skp and .dwg, are for 3D render mock-ups. 
  • Interactive Room
    • Please find here all the specs and dimensions
  • Branding Placements
    • Please find here where you can place all brandings along with their dimensions. 
    • They also have a designated supplier who takes care of printing for branding placements, including the installation and removal. This arrangement aims to make the process more convenient for us.
  • Visual Library
    • Attached is their sample visuals that we can use for the event. We are allowed to use up to 10 visuals.

Resources & Files: All project-related files, including last year's presentation, content structure, and the press conference running order, can be accessed on our shared drive. These documents will be regularly updated as we receive more materials.

Bynder Media Library Access: To access DET’s media assets:

  1. Visit: https://dubaitourism.getbynder.com/login/
  2. Register a new account.
  3. Select "Media Library User" and fill in your details.
  4. An email will be sent to set your password.
  5. Log in to access Dubai’s best media assets for the DSF presentation.


Additional Images:

  1. Link with last year’s DSF collection
  2. Link with DSF, food, city…
  3. Link with gastronomy shots
  4. Link with approved press images + renders

Deliverables and mini brief:

This is considered a keynote PPT Presentation. 

Objective: The focus is on Dubai's economic agenda for Vision 2030, specifically the pillar concerning talent. The goal is to better understand the future job market and the aspirations of high school students regarding their studies and careers.

 

An event will be organized to brief the education sector, featuring various keynote speakers and the keynote PPT that we will be working on.

  • We will be designing a digital report in PowerPoint. It will be presented to students and parents, so it needs to be professional yet fun—without being quirky.
  • The report's storyline will feature a key message at the top as a headline that needs to stand out. While you have brand guidelines, we don’t have to adhere strictly to them; you’re open to making it more illustrative.
  • for reference; In our previous project, the report adhered to DET brand guidelines while still being creative and engaging.
  • No animations needed.
  • The report should have the look and feel od the following: eco-friendly, lively, and dimensional.
  • We will opt for a dark theme.
  • Using icons is important, but they should not be quirky.
  • We will use neutral colors, specifically blues, beiges, and greys, while avoiding teal and purple.
  • You prefer illustrations over pictures.
  • The dimensions will be in PowerPoint landscape format.
  • We are also free to revamp the existing structure.

Timelines:

  • We will share the concept by Thursday at 6 PM and look forward to any feedback you may have.
  • While waiting for your feedback on the concept, we will work on the first 15 slides to be shared by October 15 at 6 PM. This timeline assumes that the feedback on the concept will be minor and can be quickly integrated into the already designed slides.

Overview:

  • Volunteering Database:
    • Contains 600,000 volunteers, registered entities, government organisations, NGOs, and around 100 volunteering teams.
  • Request:
    • Develop a comprehensive guideline for volunteering in both english and arabic.

Guideline Details:

  1. Stakeholders:
    • There will be three main stakeholders involved in this guideline.
  2. Content:
    • Introduction and definitions for terms such as volunteer, organization, opportunity, volunteering hours, etc.
    • Guidelines for Abu Dhabi Volunteering Licensing to be integrated with the Volunteers guidelines.
    • Regulations and terms and conditions of the Ministry of Community Development (MoCD) to be extracted and matched with guidelines for other emirates.
    • Separate sections for all emirates and Abu Dhabi.
    • Info to be added: Maximum limit of 8 hours for volunteering.
    • Info to be added: Guidelines for using the platform itself.
  3. Language:
    • The guideline will be created in English first and then translated into Arabic.
  4. Visuals and Branding:
    • Follow brand guidelines that they will share.
    • Include images related to volunteering.
  5. Deliverables:
    • Content - for the volunteering guide in English
    • Content - Translate the final English guide
    • Design the English version. - In-design
    • Design Arabic version once the English version is approved - In-design
    • Development of an FAQ to complement the guidelines.

      Additional to be in the guide:
    • Step-by-step guide on using the platform.
    • Guidelines on specific criteria for vests, catering, transportation, etc.
    • Separate guidelines for Takatuf (general volunteering) and Saned (specific volunteering)
  6. Printing:
    • There is a possibility that the guidelines might be printed 
  7. Deadline:
    • Proposal to include a preferred deadline of mid or end of August.
    • Aim to publish as soon as possible.
  8. Regulations:
    • MoCD regulations will be shared for inclusion.
    • Guidelines for international volunteering to be added.

Ownership of the guideline:

  • The project will be a shared effort with the Ministry of Community Development.

Plan for the content:

  • Review and integration of the Abu Dhabi guidelines.
  • Extraction and matching of regulations for other emirates.
  • Content creation in English, followed by translation into Arabic.
  • Conduct workshops for data collection

Design Brief: Dabbagh Posters , if you prefer in Arabic [Here] اذا تفضل باللغة العربية على للنك المرفق

Project Overview:

We are tasked with designing 13 posters, adhering to the agreed-upon Direction #2 style. These posters will be divided into two categories:

  • 8 posters featuring Dabbagh’s highly notable figures. ----> [Content & Pictures]
  • 5 posters showcasing Dabbagh heroes. [Content]

The goal is to create visually engaging and informative posters that align with the honor wall's overall aesthetic.

Wall Dimensions and Placement:

  • First Wall: Height: 2.18m, Width: 3.25m
  • Second Wall: Height: 2.17m, Width: 2.46m
    • Ensure the posters are designed to fit these dimensions harmoniously, leaving appropriate spacing for a clean, organized layout.

Key Design Requirements:

  1. Illustrations for Pictures:
    • The images of the notable figures are unclear, so they need to be illustrated. Ensure the illustrations are clean, professional, and consistent with the chosen design direction.
  2. Infographic-Style Layout:
    • Break down the information into clear, digestible sections resembling an infographic.
    • Ensure the design remains a poster format but emphasizes clarity and organization.
    • Use visual hierarchy to guide the viewer's eye effectively.
  3. Content:
    • Each poster will feature:
      • A prominent illustration of the individual.
      • Brief information about the person, displayed in an engaging manner.
  4. Style and Tone:
    • Follow the style outlined in Direction #2, focusing on a balance of professionalism and creativity.
    • Use consistent typography, color schemes, and layouts across all posters.
  5. Integration with Walls:
    • Provide a placeholder where we can link each poster to its respective position on the wall for easy visualization and approval.

Timeline:

The posters must be completed by November 28. Ensure to allocate time for feedback and adjustments within this timeline.

Next Steps:

  • Start by creating one sample poster for review to align on the direction.
  • After approval, proceed with the remaining posters.

rows.description:
Hello Team !

 

I hope you're all doing well! We have a new project for Emirates Group \ dnata which will kick off on Sunday

 

Designers working on this for the concept phase:

  • Rand Ababneh
  • Hala Alfayoumi
  • Nightshift Sara Shunnar and Razan Fakhoury to agree on who will be working on it

Hala Alfayoumi will continue with the execution after the approval, and a detailed plan will be shared. Our aim is to deliver the file internally by the 3rd of December, depending on the round of feedback she provides.

 

Background:

dnata, a subsidiary of the Emirates Group, manages a wide range of ground operations, including cargo handling, inflight catering, retail, and travel services.

 

We will be supporting them with the "dnata Corporate Induction Deck," which is designed for new employees joining dnata worldwide. This presentation will serve as the first tier of the employee onboarding journey.

 

The Ask:

The presentation is typically delivered face-to-face, so readability is a key consideration. While the client does not want the design to be overly flashy, they do want it to be professionally designed. Additionally, the file size needs to be as small as possible, ideally under 200 MB, to ensure accessibility for everyone across the globe.

 

The client is seeking a creative, simple, bold, and clear presentation that will leave a strong impression on the audience while presenting all the necessary information in a clear and concise manner.

 

Objectives:

  • 3 concept creations: Directions to be discussed with Razan Fakhoury. Each concept should be up to 6 slides.
  • Proposal: Showcasing the three concepts, with ideas/mood boards if needed.
  • Amplify the presentation: Up to 45 slides.

Scope & Brief:

Here you can find the presentation along with the logo and brand guidelines:

  • Format Size: Typical PPT horizontal format 16:9
  • Color Scheme: The presentation should strictly follow dnata's brand guidelines. 
  • Consistency: Ensure the design carries a consistent theme and overall direction/idea. 
  • Fonts: We will use their fonts as well
  • Transitions and Animations: Add very minimal transitions and animations to ensure the flow of the storytelling.

Design Directions:

Razan Fakhoury will brief each person of the design direction needed for each concept

 

Assets and Links:

  • Odoo link TBC
  • Drive link TBC
  • Presentation and guidelines attached however this is not the final one yet
    Induction Draft 5.pptx
    dnata Brand Guidelines 1.pdf

Timeline & Milestones:

  • Sun 17 - Wed 20 / Thurs 21 Nov: Rand, Razan & night shift to work on 3 concepts
  • Thurs 21st or Mon 25: Schedule a call with the client and share the concepts and proposal
  • Sun 24 / Mon 25th till Wed 27th: Feedback and edits if any
  • Wed 27th till Tue 3rd: Full file with WIP in between
  • Feedback and edits if any

Thank you team!

🔗Odoo link here

🔗Drive link here

 

About the client

 

  • Al Dhaheri Capital Investment Group - website
  • Main Contact is Sultan Aldhaheri | General Manager

 

 

About the project

 

Project Overview: Enhanced/ Amplified Presentation Design and Content Restructuring

Scope of Work:

  1. Enhanced & Amplified Design (35-40 slides)
    • Up to 20 slides will be fully amplified. This includes covers, section dividers, infographics, and other pivotal slides.
    • 15- 20 Slides will be enhanced with detailed design elements, icons, and imagery to maximize impact and visual engagement.
  2. Content work:
    • Storytelling (Horizontal logic): We need to shuffle things around to have a cohesive story that flows better, without going into a complete overhaul
    • Content on slide level (Vertical logic): We're not changing or adding to the content, but we can enrich it further. For example:
      • Base content: Diet: Fish, squid, and crustaceans
      • Enriched content: Diet: Predominantly fish, squid, and crustaceans, supporting their energy-intensive lifestyle.
      • We don't need to do this for every slide, assess the content and see if and where this is appropriate
    • Content/ Design collab: Content strategist should collaborate with designer on how to organize the content in such a way that it can be turned into an infographic. For example, don't give a designer a paragraph of text and expect them to turn it into an infographic

Deadline: 3 weeks from start (client may be flexible for up to 4 weeks but you can negotiate with him IF we need more time)

Audience: Presentation intended for a government audience

 

 

References they liked: Nathealth and DET Pitch pack, we can also have the products slide being infographic into a map with the content surrounding it

 

Important points to consider

  • ⁠We agreed with the client to help us cut out a lot of content, so someone needs to make sure he's complying and challenging him with what information is necessary vs. what can go to an appendix. 
  • Appendix slides: We promised him to apply the master style to the appendix slides but not to add any enhancement or amplification (this should be a super quick fix anyway, just applying the master slide and changing the fonts if needed). 
  • Amplified slides: We were very clear covers, separators, and thank you slides would count as amplified and we wouldn't go over 20 slides total amplified. 
  • Content & Design - hand in hand: We are planning to experiment with this project if design and content can go simultaneously together. Please let me know your thoughts on how we can do this. 
  • Content Enrichment: We promised to offer minor level of "content enrichment" but not adding any info to the content on the slide level, for example:

Resources

  • Designer: Leen O.
  • Content & Strategy: Bana to guide and supervise - Nora F. for technical execution
  • PM: Sara

 

Plan and next steps

  • Kick off call with the client next week
    • Details to get on the call: Branding guidelines
  • Detailed plan to be shared after the call

Task Brief: Finalizing the Master Deck for Taif Development Project

Objective:

We are to prepare the master presentation deck for the Taif development project, incorporating storytelling, data-driven insights, and cohesive visual design to appeal to diverse audiences, including investors and stakeholders.

Key Focus Areas:

  1. Structure and Content:
    • Develop a two-part deck:
      • Story-driven section: Highlight Taif's rich history, culture, and significance.
      • Data-driven section: Detail the development plan, sustainability efforts, and investment opportunities.
    • Ensure flexibility in the deck's design, allowing customization for various departments (e.g., development, strategy).
    • Include key slides tailored for specific stakeholders.
  2. Visual Storytelling:
    • Use immersive storytelling to captivate the audience, starting with Taif’s cultural and historical highlights.
    • Integrate high-quality visuals, including drone footage, scenic imagery, and brand-aligned design elements.
    • Emphasize Taif’s unique climate, landscape, and UNESCO recognition as a city of literature.
  3. Development and Sustainability Plan:
    • Showcase the master plan, including:
      • 2,500 homes, 2,300 hotel rooms, 40,000 sq.m retail space, and 45,000 sq.m leisure space.
      • A scenic route connecting three villages with hiking, biking, and camping opportunities.
    • Highlight sustainability initiatives focusing on biodiversity, cultural heritage, and community empowerment.
  4. Economic and Investment Highlights:
    • Detail the project’s impact:
      • 11,000 jobs created.
      • $31 billion in economic stimulus.
    • Highlight exclusive investment opportunities, including government and PIF-backed financing options.
  5. Branding and Presentation Standards:
    • Align the deck with interim branding (JSDC), with a transition plan for "Watch Development."
    • Ensure cohesive design and storytelling across English and Arabic versions of the presentation.

Timeline:

  • Design Development: Parallel work by the design team to refine visuals and layout.
  • First Draft Completion: Finalize the English version by 12/02; Arabic version to follow.

Deliverables:

  • Complete English and Arabic versions of the master deck.
  • High-quality visuals and animations, ensuring optimal storytelling.
  • Finalized structure and content, tailored to stakeholders’ feedback.

Next Steps:

  1. Circulate the existing presentation for feedback.
  2. Incorporate suggested edits and finalize the visual concept.
  3. Align on timelines for design and translation tasks.
  4. Schedule a review meeting to ensure alignment before submission.

rows.description:
Hello Team ! 

I hope you're all doing well! We have a new project for Richemont which we've already kicked off

Designer working on it:

  • Reem

Our aim is to deliver the file internally by January 9th.

Background:

We previously collaborated with Richemont on a Finance townhall, delivering a dark-themed amplified presentation that was highly appreciated. For this new townhall, Richemont requests the same look and feel, aligning with the previous concept and maintaining a consistent, polished design.

Objectives:

  • Amplify the presentation: Up to 32 slides.

Scope & Brief:

Here you can find the presentation along with the logo and brand guidelines:

  • Format Size: Typical PPT horizontal format 16:9
  • Color Scheme: The presentation should follow the Finance townhall presentation look and feel
  • Fonts: same as the fonts used in this presentation and the previous one that we worked on
  • Transitions and Animations: Add transitions and animations to ensure the flow of the storytelling.

Design Directions:

Overall Guidelines:

  • Keep the design consistent with the previous Finance townhall presentation (dark theme, amplified style).
  • Ensure the presentation is polished, straightforward, and externally supplier-friendly.
  • Avoid using the 3D earth backgrounds across slides, except potentially in the sustainability section.
  • Repurpose the same template and design elements where possible to ensure efficiency and consistency.

Section 1: Introduction

  • Slide 1: Use the previous townhall video but update the title.
  • Slide 2: Replicate the agenda slide from the Finance townhall.
  • Dividers: Use the same style as in the previous project.
  • Slide 4 (Welcome & Introduction): Placeholder for Edward’s introduction slide. Use a slide from the previous presentation
  • Slide 5: Adapt a slide from the previous presentation.

Section 2: Sustainability, Ethics and Compliance

  • Slide 7: Connect the “Craft the Future” motto to sustainability by using Richemont product visuals or sustainability-related imagery.
  • Slide 8: Enhance the visualization for clarity and impact.
  • Slide 9: Produce the slide and design it better, use the percentages highlighted in yellow.
  • Slide 10: Simplify text if necessary (we can ask them if the slide was too crowded). Address these key points:
    1. Introduction to the code of conduct.
    2. Importance of signing it.
    3. Applications.
    4. Conclude with the provided paragraph.
  • Slides 11-12: Add visuals like photos, logos, and conversational imagery to keep these slides simple and digestible.
  • Slides 13-16: Present sensitive topics (gifts and entertainment) in a straightforward, uncomplicated way.
  • Slides 17-19: Visualize the “Speak Up” platform with images of the interface and a step-by-step guide.

Section 3: Richemont Process Efficiency

  • Slide 21: Redesign the process visualization for clarity and simplicity, ensuring it looks like a cohesive flow.
  • Slides 22-24: Illustrate the process from start to finish.
  • Slide 25: Quickly outline required documents in a concise manner.
  • Slide 26: Deliver a creatively inspired design to reflect the importance of this topic.
  • Slide 27: Explain the annual vendor review exercise and its relevance.
  • Slide 28: Present payment terms clearly.
  • Slide 29: Refine the basic circle diagram for improved readability and design.
  • Slide 30: Summarize with final key messages for closing remarks.

Q&A Section:

  • Use the same design as in the Finance townhall. Suppliers will receive questions in advance for preparation.

General Comments:

  • The team loved the previous Finance townhall’s dark-themed amplified presentation; replicate this style.
  • Ensure the presentation is polished, engaging, and easy to understand for suppliers.
  • Minimize maison-specific designs and keep the format light for external clarity.
  • Dark themed

Assets and Links:

  • Drive link ⁠here
  • Guidelines ⁠here
  • Presentation attached however this is not the final one yet
     

Timeline & Milestones:

  • Wed 11 - Tue 17 Dec: Reem to work on the presentation
  • Wed 18 Dec: Share a WIP
  • Wed 18-Thurs 19 Dec: Feedback and edits if any
  • Thurs 19 Dec - Tue 24 Dec: Full file in between
  • Tue 24 Dec - Thurs 9 Jan: Feedback and edits if any

Thank you team!

rows.description:
Client: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence (MBZUAI)


Objective: To publish a new report by the University, which will be shared on social media and openly published online. The report is primarily targeted at UAE policy and business leaders, while also catering to general international readers with an interest in artificial intelligence (AI).

 

Deliverables:

  • Concept Creation
  • Amplification, with a particular focus on infographics and icons.
  • Approximately 35-45 pages in length, fully digital and suitable for physical printing + digital.

Scope of Work:

  1. Overall Report Design and Layout
    • Develop a visually impactful and cohesive design for the report.
  2. Creation and Integration of Infographics
    • Design custom infographics to effectively present key data and insights.
  3. Section Dividers and Visual Elements
    • Include creative and branded section dividers to improve navigability.
  4. Final Production of the Report
    • Deliver a polished and print-ready version using Adobe InDesign.

References:

  • Previous report: [MBZUAI_LLM_Report.pdf]
  • Preferred visual style: Amplified

Design Requirements:

  • Align with MBZUAI’s existing templates and brand guidelines.
  • Highly visual, leveraging creative layouts and innovative design elements.
  • Designed in Adobe InDesign for compatibility with both digital distribution and physical printing.

Timeline:

  • Content Delivery: Early next week
  • Draft Design Submission: Mid-February
  • Final Submission: Extended to the third week of February

Material:

  • Report structure and expected delivery timeframe:
  • Cover: Report name [Pending, end of next week]
  • President's Foreword [Pending, end of next week]
  • Executive Summary [Pending, mid-next week]
  • Introduction: The UAE’s Journey to AI Leadership [shared]
  • Chapter 01: [Pending, to be shared by this Wednesday]
  • Chapter 02: [Pending, to be shared by this Wednesday]
  • Chapter 03: [Pending, to be shared by this Friday]
  • Conclusion: [Pending, to be shared by mid-next week]

 

Target Audience:

  • Primary: UAE policy and business leaders
  • Secondary: International readers with an interest in AI

Notes:

  • The report content will be provided by the MBZUAI team.
  • The design must maintain a balance between sophistication and creativity while adhering to the University's brand identity.
  •  

Additional Resources:

  • Brand identity assets: MBZUAI Brand Identity
  • SharePoint here

rows.taskName: Ne'ma Food Loss & Waste Baseline Study Videos
rows.description:
Hi team,

We need your creative input to produce two videos (English and Arabic) for Emirates Foundation (Ne'ma) focusing on food waste and loss in the UAE's seven emirates. We will use a similar visual design for both language versions.

1. English Video- Due February 20th

2. Arabic Video - Due February 25th

Both versions will be developed in parallel.

Objectives

The videos aim to:

- Educate the audience about the UAE Baseline Study on food loss and waste.

- Showcase the study's process and encourage participation.

Audience Targets

Engage three key sectors of the food ecosystem:

1. Public Sector: Food importation, food security, customs.

2. Private Sector: Restaurants, supermarkets, hotels.

3. Households: This group requires special attention.

The videos will be showcased at community sessions and shared on social media platforms, featuring QR codes for easy access in community centers.

Video Structure

- Duration: Target 90 seconds, maximum 2 minutes.

- Content Focus:

  - Persuasive messaging to encourage audience participation.

  - Clear explanation of how individuals can join and a direct call to action for sign-ups.

Visual Design and Infographics

- The client prefers suggestions rather than adhering to a specific style. 

- Reference the style developed by Ashraf in the script brief and previous reports for inspiration (here).

-The client shared with us a video for inspiration as well (here).

- Review Ne'ma's website for design elements and templates suitable for our infographics (https://www.nema.ae/en/)

We need to create two scripts in Arabic and English - Content Requirements

-Develop scripts in both languages using the attached script brief (here)

  - Ensure the language is simple, suitable for an 8th-grade audience.

  - Explain the Baseline Study: its significance, reasons for participation, and how to get involved.

- Language Considerations:

  - English and Arabic scripts will have slight variations.

  - Align with Ne'ma’s language style; internal content teams will assist with final edits.

Voice Overs

- Both videos will include voice overs in each language.

- The tone should be emotional and engaging.

- We will share sample voice over artists (both male and female) for client approval this week.

Assets and Links:

Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1q6SCvJSHANWb1GnwSlU_6JEiK3b6xXuo (You can find here all the materials shared by the client).

Milestones and Timeline:

  • Tuesday Jan 21st: Nora to work on the English Script
  • Wednesday Jan 22nd - Monday Jan 27th: Hana and Abdullah to work on the sketch. 
  • Monday Jan 27th: Call with the client at 2 pm UAE time. 
  • Mon Jan 27th - Friday Jan 31st: Feedback and iterations
  • Sunday Feb 2nd - Feb 9th: Hana to finalize the storyboard
  • Monday Feb 3rd: Nora to work on Arabic translation in parallel
  • Monday Feb 10th - Feb 16th: Abdullah to work on the animation 
  • Monday Feb17th - Feb 20th: work on flipping the video to Arabic version 
  • In parallel to the above milestones, we will be looking for VOs with the client to have them ready before Abdullah starts with the animation by Feb 10th. 

Please let me know if you have any questions or need any further clarification!

Hi team

Please reproduce the slide here and provide 2 options.

Colors and guidelines: Dark blue, black and pastel colors are fine.

In case you are going use images please use construction images in light blue.

Thanks!

rows.description:
Integrated Report

  • General Notes:
    • The Integrated Report will consist of approximately 130 pages and will be available only in a digital version.
    • A printable A4 version will also be prepared for internal use from your end.


  • Content Breakdown:
    1. M&A Report: 35 pages.
    2. Listed Companies: 8 spreads, each featuring one spread per company:
      • Half of the spread for an image, the other half for company info.
      • Logos for listed companies will be placeholders if not finalized.
      • Include most updated logos (e.g., Seer Marine).
      • Mudon will replace Q Holding.
    4. Corporate Governance Report: 33 pages, designed in InDesign.
    5. ESG Report: Details to follow; will align with CG design.
    6. Audited Financial Statements: To be provided by the audit company; will be converted to PDF. Nothing to be done from our end so far.


  • Workflow:
    • Provide a rough PowerPoint template for M&A and listed companies (approximately 50 pages).
    • Team to input data into the template and share them back as PDF pages to be included in the integrated report.
    • Approach: Design section by section, lock templates, then proceed with changes.

 

Annual Report


  • Structure:
    • Approximately 220 pages.
    • Digital version to include the full report; the printable version will be reduced to 200 pages.
    • Includes content overlapping with Integrated Report (e.g., ESG Report, CG Report).
    • Financial Report: Only the first ~16 pages will be printed, with a QR code for full access.


  • Content Highlights:
    • First section: the company and the group.
    • Minimize elements in inner slides; include highlights page at the beginning (similar to last year).
    • Cover page design: Simplify, using dark blue and silver.


  • Design Preferences:
    • Incorporate circles and cutout images.
    • Stick to last year’s color scheme.
    • Font: Alexandria (supports Arabic and English).
    • Textures to be used sparingly.
    • Themes to mix light and dark depending on section content.

 

ESG and Corporate Governance Report


  • Structure:
    • Combined into a single book under 150 pages.
    • To be placed in a box with the Annual Report, maintaining the same size (25x35 cm).
    • Title: “Annual Report 2024” on the box.


  • Separators:
    • Transparent plastic separators with section names and titles in white.
    • QR codes to link to full digital sections.
    • Alternative arrangement for QR codes due to transparency concerns.
    • Font: Alexandria.


  • Content Considerations:
    • Abstract images preferred for separators; real pictures to be used selectively.
    • Extra plastic tabs will be added for easier navigation.

 

Concept Design


  • Visual Elements:
    • Circle-based concept favored; colors may vary.
    • Cutout images (e.g., “lady with the camel” style) to be included.
    • Silver and dark blue to remain core colors.


  • Design Process:
    • Prioritize concept finalization this week.
    • Start PowerPoint template for listed companies after concept approval.
    • Simplify cover page design (90% ready).


  • Font and Layout:
    • Font: Alexandria (supports dual-language use).
    • Multiple sections: Mix light-themed (numbers and text) and darker themes.

 

Action Items


  1. Integrated Report:
    • Prepare rough PowerPoint template for M&A and listed companies starting next week.

  3. Annual Report:
    • Simplify cover page design.
    • Finalize theme and highlight page.
    • Ensure QR codes for financial content are implemented.

  5. ESG & CG Report:
    • Confirm separator design and QR code placement.
    • Coordinate updates to placeholders and logos.

  7. General:
    • Consolidate images in PowerPoint for centralized access.
    • Deliver first draft of content once concept is approved.

 

Notes

  • Regular check-ins will be conducted to ensure smooth progress.
  • Constant updates during the drafting phase should be avoided; near-final drafts preferred.
  • All pages must align with the overall design theme and approved concept.

We need to implement minor design enhancements across the entire deck, while keeping the existing images and maps unchanged. The specific requirements are as follows:

  • Design Enhancements: Apply minor design updates throughout the deck, ensuring consistency and visual appeal. Focus on refining elements like fonts, colors, layout, and other aesthetic aspects.
  • Images and Maps: No changes to the images or maps are required; these should remain as they are.
  • Slide Animations: Animate the slides as part of this update. Ensure the animations are smooth, professional, and align with the overall tone of the presentation.

rows.description:
Hello! 

Scope of Work (SOW) – Brochure Design

  • Redesign the existing brochure (up to 8 pages) under Eon Coatings’ branding instead of Greenify.
  • Reorganize and structure the content based on the provided materials.
  • Enhance visuals with professional images/infographics from our end.
  • Ensure the content is adaptable for multiple industries.
  • Content restructuring

- Timelines for the brochures:

Phase 1: Kick-Off & Planning

  • Monday, March 10 – Kick-off session 

Phase 2: Content Creation & Visual Concept Development

  • March 11 -12 – Initial concept development and first draft content creation. Submission of concept and initial content drafts
  • March 13 – Review and refine content based on feedback; further development of brochure concepts tailored to each industry

Phase 3: Brochure Development & Delivery

  • March 16 – First full draft of Brochure 1
  • March 17 – First full draft of Brochure 2
  • March 18 – First full draft of Brochure 3
  • March 19 – First full draft of Brochure 4
  • March 20 – Final refinements and edits

Materials file linked here.

• Context:
    • Annual General Assembly Meeting.
  • Objective and Audience:
    • For shareholders to meet the board members.
    • Review of the previous year's financials (profit, revenue, etc.).
    • Attendance expected: approximately 20 people.
  • Theme:
    • Simple, straightforward, formal, professional, and corporate.
    • No images/ infographics are preferred. 
    • Slides are condensed/splitting them is okay.
    • Use the male/female page from the annual report as a design reference.
  • Design Elements:
    • Mixed light and dark theme:
      • Inner slides will be light.
      • Cover, agenda, and separator slides will be dark.
    • Color palette: Dark blue, light blue, and purple as a secondary color.
    • Use of icons where appropriate.
    • Smooth on-click transition animations between slides.
    • Same fonts as the annual report (we'll share them along the entire file).
  • Concept Development:
    • Create a new concept for approval before proceeding with design.
    • New concept will incorporate elements from the IHC logo, including the logo on each slide.
    • Landscape format to fit a 16:9 dimension.
    • Final display will be a large curved screen; a 52-inch TV can be used as a reference.
  • Project Timeline:
    • Two priorities for the presentation:
      • Quotation for Priority 1 will be sent.
      • If time allows, we will address slides from Priority 2 and provide an updated quotation.
    • Aim to complete all work by March 20/21.

Objective

Develop a dynamic and visually compelling presentation for an acquisition that aligns with the standards of a billion-dollar company.

Client Overview

  • Introduction of a new platform and process for data collection and compliance, aimed at protecting users from fraud.
  • The system benefits insurance companies by reducing risk through enhanced data collection.
  • The presentation will highlight how this platform mitigates risk for insurance companies.

Target Audience

  • C-level executives.

Style & Design Requirements

  • Bold, technology and data-driven color scheme.
  • High-quality visuals that reflect the professionalism of a billion-dollar company.
  • Graphs and data should be visually appealing and built based on the provided content.
  • No images, but illustrations are acceptable.
  • Abstract graphs (not necessarily editable).
  • Clean and minimalistic look with either a white or blue background.
  • Minimal animations.

Presentation Details

  • 15 slides in total.
  • 80% of the presentation should be visual.
  • Company logo to be incorporated.

Timeline

  • 2-3 weeks Starting today 
  • We shall submit the complete file tentatively on March 20th, 2025.

rows.description:
Project Brief – Anjum Hotel

Important Links

The client shared two references from Intercontinental Hotel, which contain Brand & Advertising Guidelines. We cross-checked them with the client’s needs, and Aseel created a dashboard to track each deliverable.

  • References
  • Tracking dashboard
  • Client-shared answers, which are essential to kick off production. The link is [here].
  • Client guidelines


Project Overview

Anjum Hotel is more than just a place to stay—it is a sanctuary in the heart of Mecca, offering pilgrims a deeply meaningful and comfortable experience during their spiritual journey. Our mission is to honor each guest’s pilgrimage with warmth, respect, and thoughtful care.

Our goal is to reflect the sacredness of this journey and position Anjum as the trusted destination for spiritual hospitality in Mecca. To achieve this, we will develop a strong and consistent brand identity that resonates with visitors.

Key Questions

  • Where are we today?
  • Where do we need to be?
  • How do we get there?

Phase 1: Brand Guidelines Development

The first priority is to create comprehensive brand guidelines to establish consistency and clarity across all touchpoints.

Brand Guidelines Scope

  • Logo Usage Guidelines – Instructions on proper logo application across all mediums.
  • Color Palette & Typography – A guide to brand colors and fonts for visual coherence.
  • Visual Assets – Curated imagery and iconography aligned with brand messaging.
  • Digital & Print Templates – Customizable advertising templates for digital and print media.
  • Signage & Wayfinding Design – Clear, engaging signage to enhance brand presence.
  • Sales & Marketing Collaterals – Professionally designed brochures, flyers, and promotional materials.
  • Guest-Facing Essentials – Branded in-room materials, key cards, menus, and other guest items.
  • Social Media & Digital Content Guidelines – Best practices and templates for maintaining brand consistency online.

Brand Strategy & Communication

  • Campaign & Brand Vision – Defines the brand purpose, campaign idea, guest insights, and visual tone.
  • Photography & Film – Guidelines for capturing and localizing brand imagery.
  • Tone of Voice – Consistent language for brand messaging and marketing materials.
  • Social Media Strategy – Platform-specific best practices and content guidelines.
  • Hotel Advertising – Templates for promotions, events, and digital communications.

Disciplines

  • Brand Strategy & Narrative
  • Brand Identity (Conceptual, Verbal, Visual)
  • Brand Architecture
  • Campaigns & Advertising
  • Community Engagement
  • Social Media

Anjum Hotel Expansion & Competitive Landscape

  • Current Infrastructure: Two towers with 1,717 rooms.
  • Expansion Plans: A third tower to be built by 2028, increasing capacity to 8,000 rooms.
  • Upselling Strategy: Karma (the client) is exploring premium keys for the new tower or sub-brands.
  • Competitive Set: The Address, Hilton Convention Center, Intercontinental.

Unique Differentiators & Guest Experience Enhancements

  • Exclusive Access: Escalator directly to the Haram (avoiding a 10-minute detour around the mountain per Google Maps).
  • Parking Accessibility: Limited hotel parking with expansion plans; other hotels allowed to access through Anjum’s parking.
  • Multilingual Signage: English, Arabic, Urdu, Turkish, and Bahasa (Indonesian); internal communication in English & Arabic only.
  • Dining Excellence:
    • Hijaz Restaurant: Seats 4,000 guests for daily breakfast.
    • Dedicated Dining Floor: Separate areas for Indonesian and Malaysian guests.
    • Chobaika Restaurant: À la carte and ready-meal offerings.
    • Piazza Area: Spacious relaxation and gathering space for guests.

Brand Fundamentals

Core Vision & Mission

  • Anjum Hotel Makkah is dedicated to luxury hospitality and personalized services, supporting pilgrims in their Umrah and Hajj journeys.

Brand Personality in Three Words

  • Caring, ethical, and innovative.

Key Differentiators

  • Strong focus on Sharia values.
  • Family-driven approach to hospitality.
  • Exceeding expectations of pilgrims through exceptional service.

Guest Experience Goals

  • Comfort, spirituality, and excellence in service.
  • Seamless pilgrimage experiences with exceptional hospitality.

Core Values for Guest Interaction

  • Integrity, excellence, customer focus, innovation, and care.

Brand Essence in One Phrase

  • Memorable Pilgrimage Experiences

Brand Story

History & Inspiration

  • Founded in 2013, Anjum Hotel Makkah is an independent, family-run business operating under Islamic Sharia values.
  • Part of Abdul Latif Jameel Real Estate Investment Co. Ltd (ALJREIC), which spans real estate, construction, hospitality, pharmaceuticals, and technology.

Connection to Mecca’s Spiritual & Cultural Heritage

  • Brand ethos: “Where Hospitality Meets Spirituality”.
  • Mission: Providing luxury hospitality while ensuring a seamless Umrah and Hajj experience.

Key Achievements

  • Green Key Certification – Recognized for sustainable and environmentally responsible operations.
  • World Luxury Hotel Award – Awarded Best Luxury Family Hotel in the Middle East & North Africa.
  • Food Safety Excellence – Recognized as the best hotel in food safety in Saudi Arabia for three consecutive years.

Messaging Focus: Pilgrimage & Luxury Hospitality

  • Positioning Anjum Hotel as both a spiritual retreat and a high-end hospitality provider.

Guest Experience & Brand Application

Touchpoints for Branding Integration

  • Pre-arrival communication and digital booking experience.
  • Check-in, lobby, guest rooms, dining areas, and prayer spaces.
  • Branded collateral: Key cards, in-room amenities, signage, and digital platforms.
  • Personalized services throughout the guest journey.

Premium Experience Strategy

  • Loyalty Program Development: Creating an exclusive experience for returning guests.
  • Luxury Differentiation:
    • Special guest relations check-in desk with exclusive service.
    • Dedicated breakfast areas for loyalty members.
    • Personalized service, where staff remembers guest preferences.

Operational Service Protocol (OSP) Integration

  • Developing guidelines for elevating service through personalized interactions.

Brand Application to Collateral

  • Discussion on how to integrate branding into digital and physical assets.

Emphasis on Hospitality Rituals & Values

  • Ensuring every guest experience aligns with Anjum’s values of care, excellence, and integrity.

Next Steps & Implementation

  • Finalize brand guidelines incorporating all touchpoints.
  • Refine digital and physical branding elements to align with the evolving brand identity.

rows.description:
Hello team ⁠Everyone the long overdue brief 

Designers working on it:

  • ⁠⁠Mohammad Masad
  • ⁠Cilen Alborom
  • ⁠Hana Hawari

Objectives:

  • The goal is to help people enjoy their career paths while providing guidance on transitioning between departments at ADNOC. This aligns with the concept of gamifying the presentation, featuring 9 disciplines and 112 job roles.

Scope & Brief:

⁠Here you can find the presentation along with the concept that Azza Worked on

  • Format Size: Figma
  • Color Scheme: Use the vibrant palette from the PPT and brand guidelines while introducing complementary options where necessary.
    Brand guidelines - ⁠Here
  • Fonts: ADNOC Sans

Design Directions:

Overall Guidelines:

  • The design concept is based on gamification and arcade aesthetics. We won’t need to create a new look and feel but will focus on enhancing what's already available.
  • We'll ensure the concept is seamlessly applied throughout, with interactivity (e.g., clickable buttons revealing information or navigating between slides).
  • Animations will be added to make the design more dynamic.
  • There are 9 disciplines, each with a pixelated character for the SVP of each discipline. We'll be creating more diverse characters, representing a variety of appearances, including Emirati and non-Emirati, male and female, with different features such as glasses, hairstyles, etc.
  • The bubbles will also be animated, and arcade sounds will be added when clicking buttons.
  • Navigation will be a key focus to ensure ease of use.
  • We won’t 100% follow ADNOC's brand guidelines but will adhere to the colors and fonts in the provided Illustrator file.
  • We’ll also assist in creating inventories for the presentation.
  • ⁠Illustrations & Design: Inspired by the PPT reference, focusing on authenticity and realism to reflect ADNOC offices. The style will be nostalgic and inviting, with relatable imagery.
    ADNOC Offices Images and Videos - ⁠Here
  • Scenery: Includes ADNOC's building prominently, animated with relatable elements like employees and lighthearted office moments.

Content:

While it is likely that very minor content changes may occur, these are not expected to impact the flow of the presentation or the progress of our work. We’ll be waiting to hear from them regarding this.

 

Assets and Links:

  • Drive link ⁠here
  • Odoo link ⁠here
    • Project Scope & Breakdown - Here
  • Links shared by Dima  (materials on the drive but just in case)
    • Presentation with initial concept - ⁠Here
    • Guidelines, Images & Video: ⁠Here

Timeline & Milestones:

  • First Milestone:
    By Thursday, the 30th, we will share the following:
  1. Structure (Wireframes): Most of the project in Figma, focusing on layout and flow without visuals.
  2. Full Sample Section: Featuring visuals for 1 discipline and 1-2 positions, including:
    • Office scene
    • Main character for the discipline
    • Portraits of 1-2 positions with their skills
  • A walkthrough call was proposed on Thursday the 30th between 1:00 and 4:00 Jo time / still waiting to hear back from them to schedule it
  • Final Deliverable: March 31st, 2025 (however we're trying to push a little bit to get more time in case needed)

Thank you team!

We would like your kind support to design an Investment teaser (Mobile one pager infographics) based on the attached presentation.

 

  • Should we follow the same visual style used for Tamara’s teaser? Yes the concept looks fine. However please use your creative themes for the company (To give some insight the company operates in the basic food primarily dairy focused on the cheese segment) 
    https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1bKjz0FeVoCkHSwFOAjve3Jwwp-2WRGKn
  • Would you like the content translated into Arabic? Yes please
  • Is the content final, or should we anticipate any revisions? Yes we aren’t expecting any revisions in the meantime.

Hellooooooooooooo team, 

We got a new project from Takalam UAE. 

Our main PoC is Stella and basically they are a an AI powered platform for mental well-being. Offering mental wellness smart tools and screening features, in addition to online counseling and coaching sessions for individuals and couples.

You can find their website here for more context, which of course you can inspire from while designing: https://takalamhere.com/

Please check the library here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1PALozf0h7S7Q3fZN6Y_1-5Fl4fstuNTc

What we are designing: A 5-page report

Assets we have:

1, Fonts 2, Company Profile 3, Colors 4, logo

Content: Awaiting confirmation on final file to use.

White Paper Focus: Mental Health Professionals and Decision Makers

Target Audience:

  • Executives, Vice Presidents, and Doctors in the mental health sector.

Key Messages:

  • Emphasize professionalism and innovation.

Branding Elements:

  • Brand guidelines are available.
  • Use pink as a secondary color.
  • Utilize a light theme.
  • Aim for a McKinsey-style presentation.

Design Inspiration:

  • Use the website as a reference.
  • Opt for an infographic style; the size and positioning of circles will convey specific meanings, which we will retain.

Specifications:

  • A4 size (8.5 x 11 inches).
  • Include pages and spreads.
  • Include RGB for digital and CMYK for printing.

Cover Image:

  • Update the cover to reflect the communities we're serving. Consider incorporating elements of UAE heritage and depiction of people.

Translation Considerations:

  • Pay extra attention to titles and highlights during translation.

Milestones:

  1. Theme and WIP File: Friday, April 4, at 10:00 AM (UAE) by Hala
  2. Complete English File with Arabic Translation Draft: Monday, April 7, at 12:00 PM (UAE) by Hala, Hind
  3. Full Arabic Flipped File: Wednesday, April 9, at 12:00 PM (UAE) by Hala

Looking forward to creating something amazing together!
