rows.description:
NTT DATA Briefing

About NTT DATA:

NTT DATA, a part of the NTT Group, is a global leader in IT and business services, headquartered in Tokyo. They specialize in driving digital transformation through consulting, industry solutions, business process services, IT modernization, and managed services, empowering businesses and society to embrace the digital future.

Project Scope:

  • Objective: Enhance their company profile.
  • Design Approach: Two versions will be delivered:
    • A static version for easy sharing.
    • An animated version for a more dynamic experience.
  • Slide Count: 67 slides.
  • Deadline: Submission by April 13th/14th.

This is a high-profile project with the potential for future collaboration, so let’s make it stand out.

Key Discussion Points:

  • The design and animation will follow a corporate and subtle style, aligned with brand guidelines, including the appropriate use of visuals and imagery.
  • A static PDF version of the PowerPoint file will be created for seamless email sharing.
  • Animations will be minimal and non-distracting, in line with the corporate and conservative preferences of the audience.
  • From Section #3 onward, we will incorporate Saudi-centric visuals to reflect the KSA entity, while the initial sections will maintain a global perspective.
  • Logo usage must adhere to brand guidelines, ensuring proper placement alongside the NTT Group logo.
  • The "Key Certifications and Awards" section will have a distinct visual treatment to enhance engagement beyond standard bullet points.

rows.parentProjectName: Al Faisaliah Group Holding (CJSC), Mohammad Sargani
rows.description:
• We’ll be designing a presentation based on the content they have shared, optimized for screen display for the townhall event, which is to be conducted on Monday.
  • Animations will be included but kept minimal to maintain a clean and professional look.
  • AI-generated visuals are welcomed, as long as they’re used in moderation.
  • Strategic content support will be provided to ensure the storyline flows smoothly.
  • We’re happy also to explore alternative visual directions beyond those initially suggested to elevate the final outcome.
  • The second option for the interactive slide is preferred as discussed.

    The content is as below as shared by the client:
  • For Alfa annual meeting, attended by almost 200 employees, there is a 45 slot for me to present to the team, the topic I selected is Transformation. Here is the first draft of the material and I want the agency to prepare the slides accordingly and share it by the week end. The meeting is on Monday
  • I also want the slides to be flexible so I can add/change the text below which is the first draft.

 

  • Presentation Title: "Beyond Great: The Real Recipe for Transformation"
  • Slide 1: Title Slide
  • Title: Beyond Great: The Real Recipe for Transformation
  • Visual: something like a vibrant image of chefs plating a futuristic dish or in the middle of a very busy restaurant kitchen


  • Slide 2: What Is Transformation?
  • Title: So... What Is Transformation Anyway?
  • Key Points:
    • Not just cosmetic changes (like new uniforms or digital menus)
    • It’s a full shift in how we operate, think, serve, and grow
    • “A better version of ourselves that doesn’t expire next quarter”
  • Visual: Before & after cartoon or meme comparing a silly upgrade vs. true transformation


  • Slide 3: Why Transformation?
  • Title: Why Bother Changing? Everything is Fine... Right?
  • Key Points:
    • Customer expectations are evolving fast
    • New digital players disrupting foodservice
    • Tech is changing how we deliver value
    • Pressure on margins and talent retention
  • Visual: ??


  • Slide 4: What Triggers Transformation?
  • Title: What Pushes Us to Transform?
  • Key Points:
    • Growth stagnation or competition…not mention a decline!
    • Missing targets
    • Rising costs without the ability to transfer it to the customer.
    • Customer demand for innovation, faster, better, personalized service
    • New trends hitting local taste buds
  • Visual: something like animation of a delivery app outpacing a traditional restaurant, or many tables with the same boring dish contrast with multiple vibrant dishes on multiple tables


  • Slide 5: Elements of Successful Transformation
  • Title: What Makes a Transformation Work (and Not Just a Buzzword)?
  • Key Points:
    • Clear purpose & urgency
    • Leadership sponsorship
    • The right structure, e.g. a dedicated Transformation Office (TO)
    • Having the right caliber onboard, with engaged organization
    • Fast wins, focused ambition
    • Rigorous tracking
    • Cultural shift
    • Stage-gate discipline & agility (capitalize on successes, and when we fail we fail fast and move on)
  • Visual: Recipe card with these 8 items as ingredients in a kitchen


  • Slide 6: What’s In It for Me?
  • Title: Okay, Sounds Cool... But What’s In It for Me?
  • Key Points:
    • Learn new, in-demand skills
    • Be recognized for impact
    • More say in shaping the future
    • Career growth & mobility
    • Its genuinely satisfying, the sense of pride being part of a big and continues success
    • Financially…right?
  • Visual: may be something like split screen showing an employee before & after transformation (more confident, leading a team)


  • Slide 7: Why Do Transformations Fail?
  • Title: When Good Transformations Go Bad...
  • Key Points:
    • No clear vision or target
    • Declaring victory too early. Unsuccessful transformations don’t sustain the early wins nor capitalize on it.
    • Lacking clear owners and accountability for actions. Without this, It’s impossible to embed rapid decision making and to reinforce new mindsets to keep the transformation moving.
    • Failing to refine as they go. No single action, or group of actions, defines transformation success. However, leaders who invest in tangible changes to business-as-usual structures, processes, and systems; prioritize transformations as the main event; and maintain a long-term mindset can give their organization the best chance at achieving the full potential of its transformation.
    • Lack of employees engagement, people feel excluded. No chance of sustaining success if people are not engaged
  • Visual:  a football video of a player celebrating scoring a goal too early, then the ball misses the goal while the player is celebrating…or a runner celebrating before reaching the finish line and another player comes fast from behind and hit the finish line before him


  • Slide 8: Call to Action
  • Title: Let’s Not Just Be Great — Let’s Stay Great…Together
  • Quote: "The best thing we can serve our customers... is a transformed team."
  • Message: Be proactive. Be agile. Be bold. Be part of this journey.
  • Visual: Team celebrating or a huddle image or hand stacking photo


  • Interactive few minutes (TBD):
  • First idea is a a slide with title that I can insert…something like:
  •                         Poll "How many of you introduced or experienced a tech upgrade this year?"
  •                         Quick Quiz: Myth-busting transformation beliefs….Who's responsible for the future of this company?
  •  
  • Second is a handout slide on the screen with a title: What Transformation Means for You?
    • Why we’re transforming
    • What you’ll gain (skills, influence, growth)
    • 3 ways to get involved
    • What success looks like in your role
    • QR code to feedback form or suggestion board


  • Closing: One slide, clean layout, motivational quote at the bottom (TBD)

rows.description: Client is looking to enhance it following the same concept as before(Please find below), make it clean and clear as the content is dense and he wants it easier for the audience to understand, Also, please refer to the comments included in the file uploaded as well.

Revamp the Format and Structure

  • Move away from simply updating old templates with new content.
  • Reimagine the entire structure and flow of the presentation.

Shift to a Storytelling Approach

  • Create a cohesive narrative or storyline for the past six months.
  • Move away from slide-by-slide data dumps and project showcases.
  • Focus on storytelling that weaves key information into a compelling journey.

Make It Visually-Led

  • Take inspiration from Apple Special Events: let creatives and assets do the talking.
  • Reduce reliance on informative/text-heavy slides, and elevate the visual narrative.

Shared Presentation Delivery

  • Involve other presenters (e.g. Aida, Hoor) alongside our CEO Issam delivering the entire presentation.
  • Define a clear flow and handover between speakers to make transitions seamless.

Make It Interactive

  • Introduce live audience engagement during the presentation (not just post-networking):
    • Live polls
    • Scannable QR codes for quizzes or real-time questions
    • Interactive feedback on future campaigns or market priorities

Forward-Focused Involvement

  • Ask the audience for input on upcoming or unplanned initiatives (e.g. next campaign hook, market priorities), not just feedback on past activities.

Collaborative Narrative Development

  • Build the narrative first, then design the slides around it—not the other way around.
  • Involve senior leadership early in the development process for input and industry alignment.

Shift the Focus from DET to the Industry

  • Reframe the briefing as a representation of the entire industry, not just DET’s achievements.
  • Ensure the audience leaves feeling part of a collective, citywide effort.

rows.description:
This will be a report style presentation in Landscape format, optimized for digital sharing. We very much need to follow a report-like style of design as this will be read and digested through email.

  • Attached is the word doc brief from the client (google drive link below)
  • Pages: 16 - 18 max
  • Final Outcome: Report for digital sharing
  • Objective: Update senior management on progress related to the DPO's initiatives
  • Tone: Should be serious, related to risk/ data protection topics
  • Format: Landscape PPT
  • Style: Report-like visualization
  • Design level: High-end visualization, infographics, etc.
  • Content: We don't need to restructure content, we just need a strategist to go through it and optimize it in terms of structure within the page, trimming some sentences, breaking out key pieces of info or numbers into infographics. Also, attached is the content from last year, we are yet to receive the content for 2024 (should be sent soon) but it will be more or less similar to the attached. We will receive 10 pages so I imagine end output should be 16 - 18 pages
  • Design guide: Follow the attached (although I don't really like it. You have some room for flexibility within it as it's not very comprehensive anyway) (google drive link)

Design approach: Amplified, report style design

The final outcome is a report to update senior leadership, designed in a landscape PowerPoint format. The approach is important, and it’s vital that it isn’t treated like a presentation. The tone should be serious, reflecting the nature of the report. It's crucial to align with the client’s content. its important not to overload with cutouts or overly flashy elements. The focus should be on storytelling, and infographics or illustrations should be used stylistically and sparingly to enhance understanding, not overwhelm the content.

Design Perspective

  • Brand Guidelines:
    Internal DPO Brand Guidelines will be used. 
  • Color Palette & Template:
    We’ll follow the color palette and use a presentation template that aligns with the internal brand identity.
  • Theme Direction:
    Design tone should be light and professional, avoiding dark themes due to the seriousness of the subject matter.
  • Logo Use:
    There is no need to include the Emirates Group logo at this time, but will be informed if that is subject to change

Content Optimization Perspective

  • Creative Freedom:
    We will restructure the content and optimize it for visualization and synergy with design, while maintaining maximum integrity of the content.
  • Word trimming:
    Sensitivity to word trimming — any editing must preserve the original meaning and intent.

MILESTONES:

Houssam/Mo – Investor Decks (Long Version)

  • Client is entering fundraising stage, seeking to raise ~$10M USD
  • This is their first interaction with investors
    • Audience: Friends, family, angel investors
  • Messaging must be sharp and on-point to establish credibility
  • They require two investor decks:
    • Long version: 10–15 slides (for more detailed follow-ups)
  • Key themes to cover:
    • Macro & micro market opportunity
    • Business model
    • Traction
    • Use of funds
    • Growth plan & vision
  • Client will provide content but need strategy and stprytelling
  • PrezLab to lead:
    • Storytelling and strategic structuring
    • Design (static format—optimized for easy sharing via email/WhatsApp)
  • Timeline: 2 weeks from kickoff

Client: Misk Art Institute (MAI)

Point of Contact: Ibrahem A. AlSuhaibani

Subsidiary of: Misk Foundation

Deadline: End of next week

About the Client

Misk Art Institute (MAI) is a subsidiary of the Misk Foundation, focused on nurturing the visual arts scene in Saudi Arabia. They work to empower emerging and established artists through exhibitions, residencies, education, and partnerships. Their goal is to build a sustainable ecosystem for creativity and cultural development across the Kingdom.

Project Scope

We will be developing a company profile deck for MAI. This deck is a core communication tool used primarily for presenting to high-level sponsors, including entities like Aramco.

Client's Main Challenge

Ibrahem shared that their current content doesn’t clearly explain what they actually do. The audience often leaves the presentation confused or with only a vague idea of MAI’s mission and activities. He wants this new profile to clearly and simply communicate the value of MAI in a professional, visually engaging way.

What We’re Delivering

  • A clean, professional, and highly visual deck
  • Strong infographic-driven communication to simplify complex information
  • Content that speaks clearly to a sophisticated corporate audience
  • Structure and flow that aligns with sponsorship outreach goals

Creative Direction

  • Visual style: Modern, elegant, minimal
  • Layout: Spacious, with strong focus on clarity
  • Visual assets: The client will provide stock images
  • Design emphasis: Infographics, iconography, and bold text callouts
  • Tone of voice: Straightforward, clear, and confident

Next Steps

  • Creative Strategy Involvement:
    Ibrahem has requested a call with someone from the Creative Strategy team once the project is confirmed. This will help align the messaging before the design phase begins

    Notes: slide 2 is for agenda / slide 13 parters section, so we'll be adding their logos (will be provided from his end)

rows.description:
Deck format:

  • Total aim of 10 slides 

Project Overview

  • The project will be presented to the Abu Dhabi Government, Maktab Tanfeethi. Once approved, it will proceed to Sheikh Khaled's Excellency at ADEO.
  • A lot of entities are involved in this project, who are interested in hearing about it
  • The concept is completely original to Abu Dhabi.

Structure and Approach

  • The presentation can follow the standard ADEO structure or be customized as per our design.
  • The focus is on showcasing how the project improves the local community. Avoid detailed background information about Toufeeq; including only the logo is sufficient.

About Toufeeq

  • Toufeeq has extensive experience working with the government, particularly in interior design for offices.
  • Past projects or case studies need not be included in the appendix; a simple logo on the introductory slide will suffice.

Concept and Purpose

  • The project aims to create community hubs for women, encouraging social interaction and promoting active lifestyles through sports.
  • These hubs will foster networking and community building among women.

Project Scope

  • Four centers are planned across Abu Dhabi Island to connect neighborhoods and foster harmony.
  • The idea originated from Ali and his two partners, one of whom is a government minister.
    • They observed the limited availability of women-only facilities in Abu Dhabi (only 2-3 gyms) and aimed to address this gap.
    • Rather than a standard gym, these centers promote awareness and community engagement.

Design assets (3D assets)

  • Asset renders are finalized but may be updated based on content optimization, ensuring flexibility.

By Kinzah 


Project Brief: Joy Awards 2026 PowerPoint Template (CONFIRMED)

Client: MBC Group

Event: Joy Awards 2026

Partner Agency: PrezLab

Deadline: Second week of June 2025

Key Contact: Rabih (Decision-maker, MBC)

1. Project Objective

Create a versatile, elegant, and highly visual (static - or basic animations) PowerPoint template that reflects the Joy Awards 2026 artistic direction. The template will be used internally across departments and with external stakeholders, including the General Entertainment Authority.

2. Design Direction

Theme

“An experience unlike any before”

A modern reinterpretation of royal theaters and operatic grandeur, fused with futuristic design and immersive storytelling.

Tone

  • Elegant, Theatrical, and Royal
  • Modern, Clean, and Timeless
  • Must reflect the glamour and grandeur of the event

Design Style & Visuals

  • Use contemporary royal colors (based on MBC brand guidelines)
  • Geometric lines and patterns
  • Luxurious yet functional design
  • Include light and dark theme variations (optional)
  • High-end feel with minimal clutter

3. Functional Requirements

Flexibility

  • Must be fully editable (text, images, layout)
  • Easily adaptable by different departments (content, production, marketing, etc.)
  • Clear structure with movable/reusable elements

Interactivity

  • Use subtle, professional animations for intros and transitions (optional)
  • Avoid overly complex animations that could break during usage

4. Slide Types to Include (Minimum 10 Slides)

  1. Title Slide
    With room for a tagline: “An experience unlike any before”
  2. Section Break Slides
  3. Agenda / Table of Contents
  4. Text-Only Layouts
    • Single column
    • Multi-column
  5. Text + Image Layouts
  6. Image-Focused Slides
  7. Quote/Statement Highlights
  8. Charts & Graphs Layouts
  9. Timeline / Milestones Slide
  10. Closing Slide
    With logo and contact/thank you message

5. Deliverables

  • PPTX file (fully editable)
  • Master file with:
    • Editable color palette
    • Font styles
  • Font files or download links (must be free or licensed)
  • Folder with all design assets used:
    • Icons
    • Graphic motifs
    • Decorative elements
  • Optional: Two theme variations (light/dark)
  • Optional: Subtle animated transitions

6. Usage Scope

  • Internal MBC departments
  • External partners and the General Entertainment Authority
  • Used in production, communication, and high-level presentations

7. Supporting References

Documents attached for design reference:

  • PPT Template Direction (functionality and layout guidance)
  • Creative Vision Brief (artistic direction)
  • Visual Reference Guide (mood board and inspiration)

-------

  • Review existing MBC presentations for tone and branding consistency
  • Meet with set and carpet designers to align visual direction
  • Deliver initial concepts for review before second week of June

rows.description:
Objectives • ADMO aims to revamp their social media strategy to go from reactive “news” style content to an engaging, proactive posting strategy • To achieve this, Hiba (Creative Content Development Director) needs to pitch the “what” and the “how” to ADMO’s Chairperson. She’s already sold on the “why” (but it may be worth reminding her) • The presentation should lay out a compelling case for how the new social media pillars were chosen and present a clear execution plan for moving forward 

Current Pain Points • Text overload • Lack of narrative/ storytelling approach • Lack of emotional visuals/ copywriting • Perhaps we need to add more in terms of “we did our homework” slide/s like benchmarking or looking at peers in the region 

Deliverables • Final presentation in PPT format • Amplified level of design for the main section of the deck (first ~10 slides) • Enhanced level of design for the social media pillar “cards” • Optional: Animations (if they aid the story)

Material/ Look & Feel Approach • Content is attached (I doubt it’s final, align further with the client) • Brand guidelines and templates are already in our drive from a previous job (but validate again with the client) • Try to innovate with the concept creation as they don’t seem to have much inspiration for highly-visual slides • Perhaps we can incorporate people images to represent the idea of an engaged community with a particular focus on youth

Success Criteria • Very visual deck • Compelling storyline • Emotional copy and tone

rows.description:
Objective

The goal is to introduce the Afkar app at the ForsaTEK event on June 23rd. As the app is still in development, the focus will be on:

  • Presenting its purpose
  • Sharing the story behind its creation
  • Highlighting the team who brought the idea to life
  • Showcasing the audience it’s meant to serve

Afkar is more than an app - it’s an infrastructure for innovation, a platform that nurtures employee ideas from inception to implementation.

Content Focus

From the shared presentation, we’ll mainly use Slides 2 and 5, which map out the innovation journey: Research → Trial → Launch.

We’ll also consider adding a clear call-to-action to wrap up the announcement meaningfully.

Design Direction

  • Visual Style: Clean, simple, and elevated (Apple-style)
  • Tone: Minimal text, impactful messaging, visually strong storytelling
  • Animations: Subtle enhancements to support narrative flow
  • Branding: Flexible – only the Afkar logo is confirmed so far
  • Color Palette: Open to exploration - you may experiment with Emirates palette (e.g. red balanced with blue) or something entirely fresh
  • Screen Size: 16:9

Timeline

  • Deadline: Preferably by Friday, to allow the weekend for any final refinements
  • Event Date: Monday, June 23rd

Please find all project materials linked here.

rows.description:
Hello team,

We've got our very first project from Emirates NBD. 

Context

Emirates NBD is upgrading internal executive presentations and communications to replace outdated, text-heavy formats with clear, visual, and branded design. PrezLab’s role is purely design — no content creation or restructuring.

Objective

  • Deliver polished, executive-ready presentations and dashboards.
  • Apply consistent, modern visual design aligned with Emirates NBD’s brand (guidelines pending).
  • Support clear communication of data and strategy via design only.

We will be developing dashboards, email templates, and PowerPoint slides. 

Creative Direction

  • Modern, clean, executive-level visual storytelling.
  • Clear data visualization, infographics, and hierarchy.
  • Corporate tone but visually engaging.
  • Newsletter: PrezLab to advise on a different name for it (as it isn't an actual newsletter) and visual identity only.

The project focuses on the development of new branches. Considering the audience type, the content will be light, simple, and easy to digest. The goal is for the slides to clearly convey what the audience needs to see and know instantly, avoiding heavy text.

On the G'drive, you can find some samples and older files that you can benefit from.

Notes: Documents attached are just references on previous work, we will be creating new ones with a new concept to be iterated monthly (basically adding the new updated content).

Intro & Overview:

  • Content should be cohesive, easy on the eye, and aligned with brand guidelines.
  • Include a clear hierarchy of information.
  • Unify color coding across all slides.
  • Visualize charts to make them more understandable and impactful.

Content and Style Considerations:

  • The slides will not follow the exact style of sample slides provided.
  • Potential for revamping and improving the slides:
    • Some slides will be easier to adapt.
    • Others may require more effort or a complete redesign.

Data Visualization:

  • Transform data tables into a dashboard that can be updated monthly.
  • Recreate screenshots if needed to improve clarity and visual appeal.

Formatting and Templates:

  • Maintain the overall template, but free to make changes if improvements are identified.
  • Each deck must maintain its color coding for consistency.

Newsletter:

  • Will be created in MS Word; can also be designed as an emailer or an A4 PowerPoint slide.
  • Design should be distinct and match branding.
  • The newsletter will serve as an emailer.
  • A suggested name for the newsletter should be creative and out-of-the-box.
  • Provide a sample design first.
  • The newsletter layout should align with the branding dos and don’ts, which need to be provided upfront.
  • Use relevant images, but avoid unnecessary visuals.
  • The content should be concise, primarily visual and numerical, targeted at:
    • NBD Properties
    • Board of Directors (BoD)
    • C-suite Executives
  • The newsletter should be engaging but straightforward, requiring minimal reading.


Plan, Timelines, and File Specifics:

File Name / ComponentDue DateMaterials Receipt DateNumber of SlidesAdditional Notes
Workplace Reimagined & Branch Delivery DashboardsJuly 4th, 2025 (EOD); extension till Sunday if neededTo be received by July 2nd, 2025

Approx. 12-13

Start with dashboards (template). Final versions shared on July 2nd. We can have a call afterward to review and align on design.
WRS Steering Committee PackJuly 4th, 2025 (EOD); extension till Sunday if neededTo be received by July 2nd, 2025To be developed after dashboards are finalized.
Management Committee Meeting PaperJuly 11th, 2025To be received by July 7th, 2025Around 20Second priority. Material expected by July 7th.
Newsletters / International Ops Steering Committee / Sales PerformanceTo be discussedTBATBASpecific due dates and details are yet to be finalized.


Additional notes:

• Once materials are shared, we will hold a call to showcase the developed concepts and align on the design for the new materials.

• Starting point is the dashboards since they serve as a template.


Sidenotes and Corrections:

• Visuals and images can be used freely, if they fit the requirements.

• For templates, we will adhere to color coding and guidelines, but flexibility is permitted for other design aspects.

• The newsletter will be distributed as an emailer rather than a PDF.

We are tasked with enhancing the 92 slides of the technical proposal. The design should combine the look and feel of both Cbloc and Kadana. Since this report will be presented to the Minister, it’s important to elevate the design while maintaining a professional and corporate appearance.

As per the client’s request, we can revert to the pattern used in the second slide. Let’s incorporate icons and make the content more visually engaging. For charts, please improve their visualization and use color differentiation to clearly distinguish elements. Additionally, ensure that any items highlighted in red are emphasized, as they are intended to draw the Minister’s attention.

Jul 9, 2025

The Gathering Company Profile- Strategy & PPT Amplified


Summary

We discussed enhancing the company profile's design and structure, emphasizing visual storytelling and addressing issues like text overlaying images and inconsistent design. Marah expressed a preference for keeping "brand activation" and "branding or marketing" separate, while Nour Karadsheh suggested merging them. Mariam Qadadeh discussed the preference for bilingual documentation and confirmed that she would share milestones.

The existing company profile needs significant enhancements in design, structure, and storytelling, particularly regarding services and major projects. Key issues include text overlaying images, inconsistent design, heavy text, and a lack of visual appeal. The company's original branding, featuring gradients and a grainy texture, should be maintained, and an artistic, layered image approach is preferred over simple placeholder boxes.


A major challenge is effectively showcasing both mega and minor projects while maintaining an engaging narrative without overwhelming clients. The goal is to provide value to each project and service.


The company profile needs to be adaptable and tailored to different audiences, with a focus on visuals for quick skimming. The desired tone is a balance of fun and luxurious, not overly formal. The company's history is considered important, especially for high-level executives.


Service sections should mostly remain the same, but "brand activation" will remain separate from "branding or marketing." The presentation structure will be optimized by reshuffling slides and highlighting specific projects, focusing on those lasting two to three days.


Design preferences include avoiding text overlaying images and ensuring images have a "more alive" appearance rather than being "boxed."


Multiple stakeholders, including project managers, strategy and planning, creative commercial teams, and the CEO, use the company profile, necessitating flexibility for project removal based on the audience.


Bilingual documentation is ideal, but the pros and cons of separate documents will be considered. The standard screen size for presentations is preferred, and click ability for smooth transitions will be implemented.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Details

  • Company Profile Enhancement Marah Alkhaldi identified the existing company profile as needing enhancements in design and structure, particularly in storytelling for their services and major projects (00:00:00). Mustafa AlTajer agreed, noting issues with text overlaying images and an inconsistent design (00:03:41). Marah Alkhaldi also noted that the company profile has heavy text and needs to be more visual, with minimal descriptions for services to avoid overwhelming clients while maintaining important information (00:01:57).
  • Branding Guidelines and Visuals Marah Alkhaldi stated that their brand focuses on colors and playing with text, emphasizing that they wish to proceed with their original branding, which includes gradients and a grainy texture. Mustafa AlTajer requested their brand guidelines and image library to leverage their existing assets, noting that their work is often imagery-based (00:05:03). Marah Alkhaldi confirmed that they will filter and provide approved images, explaining that they want an artistic, layered image approach rather than images simply in placeholder boxes (00:06:29).
  • Project Presentation and Storytelling Marah Alkhaldi highlighted the challenge of showcasing both mega and minor projects within the company profile, stating a need for a better way to tell the story of each service and project without overwhelming clients. They indicated that the current company profile struggles to give value to each project (00:01:57) (00:09:11). Nour Karadsheh suggested summarizing provided services for each project to highlight them, and Marah Alkhaldi confirmed that they highlight the services themselves and trust the team's recommendations on this (00:18:38).
  • Communication and Information Sharing Mustafa AlTajer proposed creating a WhatsApp group for communication regarding missing information and any quick messages that come to mind. Marah Alkhaldi agreed to provide any missing information and mentioned that a sheet could also be used if preferred (00:10:52). They also mentioned that they would provide detailed information on the main projects that have significantly contributed to the company's reputation (00:30:30).
  • Company History and Tone Nour Karadsheh inquired if including company history would add value, to which Marah Alkhaldi responded that it is important, especially for high-level executives. Marah Alkhaldi emphasized the need for an adaptable company profile that can be tailored depending on the recipient, focusing on visuals for quick skimming (00:12:19). They specified that the desired tone should be a balance between fun and luxurious, and not too formal, to appeal to both event attendees and governmental entities (00:13:44).
  • Service Section Merging Nour Karadsheh suggested merging "brand activation" and "branding or marketing" sections. Marah Alkhaldi preferred to keep them separate, clarifying that brand activation is distinct from merchandise and focuses on elements like booths (00:15:08). They also noted that they have a separate merchandise section with their branding team (00:16:41).
  • Presentation Structure and Flexibility Marah Alkhaldi expressed a desire for recommendations on the company profile's structure, suggesting initial drafts for the cover and dividers. Nour Karadsheh confirmed that they would optimize the structure by reshuffling slides and highlighting specific projects or activities (00:16:41). Marah Alkhaldi stated that the sections would mostly remain the same, but previous experiences would be changed, with a focus on projects lasting two to three days rather than one-day events (00:29:16).
  • Design Preferences and Inconsistencies Mustafa AlTajer pointed out that text overlaying images in the current profile is confusing, and Marah Alkhaldi agreed about inconsistencies in the design, particularly disliking the overlap of text. Marah Alkhaldi also noted that images should not always be "boxed," preferring a more "alive" appearance for elements like introductory slides (00:03:41) (00:19:58).
  • Stakeholder Involvement and Usage Marah Alkhaldi explained that multiple departments and individuals, including project managers, strategy and planning, creative commercial teams, and the CEO, leverage the company profile. They highlighted that the presentation needs to be flexible, allowing for easy removal of projects depending on the audience, as project managers focus more on projects, while the CEO presents to high-level entities (00:21:19).
  • Bilingual Documentation and Milestones Mariam Qadadeh inquired about the preference for language in the document, and Marah Alkhaldi stated that the ideal situation is bilingual, but they are open to recommendations on the pros and cons of two separate documents (00:24:57). Mariam Qadadeh mentioned that they would conduct research before going deeper into the content and would share milestones with Marah Alkhaldi (00:26:43).
  • Presentation Format and Clickability Mustafa AlTajer noted that the company profile's current ratio is different from typical presentations and asked if it was a design commitment. Mariam Qadadeh suggested sticking to the standard screen size for presentations, especially since it will often be shared as a PDF via WhatsApp (00:31:54). Mustafa AlTajer confirmed that clickability for smooth transitions to specific sections would be taken into consideration (00:31:54).

rows.description:
Qubital Park is a real estate development in Germany that combines entertainment, housing, and commercial all in one, and it is part of a smaller town next. 

The scope of work for the website would be 

1) Content Creation -  They have some drafts and ideas, but we will be optimizing the content and optimize it for SEO purposes

2) UI/UX Design with about 7 landing pages

o    Home (highlighting the latest news and key updates)

o    The District (project description, vision, and development progress)

o    Offers (rental of existing spaces, sale of plots)

o    News (updates and upcoming events)

o    Press (press releases and downloadable materials)

o    Partners & Initiatives (featuring Q.AX and Space2Create)

o    Contact (contact persons, contact form, and directions)

 

3) Development, which we will be outsourcing and just monitoring and ensuring that developers deliver timely.

  • Design:

o    A modern, clean layout reflecting the new logo and color scheme (pink, orange, cyan)

o    Clear and user-friendly content structure

o    Visually appealing presentation of rental and sales offers

  • Functionalities:

o    Responsive design (optimized for both mobile and desktop devices)

o    Easy content management (e.g., WordPress or a comparable CMS)

o    Ability for our team to quickly update news and listings

o    External linking to partner websites (Q.AX, Space2Create)

  • Technical Requirements:

o    GDPR-compliant implementation (including cookie consent management)

o    Basic SEO optimization (search engine visibility)

Client: Dr. Yousef – ICU consultant with 25+ years of leadership and management experience.

Project: Pitch deck for a wellness and longevity clinic he’s launching in Saudi Arabia.

Purpose: Fundraising – he has over 25 investor meetings scheduled.

Deck Requirements:

  • Immersive and compelling
  • 20–25 slides max
  • Includes strategy, not just design
  • Aligned with existing brand guidelines (logo, colors, mood)
    Deadline: no specific date but asap.

In-person Presentation Brief

  • Client hates to work online so we promised him to have 3–4 in-person touchpoints throughout the process to ensure everything is translated exactly the way he wants it. 
  • Presentation structure should start with problems, then transition into solutions.
  • He’s mainly seeking support with storytelling, structure, and pitch clarity.
  • The deck is not meant to be read off , it's a visual aid to enhance the audience's experience and understanding.
  • It will always be delivered verbally, so slides should complement the speaker, not replace him. (might work on a pdf teaser for him later)
  • Must consistently tie back to Vision 2030, MBS’s vision, and national impact.
  • He wants a strong hook around lifespan to open with.
  • Referenced presentations he loved:
    • The clickable "universe" deck (strong storytelling & flow)
    • Egis’s data visualization (impactful clarity)
    • Saudi Coffee Company’s sales journey (narrative + numbers)
  • He wants to visualize data and numbers creatively, in a way that’s compelling and memorable.


Scope + Links (To log hours)

  • Main Task
  • Creative storytelling -----> 56.00 hours (Anat)
  • Concept creation-----> 16 hours (Amani)
  • PPT immersive----> 40 hours (Amani) 

Anat Hawari will be working on the 1st section of the pitch deck and will be done  by tomorrow, we are to tackle this file in sections. 

Once done with that we can proceed on the design to create a concept from the completed section. 

The client is very picky as you can tell from the refs from here, those are previous pitches he worked on. I'll update you with more details later on.

General Notes:

Design Guidelines:

  • Fonts:

PLAN INTERNALLY:

A very beautiful thing Jann Javier helped in was this file (SCOPED_20250801_progress_update V6 (1)), in this file you will have some sort of a plan tp help you navigate which slides can handle the following level of design (this is based on the initial look, if you feel the slides can handle further beautification go on for it please ):

rows.taskName: S02147 | SERA - Proactive Consumer Care - Roadshow Presentation
rows.accountName: Accenture Middle East BV - Abu Dhabi
rows.clientAccount: Accenture Middle East BV - Abu Dhabi
rows.description:
Client Brief – SERA Consumer Journey Presentation

Client: Accenture

Contact Person: Jadoun Al-Naber

Project: SERA (Saudi Electricity Regulatory Authority) – Proactive Consumer Care Journey

Audience: His Royal Highness, external stakeholders, and other regulatory entities

Deadline: Thursday, July 31st (End of next week)

Objective

Develop a visually engaging, non-technical, and story-driven presentation that clearly outlines SERA’s transformation journey toward proactive consumer care, emphasizing the shift from reactive regulation to consumer-centric thinking. The presentation must inspire, resonate with diverse executive and public audiences, and articulate how the consumer is placed at the heart of SERA’s regulatory mission—highlighting the vision, context, challenges, strategic response, and desired impact.

Purpose

The presentation will serve multiple communication needs:

  • Used when SERA meets with other entities to:
    • Show how well they’ve done something
    • Present their vision of what they want to do
  • Shared with:
    • The Board of SEC (Saudi Electricity Company)
    • The Board of SERA
    • Intended to give an executive-level view of what SERA is trying to achieve
  • Used in global forums where SERA participates, including:
    • Industry-specific forums related to the utility sector
    • Other forums beyond the utility sector
  • Acts as a “stage zero” communication tool:
    • To explain what SERA is trying to achieve at the highest level
    • Similar in approach to how Vision 2030 was communicated at events like Expo
  • Animation is essential due to the diverse and high-level nature of its audiences and uses
  • PDF distribution: The deck must also stand on its own without animation, as it may be shared in static format

Presentation Requirements & Content Notes

Tone & Message

  • Must eloquently and consistently reinforce that the consumer is at the center of everything SERA does
  • Storytelling approach preferred – avoid traditional, corporate-style animations
  • Ensure clarity even when static (PDF)

Slide Breakdown & Key Content

Slide 2

  • Main message: “The consumer is at the heart” of the vision SERA wants to achieve
  • There is an inspiring ambition to transform what consumer care means, driven by this focus

Slide 3

  • While the ambition is strong, challenges exist that can hinder progress toward proactive consumer care:
    • Surge in demand due to population growth and increased migration into Saudi Arabia
    • This surge causes a ripple effect on electricity demand and output, leading to:
    • More pressure on the electricity system
    • More outages and strain on the sector
      • Rising consumer expectations, influenced by:
    • Other government entities offering better digital services (setting new benchmarks)
    • Private sector excellence in customer experience (e.g., Amazon, Marriott)
    • Lack of consumer loyalty in electricity regulation (only one service provider – SEC)
    • Need to build trust and assurance, not loyalty
    • AI-driven expectations: personalization and instant service

Slides 4–6

Slide 4

  • Titled “Winds of Change”
  • Explores what’s driving the demand surge in Saudi Arabia

Slide 5

  • The surge in demand leads to four major issues:
    • Service delivery pressure
    • Disruption in services
    • Response delays and backlog
    • Rising volume of requests/issues

Slide 6

  • New consumer demands are emerging:
    • Expectations shaped by government and private sector innovations
    • Influence of AI: faster service, personalization
    • Consumer expectations are evolving rapidly

Slide 7

  • Response to challenges: SERA has developed a Proactive Consumer Care Vision
  • The slide presents:
    • Vision
    • Objectives
    • Transformation pillars
    • Platform
    • Transformation enablers
  • Message: “We developed a strategy, and this is the output.”

Slide 8

  • Introduces three key themes in consumer care:
    • Monitor continuously
    • Analyze
    • Act based on insights
  • These themes are grouped under a program framework called AIDE
  • Each letter in AIDE stands for a theme in proactive intervention

Slide 9

  • Breakdown of the AIDE Framework:
    • A = Anticipate: predict consumer needs
    • I = Inform: communicate clearly with consumers
    • D = Drive: encourage self-service and reduce dependency on support
    • E = Empathize: show care and respond based on consumer feedback
  • The AIDE concept may be represented visually as a horizontally spinning sphere

Slide 10

  • Request for visual concept development of AIDE framework
  • Explanation of how interventions can span multiple AIDE areas:
    • Example: A single communication intervention may include:
    • Anticipating needs (advance notice)
    • Informing (clarity)
    • Driving self-service (link to digital service)
    • Empathizing (tone and feedback integration)
      • Some interventions target one area, others cover all
      • Example interventions:
    • Early warning/predictive detection = Anticipate
    • Automated compensation/SLA enforcement = Anticipate + Empathize
  • Suggested visualization: Venn diagram, but open to designer’s interpretation
  • Additional content may be added after visual concept is finalized

Slide 11

  • Shows a before-and-after view of applying the AIDE framework

Additional Notes

  • Animations should feel like storytelling, not traditional slide transitions
  • Design style should not be based on SERA’s website or Instagram – they are not relevant
  • Slides must be independently understandable without animation, as the deck will be shared in PDF format
  • Font: Use Bahij in multiple formats (bold/italic)

Deliverables

Date (Internal) Date (External) Milestone Description
Monday Morning Monday Midday Sketch Review Call We will have a call to present the sketches
Storyboard Submission & Approval Share complete storyboard for client review and alignment on structure/content
Tuesday COB Tuesday COB Design Concept Delivery Present initial visual concept for feedback and refinement
Wednesday Midday Wednesday First WIP Deck Submission Submit the first draft incorporating content and design for review
Thursday Thursday COB Final Delivery Deliver the final, fully approved version of the deck

rows.description:
heyy

We have a new project and we kindly need your support with the concept creation 

Please ensure you read the brief thoroughly and review the content in detail.

It is essential to understand the material before starting any design work.

Also, make sure to go through Nadine’s gap analysis, which identifies key content and storytelling areas needing visual elevation. It will guide the direction and style of the final deck.

We will work on both the design and content in parallel to have an early kick start, please let us know if anything is unclear

✅ FINAL PROJECT BRIEF: Jordan Source – Investor Pitch Deck Revamp (2025)

🎯 Objective

Revamp the current pitch deck in both content and design to:

  • Clearly communicate why Jordan is a destination for ICT investors
  • Convince investors at first glance — without requiring research
  • Highlight success stories and Jordan’s achievements
  • Simplify and clarify the investor journey and value proposition
  • Emphasize incentives and government support

To present Jordan as a tech-enabled, cost-effective, talent-rich, and strategically located hub for MENA market expansion — particularly for BPO/ITO and gaming sector investors.

🧠 Strategic Focus

  • Position Jordan as the “kitchen” for digital operations in MENA
  • Enable quick decision-making for investors without deep research
  • Use proof points and government-backed incentives to reinforce trust

👥 Target Audience

Segment

Description

ICT InvestorsIPO, BPO, ITO, and gaming companies
MENA Expansion FirmsCompanies using Jordan as a base for the region
Outreach LeadsFirms reached via roadshows or BD efforts
PromotersIndividuals/partners helping market Jordan abroad

📍 Usage Scenarios

  • Roadshows (in-person or virtual)
  • 1:1 investor meetings
  • Post-call follow-up emails
  • Outreach decks
  • Leave-behind material

📦 Delivery Format

  • Static deck (for email)
  • Dynamic version (for live presentations)
  • Optional: One-pager (after deck completion, TBC)

🔁 Revamp Scope

🔧 Content & Structure

1. Story Flow

  • Needs to be revised and strategically structured
  • Streamline messaging for clear investor flow
  • Value proposition should be instantly clear

If there are specific numbers we believe should be included, we should inform the client and they can provide them.

 

2. Deck Hierarchy

  • Keep core slides in the main deck
  • Move dense info (e.g., registration process) to appendix

3. Design Refresh

  • Align with brand guidelines (to be provided)

🔍 Key Slides That Need Work

 

Slide/TopicFix Needed
Slides 6–7Outdated global reports – client to share updated data
Slide 8Add success stories split into local vs global companies. Consider visual global map with Microsoft, Amazon, etc.
Slide 25 (Incentives)Storytelling unclear – needs restructure and visual clarity
ISP InfoNot included – client to share content
Abdali IncentivesAlready included – OK
KHBP IncentivesStill in negotiation – clarity needed from client
“Country of Firsts”Under consideration (e.g. first Arabic email) – pending client confirmation
Company RegistrationTrim, redesign visually; move to appendix with key summary in main deck

 

💡 New Slides to Add

  • Visual investor map showing Jordan’s footprint (Amazon, Microsoft, etc.) or split slide: Local vs. Global success stories
  • Incentive hub spotlight: Abdali & King Hussein Business Park (pending)
  • “Country of Firsts” tech milestone slide (if approved)

💸 Incentive Deep Dive (To Strategize)

1. Talent Support

  • For tech-enabled companies hiring ICT employees
  • Covers 50% of salaries for Jordanians for 1 year (quarterly hire: 5 employees per quarter)
  • 70% coverage for non-Jordanians

2. Market Expansion

  • Targeted for growth-stage companies — not for new companies — but meant to support existing companies expanding outside Jordan or into distant areas within the country.
    For example, if expanding within Jordan, the new location must be significantly far from their current base (e.g., from Amman to Irbid).
  • Matching grants (50/50) for BD, marketing, hiring, travel

3. Upskilling Support

  • Funding available to train Jordanian youth aligned with workforce needs

🔗 Client will provide official RFA links or documents 

📍 Next Steps

 

DateMilestone

July 20

Kick off work on both the Design Concept & Content Opt

July 24

Share design concept (visual direction)
July 31Deliver strategized content (B&W version)

Aug 3

Await feedback from your side

Aug 4–5

Finalize design and content direction

Aug 6

Begin full integration of content into visual design

Aug 19

Target final deck delivery

 

Note: These milestones assume no major changes or additions. Delays in inputs or large iterations may shift the timeline.

 

🔴 Assets from the Client

    • Content here
    • Updated reports (slides 6–7) here : You'll find two documents, one of which has information about the whole ICT sector in Jordan with mentions of Jordans performance in global reports. The other document (Jordan in Global Reports)  was based on our own findings. Please go through both documents and assess which points are worth mentioning and go with the story.
    • ISP information : It is a general slide regarding the internet service providers in Jordan (Zain, Orange, Umniah).
    • Confirmation on KHBP incentives : TBC
    •  “Country of Firsts” slide here
    • Brand guidelines & Assets library here
    • Official RFA links for incentives (appendix/hyperlinking) : https://grow.jo/ - All incentives are clearly labelled on the left panel.

We are tasked with creating a newsletter for SAMI’s 300th edition. SAMI has been consistently publishing newsletters for some time, with the main purpose of keeping employees informed about the latest company news and milestones. The newsletter is typically shared in PDF format.

Since this is a special milestone — the 300th edition — the design should stand out and be more visually engaging than the previous editions, while still maintaining consistency. We can refer to the previous newsletters (already uploaded) to follow the same overall structure, but we will enhance the visuals to highlight the significance of this edition.

Additionally, this template will include a “Newborn” section, which will be added to the design to celebrate and share personal milestones within the company community.

Finally, the file should be designed to be easy to use and edit, enabling SAMI’s internal team to seamlessly plug in content for future editions without complications.

Company profiles- Brief:

Our client, Abdul Latif Jameel (ALJ), has approached us to enhance their enterprise company profile. As you may know, their name has long been associated with "Toyota" in KSA, but they aim to shift that perception and highlight their position as a diverse group with multiple subsidiaries and service lines.

Our objective is to develop a new, refined company profile—both for the main entity and its subsidiaries—that presents a fresh, professional look while ensuring a compelling storyline and seamless flow of information.

 

Below are the project milestones for the company profiles:

Strategy:

  1. Develop the skeleton for the main company profile and identify any additional information needed (Start: Sunday, March 23 | Delivery: March 25).
  2. Provide the first full draft (~25 slides) and review with the client by April 10.
  3. Share the final draft by April 14.
  4. Begin work on the subsidiaries—each profile will be a maximum of 10 slides, with approximately half derived from the main company profile. We will create a skeleton for each and add 3–4 unique slides per subsidiary.
  5. Outsource Arabic translation.

Design:

  1. Get onboarded on ALJ’s new corporate identity (March 19–20).
  2. Develop the creative concept by March 25.
  3. Work in parallel with the strategy team and deliver the final draft by April 14.
  4. Set the style for each subsidiary by April 20, share the files by the 25th
  5. Flip the content to Arabic and deliver by April 30

Expo 2030 Saudi Presentation Concept

We will be creating a concept/template for Expo 2023 Saudi Arabia that you can use later when the content is ready in 2 weeks. The concept will be Accenture-branded while incorporating Expo visual elements, allowing you to populate the slides with your content later.

Objective of the project:

Expro2023 is bringing together all relevant companies involved to pitch how the strategy should look and define what the strategy’s digital legacy should be. The deck should consist of 20–30 slides and explain how Accenture envisions the digital strategy for Expo 2023 in Riyadh.

Scope of Work:

  • Create a concept/template deck (Accenture-branded) that can later be populated with client-specific content.
  • Heavily incorporate Expo-specific elements, such as Riyadh city visuals, digital innovation, and smart city features.
  • Present a cohesive, executive-ready structure for both high-level strategy and detailed content.

Template Design & Style:

  • Updated Accenture template, visually geared toward Expo 2023
  • Use Accenture colors and fonts combined with Expo/Riyadh-inspired design elements
  • Modern, highly visual, and impactful for C-level presentations
  • Avoid split templates; use 2–3 horizontal sections at the bottom
  • Emphasize digital strategy, innovation, and smart city themes
  • Designed for meeting room TVs – clear, readable, and visually engaging

Deliverables:

  • A ready-to-use concept/template for client review
  • Flexible structure that allows content to be added later
  • Executive-style presentation that positions the client as innovative and forward-thinking

Timeline:

  • Concept/template to be shared by Thursday, 21st for client review and feedback

Content & Structure

Jordanian Client, Ayman Abdulllah - To create a company profile for him.

Brand Concept: Nooliz

  • A new noodles brand created in KSA.
  • The concept: making noodles more relevant and appealing to a specific younger demographic.
  • Name origin: derived from Noodles → simplified to Nooliz for easier pronunciation.

Market Context

  • Current Leader: Indomie – dominates the market with 93% share.
    • Background: The original factory was Japanese. Indomie (Indonesian brand) acquired the manufacturing license and monopolized distribution.
  • Remaining Market (7%):
    • 42 competitors entered → 28 exited → 16 remained.
    • Example: Masna3 Haloomi imports Korean noodles. Expensive, but became trendy.

Research & Insights

  1. Vision Study 1
    • Indomie is unbeatable in its current space (chicken, beef, vegetable flavors).
    • Strategy: Don’t compete head-on; instead, explore new flavors.
    • Key finding:
      • 28% of Indomie’s consumer base is in West Amman (approx. $50M market).
      • These consumers have higher purchasing power and interest.
  2. Vision Study 2
    • Core age group: 6–18 years old.
    • Their consumption is parent-controlled → parental approval is essential.
  3. Vision Study 3
    • Most popular meals in Jordan skew towards chicken over meat.
    • Selected flavors:
      • Tandoori chicken (spicy)
      • Broasted chicken
      • Shish tawooq (grilled spicy)
      • Shish tawooq (mild)
      • Chicken curry

Product Vision

  • Not to compete directly with Indomie.
  • Create unique, locally inspired flavors appealing to kids and teens while also convincing parents.
  • Focus on nutritious noodles: enriched with calcium, B12, and other nutrients.
  • Packaging: lined with aluminum for freshness and health safety.

Marketing & Positioning

  • Target audience: Gen Z / Kids & Teens (6–18 yrs).
  • Insights:
    • They spend time on iPads, gaming, going out, or at school.
  • Gamification Strategy:
    • Partner with gaming platforms.
    • Each purchase includes a QR code → rewards players with in-game currency.
    • Campaign designed for 44K winners (randomized).

Ingredients & Quality

  • Made with high-quality wheat imported into KSA (not locally produced, but highest quality globally sourced).

Presentation Purpose

This deck is to be shown to investors, stakeholders, and distributors. It covers:

  • Why: Market gap and consumer need.
  • How: Strategy, positioning, and execution plan.
  • When: Market entry timing.
  • What: Product features, flavors, and value proposition.

The deck should be crafted with a strategic, visionary tone, aiming to both inform and inspire the board members, particularly Faisal Belhoul, to take action in supporting INJAZ’s expansion and sustainability.

• The final name would be Omnipreneurship Newsletter- Q2 2025
  • We stick to the same design we have done previously.
  • Mailchimp username is d.bahareth@dabbagh.com / Password is Db@123456

rows.description:
Hi team, 

We have a very exciting project for a firm called Integra, a commercial consulting firm expanding into wholesale food manufacturing and exporting Mexican products to the US. Their objective is to revamp and standardize their core presentations to strengthen their storytelling, simplify their narrative, and align visuals with their brand identity. 

The project focuses on three key deliverables:

  1. Commercial Presentation
  2. Business Plan + Framework Slides
  3. Proposal Template

Areas to Improve & Proposed Approach

1. Commercial Presentation (attached)

  • Current Gaps:
    • Informative but lacks clear story flow
    • Text-heavy in sections, complicating message delivery
    • Competitive advantages not presented in a simple, impactful way
  • Proposed Approach:
    • Restructure into a concise, executive-style story
    • Simplify messaging for clarity and impact
    • Highlight competitive advantages using infographics, visuals, and sharper messaging

2. Business Plan (here) + Framework Slides (attached)

  • Current Gaps:
    • Heavy reliance on basic tables/boxes with limited visual appeal
    • Inconsistent design styles (icons, boxes, colors)
    • Lack of data visualization assets (charts, graphs, etc.)
  • Proposed Approach:
    • Enhance visual appeal and consistency
    • Integrate infographics for key ideas
    • Add charts and graphs to visualize data more effectively

3. Proposal Template (attached)

  • Current Gaps:
    • Strong content flow but lacks visual impact
    • Overuse of bullet points/text-heavy slides
    • Repetitive layouts with limited emotional engagement
  • Proposed Approach:
    • Shift toward visual storytelling
    • Introduce contextual infographics to reinforce messages
    • Use dynamic layouts to create variety and engagement

4. Brand Guidelines: You can find their brand guidelines, color palette, etc. in the document (here).

Overall Goal

Revamp Integra’s core presentations by simplifying storytelling, enhancing visual impact, and aligning designs with a consistent brand identity — ensuring materials are compelling, easy to digest, and effective in supporting business objectives.

rows.description:
Summary / Direction

  • Maintain the client’s current visual language; they’re comfortable with the PDF reference.
  • Convert the deck to 16:9 while preserving the existing template (master).
  • Typography: Georgia or Arial (match the firm’s template if specified).
  • Color approach: client dislikes heavy white backgrounds—use light-blue over white where applicable and expand color blocks/boxes to reduce white space.

Design System & Layout Rules

  • Template fidelity: Follow the PDF closely; adjust proportions for 16:9 but don’t change structure.
  • First slide: Keep the “A” element. Do not remove it.
  • Imagery: Agenda slide may include one photo; no photos on the rest to keep it clean and professional.
  • Type & hierarchy: Use firm-approved font (Georgia/Arial). Keep headings/subheads consistent with the PDF.

Slide-by-Slide Guidance

  • Slide 1 (Title): Keep the “A”. Keep the light-blue on white treatment; ensure balance in 16:9.
  • Agenda: May include a single, tasteful photo. Otherwise minimal.
  • Slide 5 (Info Graphic): Rework into infographic style; add a contrasting bottom color band to anchor content.
  • Slide 7 (12-Month Plan): Not a sequential roadmap. Visualize parallel tracks (e.g., racetrack lanes) to show initiatives moving side-by-side over 12 months.
  • Slide 8 (Closing): Make it strong & bold. Two safe variants:
    • Dark theme statement slide,
    • Or light-theme report style with assertive color blocking (minimize plain white).
  • All slides: Content text may change later; lock layouts now so revisions are text-only.

Deliverables & Timeline (Today)

  • By EOD: 3–4 slides
    • Cover Page + Agenda + two content slides (e.g., Slide 5 + Slide 7),
    • Feedback slide (notes/questions/assumptions).
  • Provide both PDF preview and editable file.

Assumptions / Notes

  • Final text may be updated by client; no layout changes expected after today.
  • Colors drawn from existing PDF; extend light-blue + complementary blocks to reduce white background.
  • Maintain professional tone—no heavy imagery beyond the agenda photo.

Action Items

  1. Convert master to 16:9 while retaining the current template structure.
  2. Lock fonts to Georgia/Arial per firm standards.
  3. Build Slide 5 infographic with a contrasting bottom strip.
  4. Build Slide 7 with parallel racetrack lanes across 12 months.
  5. Build Slide 8 in two variants (dark statement vs. light report blocking).
  6. Prepare Agenda slide with a single photo.
  7. Compile a Feedback/Assumptions slide.
  8. Export PDF + source file and share for review by EOD.

Client: Emirates (Recruitment Team)

Speaker: Andrew (Head of Talent Integration, Emirates)

Event Host: Avature (October Conference)

Output: Animated PowerPoint presentation (potentially exported as timed MP4) + Mood Film (TBC)

Delivery Deadline: 30th September

Objectives

  • Design a slick, impactful, bold presentation to support Andrew’s keynote speech.
  • Create a smooth visual flow that works across three large screens on stage.
  • Ensure the presentation functions as a visual narrative (minimal text, bold statements, facts, quotes).
  • Option to explore video-style format vs. click-through waypoints.
  • Develop a fresh, modern creative direction (not tied to Emirates or Avature brand guidelines), with subtle nods to Emirates (e.g., red tones).

Event & Screen Setup

  • Stage Dimensions: 13m (W) × 3m (D) × 0.4m (H)
  • Backdrop (White Panel): 13m × 4.9m (H)
  • Screens: 3 total, each 4.0m (W) × 2.25m (H)
  • Placement: Screens arranged side-by-side along the backdrop

Stage Layout Reference:

Format

  • Please design in a standard 16:9 presentation layout (for both slides and film).
  • We’ll first explore if we can use the AI cabin crew mentioned, and plan accordingly.

Screen Capabilities

  • Option 1: Same content displayed on all three screens.
  • Option 2: Content A on the central screen, and Content B mirrored on both side screens.
  • We can switch between these two modes during the presentation.

Limitations

  • We cannot display three different contents across the screens (e.g., A on left, B center, C right).
  • We cannot stretch one piece of content across all three screens.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Content Approach

  • Key Themes: Future of recruitment, AI in hiring, people analytics.
  • Tone: Bold, futuristic, minimal text, high visual impact.
  • Presentation Style:
    • Option A: Animated presentation with smooth, timed flow (exported as MP4)
    • Option B: Animated click-through PowerPoint with controlled waypoints
  • Suggested Visual Ideas:
    • Use one of the screens for a continuous background video (e.g., AI cabin crew avatar moving subtly, scanning the room).
    • Other screens for supporting bold statements, data points, or visuals.

Branding & Design Direction

  • Not Emirates-branded
  • Not Avature-branded
  • Fresh, bold creative concept
  • Some Emirates nods (use of red, accents)
  • Fonts, graphics, and style to be developed independently

Deliverables

  1. Animated PowerPoint presentation
    • Designed for 3 screens setup
    • Minimal text, high-impact visuals
    • Smooth transitions and animations
  2. Mood Film (TBC)
    • If confirmed, short film aligned with presentation theme
    • Editing capability required

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Event Date: October 1st

Opening Sequence

  • Presentation will begin with the CEO of Avature and Andrew on stage.

Context & Connection

  • Emirates Group is one of Avature’s largest users.
  • Avature is an AI recruitment platform used by Emirates for the full hiring lifecycle, from candidate matching to data analytics.
  • The key message: platforms like Avature are shaping the future of recruitment. Emirates serves as a proof point for why others should embrace this shift.

Andrew’s Script & Storyline

  • He wants the presentation to be brought to life with punchy, impactful statements.
  • Current draft is only for context; it needs to evolve into a strong narrative.
  • Andrew’s angle:
    • Recruitment before AI was slow and difficult.
    • With AI, the process is transformed and efficient.
  • Presentation style: Andrew prefers keywords and prompts rather than a fully scripted speech.

Design Direction

  • Visuals should be dark-themed, moody, and striking.
  • Goal: leave the audience feeling that not adopting AI platforms like Avature means falling behind.
    • The tone should indirectly instill urgency and a subtle sense of fear of missing out.
  • Potential design motif: electric light bulb (symbol of innovation, clarity, transformation).

Flexibility

  • Team is open to improving the script and content material to better fit the story.


Internal meeting notes:

  • We do not recommend adding AI Cabin Crew to the screens 
  • What is AI Cabin Crew? 
  • Audience might get distracted 
  • Our recommend is that we use the left and right screen and in the middle to only have a title or a hashtag and w might use graphical elements to give the look and feel of connectivity between screens.
  • We need to understand who will be clicking and controlling the screens
  • For the screens, mood film: we will have the mood film on the side screens and another simple video in the middle screen with a few words that will help connect the ideas with the video (connect the scenes)

Our content and design teams have thoroughly reviewed the files shared. We estimate that it will take around one week to structure the content flow, followed by another week for the design work.

For the content, we will ensure all necessary information is presented in a strategically sequenced manner, adding a storyline touch to guide the reader through the material naturally. We’ll keep the file concise, aiming for around 30 slides while maintaining clarity and impact.

For the design, we will create a fresh concept and visual direction aligned with the brand guidelines. We plan to prepare two versions: a static PDF and another with subtle animations suitable for presentations. 

There are three files uploaded, in which we take all relevant and need information in compile it one file for the company profile.

Reach out if you need anything!

designers.name: Tanya M. Amin Abdal Qader Shteawi

rows.description:
Hello Team,

You’ll find here a tracking table that outlines each document along with:

  • What it includes
  • Effort required
  • Deadline
  • Responsible person

Our target is to finalize everything by September 15th, so sticking to this plan is crucial, it covers every hour of your shifts until then.

I’ve already gone through the documents and removed any empty or one-liner pages

To make things easier, I also asked ChatGPT to summarize each document. You’ll find these summaries on separate sheets, I found them helpful, and I hope you will too. (WIP)


Project Phases

  1. Word Documents (Priority Phase)
    • ~500 pages across 14 documents.
  2. Executive PPTs (Phase 2)
    • A concise version of 2 Word Documents.
    • Strictly executive-level: some storytelling, clear display of numbers and insights.

Timeline & Delivery

  • Word Documents: Kick-off already in progress. First full delivery expected by Tuesday, September 15th.
  • Files will be shared in batches for review to keep alignment and avoid drift.

Scope of Work – Word Documents

Main Service: Formatting, alignment, and consistency check.

Subservice: Visual enhancement of key elements.

Enhancement Scope

  • Design main pages (covers, table of contents, dividers).
  • Enhance visuals (charts, tables, graphics) with consistency across both docs.
  • Apply a subtle abstract background image.
  • Use normal imagery only on covers.
  • Develop a style guide including:
    • Text hierarchy
    • Bullet and numbering styles
    • Headers/footers
    • Color scheme
  • Ensure layout consistency across all pages and documents.

Context & Objective

The documents focus on:

  • Financing, loans, exports, management
  • Appraising investment opportunities and market value trends
  • Loan performance: repayment plans, ROI analysis

The objective:

  • To present findings clearly to management for approval on a loan/risk strategy.
  • To evaluate and communicate both client input and our own analysis/opinions.
  • To maintain an executive, professional tone, straightforward, data-focused, and consistent.

PPT Guidelines (Phase 2)

  • Trimmed-down decks of 2 Word Documents.
    "FI Line of credit template 2025"
    "Risk Management report clean"
  • numbers and analysis should speak clearly.
  • Focus on clarity, consistency, and precision.

👉 Key Emphasis for Design:

  • Clarity of data presentation.
  • Professional, executive-level look and feel.
  • Easy navigation through sections (covers, dividers, ToC).
  • Visual consistency across 500+ pages.

Clustering

  • Templates (light formatting only & style setting): Consultation templates, appraisal templates.
  • Procedures & Policies (content-heavy, formal style): Risk Management, Policy, Guarantee, Export Loan Procedure, FI line procedures.
  • Manuals (guide-like, need more design treatment): Book 3 Export Loans, Manual 1 General, Manual 2 FI Line of Credit.

Risks

500 pages can easily go off-track without consistency. The success of this project depends on a strong base style system that applies to both Word and PPT.

 

Let me know if you have any questions!

Danone Brief  

Folder : Danone Onboarding Prez

Project Overview:

  • Client: Danone (Onboarding of Regional President Strategy presentation)
  • Scope: 25 slides (some will be merged), concept creation, and Do a mix of enhancement for 10 slides and amplification for 15.
  • Deadline: Monday 27th October (urgent). 

Key Deliverables:

  1. Concept Creation: Develop a creative approach that aligns with Danone's brand identity, focusing on visual appeal and clarity,refer to this Brand Identity Guidelines. 
  2. Slide Design : Manage expectations on which 15 slides will be more Amplified and which will be enhanced. Presentation

Key Expectations:  

  • Collaboration: Myreim and Malak are the focal point to help with client input or alignment if needed 

Meeting Minutes

Audience & Objective

  • Audience: GM of MENA (top regional executive).
  • Delivery: To be sent by email.
  • Theme: Light theme presentation.
  • Objective: Impress the audience with a “wow factor” — the presentation should feel polished, impactful, and highly professional.
  • Tone: Reflect professionalism and team excellence, showcasing strong visuals and achievements.

General Notes

  • Maintain guideline alignment while keeping the presentation visually appealing.
  • Highlight the region’s leadership position,  team collaboration, and achievements.
  • Use team photos strategically — ensure they convey authenticity and professionalism.

Slide-by-Slide Notes

Overall visual tiering (per quotation):

  • We will designate around 15 slides as highly visual / amplified and around 10 slides as visual / enhanced.
  • Selection will be based on content priority and density to maximize impact while maintaining coherence.

Slide 4:

Slide 9:
