rows.description:
Hey team,

 

Here’s the full brief below:

 

Building on the successful development of the Zenith Annual Report, we received a request from Accenture’s leadership to develop a standardized presentation template and visual identity for all future Zenith presentations — aligned with the new look and feel that was previously created — and to amplify an existing strategy deck currently in use.

Prezlab’s Role

  1. Amplify and redesign Zenith’s existing 38-slide strategy presentation in the new look and feel.
  2. Create a comprehensive PowerPoint template system — a reusable framework for all future Zenith decks.

Both deliverables must align with Accenture’s approved visual direction.

Deliverables

 

Deliverable 1: Strategy Presentation (38 slides)

Deadline: October 29, 2025

Details:

  • Apply the new approved Zenith look and feel.
  • Visually enhance slides (minor restructuring allowed) — Amplification level.
  • Include light, minimal animation for key visual emphasis (avoid heavy motion).
  • Keep file size optimized for easy sharing by email.

Deliverable 2: PowerPoint Template Pack

Purpose: Create a reusable PowerPoint template library for all future Zenith presentations.

Deadline: To start after October 29 (Phase 2 project).

Details:

The template will be used for internal weekly, monthly, and quarterly Zenith updates.

Dareen will confirm the final list of recurring slide needs (reporting, initiative tracking, etc.).


The template should include layout variations commonly used by the Zenith team, such as:

  • Title slide (title only)
  • Title + strapline (centered and left-aligned options)
  • Content slide with half/half vertical and horizontal splits
  • Table layout (aligned column design)
  • Graph/infographic slides
  • Overview, summary, and transition slide types

 

Meeting: here

Material: here

designers.name: Tanya M. Amin Abdal Qader Shteawi

rows.description:
🎯 Intro

The current content is around 90% complete but requires uplifting through improved structure, enhanced flow and language, and stronger brand integration. 

 

Objective and audience: The goal is to ensure the presentation aligns with ne’ma’s brand identity and makes it easy for potential sponsors to grasp the purpose, the impact, and how their involvement contributes to national goals. We needto make the presentation cohesive, coherent, and aligned with the brandbook, to structure the material into clear initiatives and projects that can be translated into project charters, allowing sponsorship of specific programs under ne’ma.

Extra context: The content will include initiatives and projects, showcasing how each potential partner can sponsor specific initiatives relevant to their focus area. The presentation should guide viewers through a clear, emotionally engaging journey, from understanding the problem to seeing tangible outcomes and ways to get involved.

 

📊 Slide-by-Slide Summary

Slides 1–2 – Cover & Opening

The presentation will start with a cover, followed by a quote from His Highness to emphasize that ne’ma is a presidential initiative. This sets the tone before transitioning into “About ne’ma.”

 

Slide 3 – Problem First

Introduces the issue of hunger and food waste, establishing an emotional connection and highlighting the urgency of the problem.

 

Slide 4 – Global Challenges & UAE Proactivity

Showcases how global challenges align with the UAE’s proactive response. The slide should avoid being text-heavy, using large statistics and impactful visuals.

 

Slide 5 – Why ne’ma Was Mandated

Explains the rationale behind ne’ma’s establishment, connecting to national priorities and the direction of His Highness.

 

Slide 6 – Members & Stakeholders

Presents the steering committee and members to reinforce credibility and showcase collaboration across key national entities this will increase their willingness to contribute considering it is a presidential project back by key strategic partners.

 

Slide 7 – Vision, Mission & Ecosystem

Covers vision, mission, and the ecosystem. There is consideration to simplify it under one slide to avoid congestion and improve readability and comprehension on what to focus on.

 

Slide 8 – Key Pillars & Definitions

Defines food loss and waste while outlining ne’ma’s core pillars. The layout is clear but may need refinements to elevate its visual hierarchy and brand consistency.

 

Slide 9 – Mobilizing Nationwide Action (Approach)

The goal is to visually communicate Ne’ma’s nationwide call to action to reduce food loss and waste by 50% by 2030 through a clear three-step approach: Target, Measure, Act.

  • Target: Define specific reduction goals.
  • Measure: Establish a baseline to identify where waste occurs and gather data for informed interventions.
  • Act: Implement targeted solutions based on findings.

The framework connects five key pillars — data & metrics, awareness, collaboration, policies, and tech solutions — across the entire food value chain, showing how all sectors are involved.

The outcomes go beyond waste reduction, contributing to environmental, social, and food security impacts.

Visually, the current slides feel cluttered. The team suggests simplifying through a clearer illustration, emphasizing key messages, and improving font size and hierarchy for readability, especially since it will likely be presented (not printed).

 

Slide 10 – Knowledge Hub

Highlights reports, studies, and toolkits that showcase tangible outcomes and partner contributions. The placement of this slide may be reconsidered to ensure it doesn’t interrupt the flow.

 

Slide 11 – Driving Measurable Impact

References previous one-pager work and emphasizes measurable outcomes across environmental, social, and economic dimensions. The tone and design should remain simplified and results-focused.

 

Slide 12 – From Pilots to Nationwide Practice

Describes the transition from pilot initiatives to large-scale adoption, covering behavioral change, policy impact, and nationwide implementation. The sequence may be revisited for stronger narrative alignment.

 

Slide 13 – Transition to Call to Action

Acts as a bridge slide to prepare for partnership and sponsorship engagement.

 

Slide 14 – Partnership Opportunities

The section focuses on refining Ne’ma’s call to action, clarifying how external partners can engage and support the initiative. The aim is to communicate that Ne’ma’s objectives align with partners’ goals, creating a “help us help you” message that encourages collaboration.

There are two partnership tiers:

  • Ne’ma Initiative Partner ⭐⭐⭐– the top-tier partner supporting all pillars (behavioral studies, awareness, events, etc.) and gaining full exposure across Ne’ma’s initiatives.
  • Ne’ma Project Partner ⭐– supports specific projects (e.g., food rescue) and receives recognition only within that scope.

Currently, the distinction between these tiers is shown using three stars vs. one star, but the team suggests exploring clearer visual symbols or design language (e.g., gold/platinum-style differentiation) to make the hierarchy more intuitive.

The visual and textual framing needs enhancement to ensure clarity and appeal, especially since the deck might be shared independently (not always presented live). It should clearly communicate the value and structure of the partnerships without requiring extra explanation, helping potential partners or internal teams easily understand and act on it.

 

Slide 15 – Benefits of Partnership

Consistency & Terminology:

The titles are inconsistent across slides (e.g., Ne’ma Initiative Partner vs. Overall Initiative Partner). The team suggests unifying the naming and possibly finding stronger, more strategic terminology to distinguish the two tiers.

  • Benefits Section:
    This slide outlines exposure and acknowledgment opportunities, including access, branding, logo placement, media exposure, and recognition. The team wants to enhance both content and visuals to make the benefits more compelling and aligned with best practices from previous partnership proposals.
  • Strategic Positioning:
    The goal is to encourage partners to aim for the Initiative tier rather than the Project level. However, the team agreed the approach should be flexible:
    • For major partners, present only the initiative-level option to position it as the premium, default choice.
    • For smaller partners, show both tiers but visually emphasize the initiative tier (e.g., feature it prominently while presenting the project level as a smaller or footnote-style option).
  • Visual Storytelling & Editable Design:
    The client emphasized the power of visuals to guide perception — “maximize one, minimize the other.” They requested that the slides be editable so the internal team can adjust the emphasis depending on the audience.

Overall Objective:

To refine content, structure, and visuals so the partnership framework clearly communicates value, nudges larger partners toward initiative-level involvement, and tells a visually persuasive story that supports both live presentations and standalone document sharing.

 

Slide 16 – Measurable Impact from Support

Current Issue:

The slide feels dense and difficult to follow. While it aims to show partners the tangible outcomes of their investment (e.g., how much CO₂ is reduced, how many meals are provided, or the economic value saved), the heavy text and small visuals make it hard to grasp the key message at a glance.

  • Core Message:
    The intent is to clearly communicate that “your investment drives measurable impact.” For instance, investing 5 million AED in a program like Food Rescue generates direct and quantifiable social and environmental returns — not just financial sponsorship.
  • Simplification & Storytelling Approach:
    The team suggests cutting down the content and presenting it in a more storytelling, visual format, such as:
    • “Invest X → Create Y impact” style infographics.
    • Using icons, numbers, and visuals to highlight outcomes instead of paragraphs.
    • Making the impact feel immediate and tangible rather than data-heavy.
  • Balancing Detail & Readability:
    While simplification is important, certain audiences (especially mid-management or technical teams) will still want detailed ROI breakdowns.
    • Top management: wants a high-level, visual summary.
    • Operational teams: need the detailed figures and explanations.
  • Proposed Solution:
    Create a two-layer approach:
    1. Main Slide – concise, visually driven storytelling highlighting impact.
    2. Appendix Slides – detailed breakdowns and calculations for those who need to dive deeper.

This way, the presentation remains clear and engaging for decision-makers while still providing comprehensive data for supporting teams

 

Slide 17 – Partnership Targeting & Customization

This section focuses on refining the flow and tailoring the partner presentation strategy.

Currently, slides 16–19 all expand on the two partnership tiers (Initiative and Project), but the content feels repetitive. The goal is to streamline and structure the slides so that each has a clear, distinct purpose.

Key points:

  • The slide should clarify why partners should support Ne’ma — emphasizing their role, impact, and benefits as contributors to the national agenda.
  • The content for each partnership type (initiative vs. project) should be merged and simplified to create a more cohesive flow.
  • The approach will differ depending on the type of partner:
    • Big partners: Show only the initiative-level option — position it as the main opportunity.
    • Small partners: Show both options, but visually emphasize the initiative-level tier and place the project-level option as a smaller or secondary element.
    • Uncertain partners: Present both, but design the layout to subtly steer them toward the initiative tier through stronger visuals, positioning, and messaging.
  • The team suggested creating customizable versions for each audience type to adapt based on the partner’s potential.

 

Slide 18 – Project-Level Focus

This slide introduces the project-level partnership, answering the key question:

“What are your projects?”

Key points:

  • It should provide an overview of Ne’ma’s main programs (e.g., Food Rescue, Workshops, Educational Initiatives, Baseline Studies).
  • These can be summarized briefly on the main slide, while detailed project descriptions can be moved to the appendix for those who need deeper information.
  • The idea is to show potential project partners the range of engagement opportunities available without overloading the main presentation.

 

Slide 19 – Closing / Wrap-Up Slide

This is the final visual impression of the partnership section.

Key points:

  • The current slide uses repetitive visuals (mainly Food Rescue photos). It needs to better reflect the diversity of Ne’ma’s initiatives — showcasing a mix of activities such as awareness events, research, education, and policy work.
  • The team discussed possibly removing the photo altogether or redesigning the layout depending on what fits best visually.
  • The ending should feel polished, inclusive, and representative of Ne’ma’s broader impact — not limited to one project.

📁 Appendix (to follow)

Projects will be structured under pillars, each presented in a standardized format: project name, pillar, description, visuals, qualitative/quantitative impact, and monetary value if relevant. Two versions will be developed — one with numerical data and one without. Around 20 projects will be plugged into this template.

  • Purpose of Slides 22 & 23:
    These slides currently showcase Ne’ma’s five core pillars (e.g., Data & Metrics, Awareness & Behavioral Change) and the projects that fall under each. For instance, under Data & Metrics there are three projects, while Awareness & Behavioral Change includes campaigns, educational programs, community events, and advocacy work.
    The goal is to give a pillar-based overview while clarifying that projects are distinct from pillars — they are the actionable programs that fall beneath each theme.
  • Project Detailing (e.g., Slide 33 Example):
    When highlighting a specific project (like the Ne’ma Seven-Day Diary), the emphasis should shift from detailed numbers and achievements to purpose and impact — what the project aims to achieve, who it influences, and how it contributes to the national goal.
    • For instance, Food Rescue may show quantitative impact (e.g., meals saved, CO₂ reduced), while smaller initiatives like educational programs should focus more on qualitative outcomes (e.g., awareness, behavior change, influence).
    • The team agreed that detailed numbers belong in reports, not on the main slides.
  • Unified Project Template:
    There was strong alignment on creating a standardized “project card” template to be used across all future decks. Each project card would include:
    • Title & pillar it falls under
    • Brief description & objectives
    • Key impact statement (“why it matters”)
    • Optional space for visuals and monetary value
    • Versions with or without metrics, depending on audience type
      This approach helps partners easily navigate information and understand where their investment fits within Ne’ma’s ecosystem.
  • Appendix Use & Customization:
    The appendix will serve as a project library, holding detailed versions of the slides. Whether it’s shown or hidden depends entirely on the audience:
    • Initiative partners: won’t see the appendix — they fund the entire initiative, so only high-level impact matters.
    • Project partners: will receive the appendix, as it shows the specific programs they can choose to support.
      The client emphasized the need for editable, customizable files to adapt presentations depending on who they’re meeting.
  • Purpose of Showing Pillars:
    The team agreed that showing all pillars makes sense mainly when pitching to initiative-level partners, to demonstrate the breadth of Ne’ma’s work.
    For project-level partners, the focus should be narrowed down to the relevant projects under the pillar that aligns with their interests.

🎨 Deck Strategy & Flow

  • The presentation should speak for itself, with language and visuals conveying clarity and confidence.
  • Visual consistency and font hierarchy are essential to improve readability and highlight key messages.
  • If content reduction is needed, bold and impactful messages can remain in the main deck, while supporting details can move to the appendix.
  • Imagery should showcase the variety of initiatives and emphasize that ne’ma’s work goes beyond food rescue.

Visual Direction & Design Notes

Use of Pictures:

  • Strongly encouraged — “a picture is worth a thousand words.”
  • Photos help make the work more tangible and understandable.
  • The ne’ma team is open to integrating visuals naturally within the slide flow.
  • Media team can provide high-quality, relevant photos for use.

Design Approach:

  • Infographic-based, with clean data visualization and storytelling visuals.
  • Use of icons and clear illustrations is encouraged — especially to simplify text-heavy content.
  • Keep it neat, professional, and sleek — avoid “boring” corporate tones, but stay credible and polished.
  • Reflects a presidential initiative in look and feel.
  • Avoid animation (static slides preferred).

Brand Guidelines:

  • Logo and colors are non-negotiable — must adhere to ne’ma brand palette.
  • Open to new visual treatments, as long as they respect brand identity.
  • Prezlab’s past design work (impact report, one-pager, short deck) is the desired visual benchmark.

Typography:

  • Avoid custom fonts that require download (to ensure shareability across partners’ systems).
  • Use Montserrat for headlines and a clean, standard font (like Arial or Calibri) for body text.

📅 Plan & Milestones

Below are the agreed milestones:

 

1. Content Work Mais Tafesh Nour Karadsheh Nora Fitzgerald

Restructure the deck to ensure logical flow and coherence. Optimize the language and content to deliver the message clearly and align with ne’ma’s tone and brand voice.

2. Design Work Sara Shunnar Hala Alfayoumi

Develop a comprehensive design template that fits both the main slides and the 20 project slides. Once approved, proceed to design the full deck.

3. Translation & Finalization Hind Alnajjar

After approval, the deck will be translated into Arabic and finalized through slide flipping.

📌 Project Overview (Based on Scope & Prior Notes)

Deck Structure

  • One modular master deck (approx. 50–60 slides, per RFQ)
  • Restructuring and enhancement of “About ne’ma” and fundraising tiers
  • Inclusion of 20 project overview slides

Templatization

  • Flexible, editable templates aligned with the main storyline
  • Separate project overview templates with placeholders and charts

Bilingual Alignment

  • Deliverables in English and Arabic with localized adaptation

Final Deliverables

  • Ambition session
  • English fundraising deck
  • Arabic fundraising deck
  • Template adaptation toolkit

Current Deck Breakdown

  • Slides 1–2: Cover pages
  • Slides 3–12: About ne’ma
  • Slide 13: Call to Action cover
  • Slides 14–19: Fundraising tiers & matrix with benefits
  • +20 project slides from appendix

Additional Notes

  • The deck should remain modular and adaptable to different partner types.
  • Recommendations and enhancements will focus on clarity, tone, and visual flow.
  • Language will stay consistent with brand guidelines and bilingual requirements.

PROJECT BRIEF: AI Excellence Juror Infographic Motion Video

Client: ADMO (Abu Dhabi Modern Office)

Collaborating Entity: ADEO (Abu Dhabi Executive Office)

Deadline: Wednesday, 12 November 2025

Reference Video: YouTube link

🎯 Objective

To create an engaging, infographic-style motion video that explains the AI Excellence Juror — what it is, how it works, and how it’s transforming the Abu Dhabi Award for Excellence in Government Performance (ADAEP).

The goal is to make the content accessible and engaging for both government and public audiences, highlighting Abu Dhabi’s global leadership in innovation, transparency, and data-driven governance.

🧩 Context

  • The video is part of the 2025 ADAEP awareness and storytelling campaign led by ADMO.
  • ADEO has provided draft written content that must be reviewed and approved before proceeding to visual or animation stages.
  • The AI Excellence Juror is an in-house Abu Dhabi innovation designed to enhance jury deliberations using AI-generated insights while maintaining human judgment.
  • It will be presented as one of the key innovations of the 2025 cycle, positioning Abu Dhabi as the first government globally to operationalize such tools in award governance.

📊 Key Facts to Highlight

CategoryFigures / Details
Assessment Team90 total — 13 UAE, 77 international
Countries Represented27
Excellence Ambassadors74 for 37 entities
Government Entities37 participating
Projects Submitted104
Award Timeline 2025May – Initiation
June–July – Building Capability
August – Entity Applications
October – Assessment Begins
November – Assessment Ends

These statistics can be visualized dynamically throughout the video to add credibility and visual rhythm.

🧠 Content Outline (suggested)

  1. Opening / Context
    • Introduce the Abu Dhabi Award for Excellence in Government Performance (ADAEP) and its purpose.
    • Briefly highlight the scale of participation and the global diversity of the assessors.
    • Lead into the need for precision, transparency, and fairness in large-scale assessments.
  2. What Is the AI Excellence Juror?
    • A government-developed AI tool supporting jurors with data-driven insights.
    • Emphasize that it does not replace human judgment, but strengthens it with analytical depth and consistency.
  3. How It Works
    • Analyzes assessment reports, performance data, and feedback.
    • Maps information against the Abu Dhabi Government Excellence and Performance Model (Drivers, Accelerators, Outcomes).
    • Generates insights and recommendations for more objective evaluation.
  4. Impact
    • Enhances transparency, consistency, and fairness in evaluation.
    • Empowers jurors to make confident, evidence-based decisions.
    • Positions Abu Dhabi as a global pioneer in AI governance excellence.
  5. Timeline & Integration
    • Visualize the 2025 cycle, showing where the AI Juror comes into play (during October–November assessment phase).
    • Reinforce the message of continuous improvement and digital transformation.
  6. Closing Message
    • “A smarter way to evaluate excellence — powered by AI, guided by insight, and led by Abu Dhabi.”

🎨 Stylistic Direction

  • Tone: Professional yet accessible — informative, modern, and optimistic.
  • Style: Infographic explainer motion video — clean transitions, dynamic typography, iconography, and data visualization.
  • Visual Identity:
    • Follow ADAEP and ADMO’s official color palette and minimal geometric design system.
    • Avoid overly cinematic or documentary tones (the client’s reference was for clarity and narration style, not visual complexity).
    • Integrate AI-inspired motion cues — data waves, analysis lines, and glowing connection nodes — to visualize intelligence and insight. (confirm details of it with client)
    • No VO needed

      👥 Roles & Responsibilities
RoleResponsibility

Content 

Prezlab content team – finalize content draft, to be reviewed by ADEO before storyboarding
Project ManagerCoordinate approvals, track deadlines, and manage review loop with ADMO/ADEO
Storyboard DesignerTo be assigned by CS team — visualize narrative flow and motion direction
Animator (Leen)Execute motion graphics and transitions — may require overtime allocation for timely delivery
Reviewer (Client)ADMO + ADEO – review and approve content before animation phase

🗓 Timeline

TaskOwnerDeadline
Content draft for client reviewContent team

 

Client feedback (ADEO review)ADMO/ADEO

 

Storyboard developmentDesign team

 

Animation & final deliveryLeenBy 12 Nov (Wednesday)

✅ Expected Deliverables

  • 1 infographic motion video (approx. 1 min) > 1 version in arabic and 1 in english
  • Storyboard (bilingual-ready)
  • Final output in HD with clean graphic treatment, ADAEP branding, and accessible tone

designers.name: Tanya M. Amin Abdal Qader Shteawi

rows.description:
Design Brief – Expats Guidebook (Saudi Arabia)


Project Objective

Design a comprehensive, visually compelling expats guidebook that presents Saudi Arabia as a modern, ambitious, and welcoming destination for global talent, while maintaining credibility, clarity, and cultural respect.

The guidebook should balance authority and warmth, positioning the Kingdom as a place of opportunity, growth, and high quality of life.

Design Principles

  • Savvy-first: All design must strictly adhere to Savvy’s brand guidelines (typography, color usage, layout logic, hierarchy, and tone).
  • Saudi-centric storytelling: Imagery and visual language should be deeply rooted in Saudi Arabia—its people, cities, landscapes, culture, and transformation.
  • Modern & aspirational: Reflect progress, Vision 2030 momentum, and global competitiveness.
  • Human & relatable: While informative, the guide should feel welcoming and accessible to expats and their families.
  • Clarity over decoration: Information-heavy sections must remain easy to navigate, scan, and digest.

Overall Look & Feel

  • Tone: Confident, progressive, welcoming, and credible.
  • Mood: Forward-looking, optimistic, grounded in heritage yet clearly modern.
  • Visual balance:
    • Leadership & Vision sections → more formal, iconic, authoritative
    • Lifestyle, culture, and living sections → warmer, human, experiential

Imagery Direction

Primary rule: Imagery must be Saudi-focused and authentic, not generic expat stock.

Preferred Imagery

  • Real Saudi environments: Riyadh, Jeddah, Eastern Province, heritage sites, giga-project visuals
  • People-focused imagery:
    • Diverse professionals (Saudi and expat)
    • Women in leadership and professional settings
    • Families, social life, cafés, cultural events
  • Lifestyle visuals:
    • Cafés, malls, art spaces, concerts, parks
    • Nature escapes, deserts, wadis, outdoor activities
  • Urban transformation:
    • Modern architecture, skylines, infrastructure, transport
    • Vision 2030 projects (tastefully, not over-promotional)

Avoid

  • Overused desert clichés
  • Non-Saudi stock photography
  • Overly staged or corporate-looking visuals
  • Political or religious symbolism used excessively or without context

Hi team,

Kindly enhance the look and feel of the 41 slides and make them more visual and engaging.

Many thanks!

rows.description:
DET Destination Presentation Revamp 

Context/ Background: 

In 2022, PrezLab designed DET’s destination presentation, the key tool used by DET’s ambassadors and sales representatives internationally to present and sell Dubai to travel agents and tourism agencies. 

The key objectives of the presentation at the time were to: 

  • Have a visually appealing and on-brand deck to represent Dubai in the best way possible 
  • Have a high degree of editability and navigability for users to modularly plug in and out content as needed 

Current Challenge/ Requirements: 

DET has decided that it is time to revamp the presentation for a few reasons: 

  • Here’s the current version (double check with the client as this may have changed) 
  • They felt the design may be simple and outdated, and could use a refresh to be more modern/ “fresh” 
  • Content enrichment/ edits: There were several content edits/ changes/ and additions coming in from several departments (latest received sheet here, but check again for updates) 
  • Content optimization: 
  • We could look at potentially optimizing the content of some slides like the destination template slides, text-heavy slides, etc… 
  • There might be some redundancy solved by potentially merging slides 
  • What we are NOT doing is a structural or storyline overhaul, the client is HAPPY with the current flow/ sectioning 
  • People weren’t necessarily aware of the best way to use/ edit the presentation. For example, some users were surprised that there were navigation elements on some of the menu slides and editability may be a struggle 
  • Random note from the client that may overlap with the comments in the sheet: 

Attractions. 

List of all new attractions in Dubai (I started to work on the list, kindly find attached) 

Free Attractions for budget-conscious travellers. 

Grouping similar attractions on a single slide to streamline the content. However, we could also have separate slides for each attraction. For example, Immersive Theme Parks (Aya Universe, House of Hype, Arte Museum, Immerse, TODA) could be grouped together, or each could have its own slide. 

New Personas: Senior Citizens, Stopover Travelers, Muslim Travelers, eco-friendly travellers 

New Locations, J1 Beach, West Palm, Expo City, and etc. 

More slides on Public Transportation and getting around Dubai easily using metro, buses, and taxis.        

Slides on gastronomy and wellness ( we will provide context , need to work on visual part only)      

  • There’s a list of other presentations (that are eseentialy slides extracted from the original presentation) that need to be revamped. Not too many hours were added for this exercise as the slides are essentially the same as in the OG deck: 

Hotel Groups Branded  

Hotel Groups Unbranded 

Travel Trade Branded 

Travel Trade Unbranded  

  • There may be a need to create a version of the presentation without Dubai’s logo, but no need for it to have a different design concept (double check this point but be mindful that we scoped for only one design concept) 

Solutions/ Scope of work: 

 

*I wrote in the email that we can accommodate up to 180 slides but didn’t mention it in the proposal! This can be reiterated in the kick off call 

Client proposed timelines (negotiable, but end of August is realistic for the whole thing) 

These timelines and milestones can be clarified or shifted around as long as we’re keeping the end deadline intact 

Deliverables 

Milestone Date 

Look & Feel of the presentation slides design 

24th June 

Content Draft (optimization + enrichment) 

Won't be fully designed, as we'd need approval/ comments on the content before polishing it. But we'll make sure it looks neat/ clean enough for stakeholder review. 

18th July 

Fully Designed Draft Presentation Slides 

The slides will be fully designed/ polished following the content approval in the previous milestone 

11th August 

Final Presentation Slides Design 

By 31st August 

Next Steps: 

  • Aspiration setting session with client 
  • Finalizing milestone and timelines 

Chances for upsell: 

  • Translation and adaptation 
  • Interactive learning experience for users on how to best present Dubai (min-guide)

Approved script here: https://prezlab.sharepoint.com/sites/ClientDatabase5.0/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FClientDatabase5%2E0%2FShared%20Documents%2FMawhiba%20Org%2FMWH%2DT1%20Achievements%20Video%2FDeliverables%2FApproved%20Script&viewid=87324605%2D829b%2D4ace%2D8d35%2D27d454daedda

Brief:

الهدف

تذكير مجلس الإدارة بالأثر العميق والممتد الذي حققته «موهبة» منذ تأسيسها، وبناء شعور بالفخر، الثقة، والمسؤولية تجاه استمرارية هذا الأثر.

الجمهور المستهدف

  • مجلس الإدارة 

النبرة

  • عاطفية
  • غير تسويقية
  • ملهمة مع الحفاظ على الطابع المؤسسي

تسلسل الستوريبورد (Visual Narrative)

1. الجذور والرؤية 

  • استخدام فيديو المؤسس كلقطة محورية.
  • حركة بصرية هادئة، انتقالات ناعمة.
  • ألوان محايدة مائلة للدفء.
    الإحساس: الهيبة، الأصالة، العمق.

2. التحول من الرؤية إلى الفعل

  • انتقال بصري من الأرشيف إلى مشاهد واقعية للبرامج والأنشطة.
  • تفاصيل إنسانية قريبة (وجوه، أيادٍ، تركيز).
  • إيقاع بصري يبدأ بالتصاعد.
    الإحساس: الالتزام، الانطلاق، الجدية.

3. الإنجازات الملموسة

  • مشاهد متنوعة للطلاب، المنافسات، المختبرات، العمل الجماعي.
  • دمج الإنجازات بصريًا (أرقام، سنوات، محطات) بطريقة أنيقة وغير مباشرة.
  • إيقاع أسرع نسبيًا مع تنوع في الزوايا.
    الإحساس: الفخر، المصداقية، الثقة.

4. الأثر بعيد المدى

  • ربط الأفراد بالصورة الأكبر (مستقبل، ابتكار، معرفة).
  • لقطات أوسع، حركة للأمام، ضوء أقوى.
  • حضور رمزي للوطن والمستقبل.
    الإحساس: الاستمرارية، المسؤولية، الرؤية الاستراتيجية.

5. الخاتمة

  • تهدئة الإيقاع البصري.
  • عودة لعنصر بصري قوي (المؤسس / رمز وطني / لقطة إنسانية مؤثرة).
  • نهاية نظيفة وواثقة بشعار «موهبة».
    الإحساس: الاكتمال، الإلهام، الاستعداد للمرحلة التالية.

التوجيه البصري

  • أسلوب سينمائي بسيط وغير متكلف
  • اعتماد الإضاءة الطبيعية قدر الإمكان
  • انتقال تدريجي من لقطات قريبة إلى لقطات واسعة
  • تطور لوني من هادئ إلى أكثر دفئًا مع تقدم القصة

الإيقاع

  • بداية بطيئة → تصاعد مدروس → نهاية هادئة
  • كل مشهد يخدم التالي بدون قطع حاد
Quick fix, making them more visual by adding images, icons, and light visual elements. No major changes

rows.description:
Hi team, 

Our main objective is to transform the current Word document into a high-level "Knowledge Paper" that effectively communicates ADFD’s impact on global climate and sustainability. We will be focusing on a clean, modern design that balances technical depth with a visually engaging layout suitable for both digital and print audiences.

You can find Ne'ma reference : ADFD Knowledge Paper Report Design - OneDrive

 Another reference to consider from the client: Enmaeya

Also, to ensure we are fully aligned on the creative direction, I’ve summarized our discussion and the next steps below:

Project Overview & Objectives

  • Focus: An impactful report highlighting ADFD's role in financing climate initiatives that promote renewable energy, resilience, and sustainable development in developing countries.
  • Target Audience: Decision-makers, academics, leadership, institutions, and anyone who's interested to know more about ADFD.
  • Format: A professional InDesign report optimized for both digital and print use.

Design Approach

  • Visual Style: Eye-catching and attractive, yet clean and professional (avoiding an overly colorful look).
  • Imagery: A focus on "Human-Centric" and positive imagery that demonstrates success, strictly avoiding any depictions of poverty.
  • Elements: Use of futuristic illustrations (e.g., modern farming) and high-quality, custom infographics.
  • Branding and fonts: We have the flexibility to explore design elements and fonts beyond the standard guidelines to best suit the report’s narrative.
  • Layout & Size: Standard A4 size, with the flexibility to reorganize sections or split pages to ensure the best possible reading experience.
  • Visualizations: We will recreate maps and key infographics to better visualize the data provided in the text.
  • QR Code: We can generate this directly within InDesign for the necessary section. We’ll look into the best placement and way to add it with no expiry date, but please let us know if you have a specific link you'd like us to use.

Timeline & Milestones

  • Design Concept Delivery: Thursday, Dec 25th (6:00 PM UAE)
  • Feedback/Approval Due: We need to receive your approval/feedback on the concept by Sunday, Dec 28th – Monday, Dec 29th
  • Final Deliverable: Thursday, Jan 8th (6:00 PM UAE)

Initial brief received from BD: 

Project: ADFD Knowledge Paper Report Design
Purpose & Timeline: Design pages and visualize data/infographics in the report using ADFD Branding, Client wants to start ASAP as they need it by first Week of Jan ( They need ready to use before the World Government Summit) - Let's aim for Jan 5th please
Audience: External 
Number of Pages: 24 
Content Status: Ready from Client Side
Language: English
Output Format: Digital and Print
Preferences: Client liked and wants something similar to Ne'ma Report Look and Feel

rows.description:
Hi team,

we previously worked on the EXPO 2030 presentation, which was a highly immersive request and very well received. Based on that success, Accenture has asked us to support two key deliverables for this engagement.

Deliverables Overview

1. Proposal Deck (Primary Focus)

  • Format: Static PDF
  • Scale: ~200–300 unique slides (details below)
  • Purpose: Support an 18-month digital transformation engagement
  • Key requirement: Design execution and visual uplift only (no concept creation, no animation)

2. Oral Presentation Deck (Secondary / Phase 2)

  • Built from the proposal content
  • Uses the same look and feel
  • Enhanced for pitch format with light animations (similar in spirit to Tasmu)
  • Developed toward the end of January, in parallel once proposal work is underway
  • More details to be shared later

Deliverable 1: Proposal Deck — Design Brief

Project Overview

This is a large-scale proposal requiring expert design support to handle volume, density, and complexity.

The goal is to:

  • Elevate the visual look and feel
  • Improve readability and structural clarity
  • Reduce visual noise through layout restructuring
  • Create and apply consistent templates across a very large document

Important:

  • No animations
  • Focus is on design execution, not new concept for the slides

Scope of Work

  • Content is largely available (with some phases still pending: 1, 5, 4.2, 4.3)
  • Estimated total slides: ~200–300

Not all slides are unique:

  • ~100 CV slides → one reusable template
  • ~50 credentials slides → one reusable template
  • Many slides are table-based
  • Several slides are repetitive and will be standardized via templates

Target Audience

  • CDO and leadership team
  • Senior digital and technology leadership
  • Other internal stakeholders (e.g. Strategy, MarCom)

This is a senior, time-constrained audience reviewing a very large document — clarity, hierarchy, and ease of reading are critical.

 

Design Direction & Principles

Design Direction

  • Defined and led by Accenture Song (They are the design department within Accenture)
  • Built on an existing concept previously developed with Prezlab (EXPO 2030)

Branding

  • Prezlab (EXPO 2030)

Theme & Style

  • Dark theme
  • Minimalistic approach
  • Reduced visual noise
  • Cleaner layouts
  • Smaller text and logos compared to previous versions
  • Simplified layouts to make dense content easier to read

Layout Rules

  • Title + strapline:
    • Fixed position
    • One line
    • Same size and placement across all slides
  • Use subtle tonal variation (dark / lighter treatments) to distinguish:
    • Activity charters
    • Filler / supporting slides

Timeline & Delivery

  • Proposal completion target: 10 January
  • Proposal submission: 15 January
  • Oral presentation:
    • Developed in parallel
    • Expected toward the end of January

Delivery will happen in multiple drops, not a single final handover.

 

Roles & Responsibilities

Accenture Song

  • Defines and locks the design direction

Prezlab

  • Executes the visual uplift
  • Builds and applies templates
  • Improves layout, hierarchy, and consistency

Accenture

  • Provides content
  • Reviews and aligns internally

rows.description:
Internal Brief: Deck Content Optimization and Design

Objective:

To optimize the content and design of the deck for a more engaging, streamlined, and visually-driven presentation. The goal is to maintain the essence of the original message but ensure the content is concise, more digestible, and aligned with visual storytelling techniques. This deck is intended for high-level chairmen, private sector partners, and media partners, so it should strike a balance between professionalism and dynamic, visually appealing design.

Content Optimization:

Key Principles:

  1. Flow & Structure:
    • Ensure the content is organized logically and smoothly, allowing the narrative to progress naturally.
    • Transition between sections should feel seamless, guiding the audience through the key messages without overwhelming them with dense paragraphs or overly technical jargon.
  2. Wordiness Reduction:
    • Remove repetitive phrases, unnecessary details, and overly complex sentences.
    • Streamline the text to make it more concise without losing the core message.
  3. Visual Storytelling Focus:
    • Convert key points into data visualizations or infographics wherever applicable.
    • Focus on turning text-heavy points into succinct visuals or engaging charts.
    • Use powerful, minimalistic language that complements the visuals and enhances understanding at a glance.
  4. Target Audience Alignment:
    • Ensure that the tone and language are suitable for the high-level stakeholders. The content should be professional but accessible, clear, and impactful for chairmen, private sector partners, and media partners.
  5. Preserve Core Messaging:
    • Avoid major changes to the verbal content unless necessary to fit the design flow, keeping the integrity of the message intact.
    • Ensure that the key takeaways and action points are highlighted and easy to grasp.

Design Guidelines:

  1. Approved Design Concept:
    • Follow the design concept that will be shared with the content strategist and designer. This concept will guide the color scheme, typography, layout, and overall visual tone of the deck.
  2. Visual Storytelling:
    • Use high-quality visuals, including imagery, icons, and custom illustrations, to break up dense sections of text.
    • Focus on creating a strong visual hierarchy, guiding the viewer’s attention to the most important points.
    • Infographics, charts, and data visualizations should be clean, impactful, and easily understood at a glance.
  3. Minimalism & Elegance:
    • The design should be sleek and elegant, with plenty of white space to avoid visual clutter.
    • Use subtle animations and transitions to enhance the storytelling experience, without detracting from the core content.
  4. Consistency:
    • Maintain consistency across all slides in terms of layout, font usage, and visual elements to ensure cohesiveness throughout the deck.
  5. Brand Alignment:
    • Ensure the design aligns with the company's branding guidelines, ensuring all visual elements are consistent with brand colors, fonts, and logo usage.

Next Steps:

  1. Content Review:
    • The content strategist will make necessary revisions and optimizations to the content based on the guidelines above.
  2. Design Integration:
    • The designer will incorporate the approved visual elements into the slides based on the content adjustments, ensuring the design is aligned with the approved concept.
  3. Review & Final Adjustments:
    • Once the content and design are aligned, a final review will be conducted with the team to ensure it meets the objectives and is ready for presentation to stakeholders.

Timeline:

  • Content Optimization: Wednesday 21st/ Thursday 22nd
  • Design Finalization: Tuesday 27th
  • Final Review: Wednesday 28th

1. Objective

Al Bawani Holding is seeking Prezlab’s support in developing a standardized company profile presentation that can be:

  • Used across multiple sectors (defense, healthcare, real estate, oil & gas, etc.).
  • Adaptable by their BD teams for various client meetings.
  • Easily updatable internally as new projects and achievements are added.

2. Client Needs & Scope

RequirementDescription
A. Main Holding Deck (Master Profile)A standard company presentation for Al Bawani Holding that gives an overall overview — mission, structure, subsidiaries, capabilities, major projects, and differentiation. Expected to be ~20 slides.
B. Subsidiary Decks9 subsidiaries (potentially expanding to 11). Each will have a standardized presentation template that can be regularly updated internally. Includes overview slide(s) and project detail slides.
C. Project Slide TemplateA modular slide format for individual project overviews (e.g., client, scope, sector, location, value, completion status, visuals). Enables the BD team to plug and play for different audiences.
D. Data Updating MechanismA method or system (e.g., Excel database) for internal teams to input project details, which Prezlab can later translate into slide updates or visuals.
E. Content OptimizationGuidance on structure, hierarchy, and key messaging for each subsidiary (what to include, what to highlight).
F. Bilingual OutputFinal deliverables first in English, followed by Arabic translation once English is approved.

3. Context & Insights

  • Al Bawani currently has no unified standard presentation — content is scattered and inconsistent across subsidiaries.
  • The Holding company includes diverse subsidiaries across construction, capital, water and power, pipeline, MEP, facilities management, security, technology, etc.
  • End-to-end capability is a key differentiator — from financing (Capital), to contracting (Construction), to operations (Facilities/Services).
  • Recently 30% acquired by PIF, signaling growth and diversification — an important corporate milestone to highlight.
  • The client is undergoing a major rebranding exercise, but needs this presentation immediately, before brand updates are finalized (brand refresh expected by March–April).
  • Existing logos and visuals are temporary; design should be flexible to adapt to future brand changes.

4. Pain Points

  • No centralized visual identity or presentation template.
  • No system to update project information (project wins, completions, delays).
  • Difficulty maintaining consistency across subsidiaries.
  • Lack of time and design capabilities internally.

5. Deliverable Structure (Proposed)

Phase 1 — Core Holding Profile

  • Company overview, key milestones, leadership, group structure.
  • High-level overview of subsidiaries (1 slide per subsidiary summary).
  • Signature projects and differentiators.
  • Key stats (employees, revenue, geographies, etc.).

Phase 2 — Subsidiary Templates

  • Individual presentation per subsidiary with:
    • Overview (what they do, sectors, capabilities).
    • 4–5 project slides (modular template).
    • Optional: clients served / awards / certifications.

Phase 3 — Project Database (Optional)

  • Excel template with key fields: Project Name, Sector, Client, Scope, Value, Completion Status, Visual Link, Description, etc.
  • Can be linked for periodic updates.

6. Timeline & Priorities

  • Immediate priority: Main Holding presentation (Phase 1).
  • Parallel work: Begin data collection for subsidiaries through Excel.
  • Future phases: Top 2–3 subsidiaries after Holding profile completion.

7. Language

  • Start with English version (locked first).
  • Arabic version to follow for the Holding deck, potentially for subsidiaries later.

 

8. Key Takeaways

  • This is a strategic account with long-term potential (ongoing updates, future rebranding alignment).
  • The client values structure, scalability, and ease of update over aesthetics alone.
  • The project requires design, content structuring, and process thinking — not just visual redesign.

HLU Impact & EoY Reports – Kick-Off Call Recap

Project Overview

We are supporting the Healthy Living Unit (HLU) under Abu Dhabi’s Department of Health in developing two key communication outputs:

  1. End-of-Year Report  – A comprehensive status update covering initiatives, achievements, partnerships, governance, and projected impact.
  2. Impact Report  – Public-facing, narrative-led, inspirational report focused on human stories, change, and international positioning.

HLU will provide content; our role is to refine, copy-edit, design, and create compelling visual storytelling.

Context

  • HLU was established in 2025 and is still in early stages of public visibility.
  • Many initiatives are newly operational; no formal impact metrics yet.
  • Communication efforts so far have been G2G, with a strong emphasis on developing the overarching Healthy Living Strategy (“North Star”).
  • The EoY Report will serve as the first comprehensive outward-facing summary of HLU’s identity, work, and collaborations.

Objectives

  1. Raise awareness of HLU as a central orchestrator of healthy living initiatives in Abu Dhabi.
  2. Highlight 2025 achievements and outline strategic direction for 2026.
  3. Inspire stakeholders (government + public) with an emotionally engaging narrative that motivates collective action.

Narrative & Tone Alignment

  • HLU wants the reports to be inspirational, ambitious, and emotionally resonant, but balanced with clarity and G2G professionalism.
  • Previous attempts at metaphorical storytelling (e.g., “New Dawn”) weren’t fully aligned.
  • Agreed: We must come to the next meeting with a clear, justified tone-of-voice recommendation, not an open-ended proposal.
  • A dedicated tone & narrative workshop with Dr. Ahmad will be scheduled mid-December.

Content Readiness

  • Claudia is consolidating initiative-level content into a master document (draft exists; pending Dr. Ahmad’s review).
  • Additional initiative updates will be shared (nutrition, physical activity, Dagayeg community program, policy work in schools, food marketing regulations, etc.).
  • HLU’s work spans complex multi-sectoral partnerships (DOH, DMT, private sector).
  • The food & nutrition vision (2025–2030) includes multiple layers of policy, compliance, and community programs.

International Positioning

  • Impact Report will be leveraged internationally, especially around Abu Dhabi Health Week.
  • Key addition: a “Lessons Learned” section to support global knowledge exchange on multi-sectoral approaches to healthy living.

Branding & Visuals

  • HLU Brand Guidelines are in final stages (visual identity already defined; color palettes to follow within ~1 month).
  • These guidelines will inform the design direction for both reports.

Timeline

  • No confirmed rollout date yet; clarity expected after meeting with Dr. Ahmad.
  • End of January is considered a realistic target for delivery.

Next Steps

  1. Receive the consolidated content draft + additional initiative materials.
  2. Internally develop tone-of-voice and narrative direction to present to Dr. Ahmad.
  3. Schedule the mid-December alignment workshop once Dr. Ahmad returns.
  4. Await final brand guidelines to begin design alignment.

    ------------------------

HLU Project Plan — Revised (EoY Report Only)

Start: 11 December — End: 31 January

Deliverable:

End-of-Year Report (English + Arabic)

PHASE 0 — Content Collation, Research & Synthesis (Pre-Ambition Prep)

Early December → 17 December (including weekend gap)

Objective

Build a strong foundation for the Ambition Session by collecting content, researching benchmarks, filling knowledge gaps, and drafting initial narrative directions.

Tasks

A. Content Collation (HLU Materials)

  • Collect all strategies, initiative decks, contributions
  • Map content per initiative into a structured inventory
  • Identify information gaps & questions for the ambition session
  • Begin drafting preliminary structure & narrative hypotheses

B. Research (Global Benchmarks & Best Practices)

 

Design team + Editorial team run parallel research streams:

Design Team Research:

  • Review 5–7 global government health-related reports:
    • WHO
    • Singapore MOH
    • Public Health England
    • Saudi Public Health Authority
    • Australian Health Promotion reports
  • Extract:
    • Visual systems
    • Data visualization styles
    • Layouts
    • Use of photography vs. illustration
    • Tone & narrative presentation
  • Compile a "visual best practices" section for the workshop deck

Editorial Team Research:

  • Assess global reports for:
    • Tone of voice
    • Writing style
    • Narrative arc
    • Executive summary structure
    • Policy framing
    • Behavioral insights communication
  • Produce a 1–2 page “narrative & writing best practices” sheet
  • Draft narrative options for HLU based on observed benchmarks

C. Synthesis

  • Merge content mapping + research insights
  • Draft:
    • Preliminary structure
    • Narrative directions
    • Mood inspirations
    • Tone options
    • Key story pillars

Outputs (Delivered on 17 December)

  • Full workshop deck for 18 Dec including:
    • Narrative directions
    • Tone of voice options
    • Draft structure
    • Visual moodboard & benchmark references
    • Best practices (writing, layout, data viz)
    • Questions for alignment with Dr. Ahmad
  • Clear knowledge gaps for the ambition session

PHASE 1 — Ambition Session

18 December

Objective

Lock the strategic direction before concept and editorial development begin.

Workshop Outputs

  • Final narrative direction
  • Tone of voice
  • Story pillars
  • Report structure
  • Design mood alignment
  • Approval of direction & next steps

PHASE 2 — Concept Creation (Editorial + Design)

19, 22 → 24 December

Objective

Translate the ambition direction into a full editorial blueprint + initial visual concept.

Tasks

  • Write full English narrative (based on approved narrative direction)
  • Develop executive summaries
  • Convert initiatives into final structured stories
  • Draft data/impact visualization outlines
  • Create initial design concept:
    • Cover exploration
    • Layout principles
    • Infographic style
    • Imagery style
  • Build 1–2 example designed spreads

Outputs — By 27 December

  • Full editorial draft (English)
  • Concept deck
  • Structure locked
  • Content ready for full design phase
  • Approval checkpoint

PHASE 3 — Design Phase

28 December → 31 January

(Final date can be broken into checkpoints if needed)

Objective

Develop the full End-of-Year Report in English and Arabic.

Tasks

Week 1:

  • Full layout v1 in English

Week 2:

  • Present v1
  • Incorporate feedback → v2
  • Continue Arabic layout

Week 3:

  • Final English layout
  • Final Arabic layout
  • QA + alignment with brand
  • Prepare packaged files

Week 4 (Buffer):

  • Final approvals
  • Delivery of all assets

Final Delivery

31 January – English + Arabic EoY Report Delivered

Revised Timeline Summary

Dec

  • Early December -17: Phase 0 – Content + Research + Synthesis
  • 18: Ambition Session
  • 19, 22–24: Phase 2 – Concept Creation
  • 28 → Jan 31: Phase 3 – Design

rows.description:
1. Business Challenge

KAUST is establishing its first Impact Office and needs a flagship annual Impact Report that credibly demonstrates real-world impact to high-stakes stakeholders.

  • The report is board-requested and high-stakes, reviewed by ministers and senior leadership, with visibility up to the highest level.
  • It must prove impact, not market achievements.
  • It serves multiple purposes:
    • Evidence and advocacy for the Board
    • Public transparency and leadership positioning
    • A baseline framework for future impact measurement
  • The report is intended to set a regional benchmark, as KAUST positions itself as the first university in the Middle East with a dedicated impact function and published impact report.

2. Current State

The report content is largely complete, but the current design fails to carry the weight of the message.

  • ~120–130 pages, 9 chapters, long-form narratives intentionally approved by leadership.
  • Content and impact framework already defined:
    • Inputs → Outputs → Outcomes → Impact
    • Each chapter includes:
      • Impact summary table
      • Timeline of milestones
      • Capitals table (human, financial, social, cultural, etc.)
  • Existing design (from comms):
    • Very text-heavy
    • Generic infographics
    • Predominantly photo-based imagery that does not add meaning
    • Limited visual hierarchy and weak emphasis on key insights
  • Editable InDesign files are available.
  • Translation to Arabic is in progress (multi-pass review, high quality standards).

rows.description:
Hey team, we received a new project from Richemont

 

Project Brief

 

Richemont Procurement – 3-Year Reframing Piece

1. Background & Context

  • The fiscal year ends in March.
  • When she joined Richemont 3 years ago, there was no Procurement department. She was tasked with building it from the ground up.
  • She developed and implemented a 3-year strategy program, which is now coming to an end.
  • The department is currently at a pause moment — closing one strategic chapter and preparing for the next 3-year strategy.

This piece will mark that transition.

2. Objective

This is:

  • Not a report
  • Not a recap
  • Not a summary

It is a:

  • Reframing piece
  • A strategic tool that gives credibility
  • A piece that creates HUNGER for what comes next

In essence, this is:

Storytelling + Proof + Elevation

It should:

  • Recap the transformation journey of the past 3 years
  • Demonstrate impact without bragging
  • Elevate the perception of Procurement
  • Build excitement for the next 3-year strategy
  • Position Procurement as a strategic function

3. Audience

Primary audience:

  • High-level management (shared via email)

Secondary audience:

  • Broader internal stakeholders

Tone of voice:

  • Strategic
  • Confident but not boastful
  • Credible
  • Inspirational
  • Elevated
  • Story-driven

4. Format & Structure

Format

  • PDF format
  • Editorial feel
  • Maximum 3 pages
  • Can be read in 5 minutes
  • Designed for impact review
  • Not linear in structure
  • Visualized in columns (newsletter-style layout), but not referred to as a newsletter
  • Images are illustrative only (can be small, with link to high-resolution if needed)

Important:

  • “Pages” do not equal “years”
  • We don’t have to strictly balance content per page
    (e.g., 1.5 pages for Impact and less for Shift is acceptable)
  • Page 1 must be strong and concise to avoid being skipped

5. Content Structure (Client Direction – To Keep As Is)

Page 1: The Transformation

From fragmentation to a regional standard

Focus:

  • The journey
  • The setup of the function
  • The evolution from non-existence to structured regional capability

Page 2: The Impact

From intent to capability

The outcomes / achievements

Focus:

  • What was built
  • What changed
  • Measurable and qualitative impact
  • Proof points

Page 3: The Shift

What this changed

Focus:

  • The mindset shift
  • Organizational shift
  • Procurement’s new positioning
  • Elegantly introduces the next 3-year strategy plan

6. Design Direction

  • Must feel distinctly Richemont
  • Elegant, elevated, refined
  • Not a template
  • Custom design
  • However, flexible enough to be reused as a base if needed in the future
  • Must feel owned by Procurement
  • Should NOT feel like an external agency product

No content creation required — all content has been drafted by the client.

Scope is format structuring, concept creation, and design.

7. Key Success Criteria

  • Strong storytelling without exaggeration
  • Clear strategic credibility
  • Executive-friendly and digestible
  • Visually elevated yet minimal
  • Leaves leadership curious and engaged about the next chapter

8. Milestones & Timeline

  1. Format Structuring
    Define layout logic and editorial structure
  2. Concept Creation – 2 Days
    Visual direction and design concept
  3. Full Design – 3–4 Days
    Final design and production after concept approval

Here's the initial brief received, and attached you can find the document. 

 

Dana will be PMing the project and it will be under Dina's design pool

Procurement in MEIAT - a 3 years capability build VF.docx

Hello team, 

We got a new project from ne'ma

Project Objective

To produce a visually engaging, strategically structured, and content-rich bilingual impact report (English and Arabic) that:

o Communicates ne’ma’s progress, impact, and national relevance.

o Enhances the visibility and understanding of ne’ma’s systemic approach to food loss and waste.

o Inspires cross-sector stakeholders and the public through data-driven storytelling, human stories, and design excellence.

The report should build upon and evolve the 2022–2023 Impact Report structure, tone, and visual language (see attached), while reflecting the growth of ne’ma in scope, delivery, and ecosystem engagement during this phase.

 

Project Overview

o Language – English and Arabic - two Impact Reports ( English and Arabic) encased in a custom packaging.

o Audience – UAE leadership, federal and local government stakeholders, partners, general public.

 

Strategic Content Development & Communications Direction

o Strategic content structure, narrative framing, and messaging hierarchy.

o Enhancement and rewriting of existing content for impact, clarity, and strategic alignment.

o Development of new content sections where required (e.g., case studies, quotes, vision forward, testimonials).

o Integration of storytelling elements and thematic chapter framing.

o Development of a high-level communications strategy for the report – positioning, messaging tone, visual language, and key takeaways.

o Internal content outline or narrative map to be co-created with ne’ma prior to design.

 

Creative Direction & Design

o Development of a creative concept aligned with ne’ma’s brand identity.

o Design of cover, layout templates, infographics, and visual sections.

o Mockups and design proposals for review.

o Design suggestions for format: A4, square, or coffee table format.

o Integration of ne’ma’s image database and sourcing of licensed assets (e.g., Shutterstock) as needed.

o Visual representation of program data, metrics, and outcomes through charts, timelines, icons, and infographics.

o Design and production of section dividers, highlight pages, and call-out pages for emphasis.

o Recommendations for layout best practices to improve readability, navigation, and content hierarchy.

 

Bilingual Content & Translation

o English copyediting and proofreading of all materials.

o High-quality Arabization and translation of approved English content into Arabic.

o Cultural and linguistic adaptation for relevance and impact.

 

Digital & Print Versions

o Print-ready design files with all specifications (open-source and packaged).

o Web-optimized PDF version suitable for ne’ma website and social sharing.

o Mobile-friendly interactive PDF (optional based on design concept).

o Suggestions for interactive digital features (e.g., embedded links, visual navigation aids).

Packaging & Printing

o Recommendations on packaging concept and material (e.g., sleeve, box, envelope).

o Final print specifications: paper stock, binding, size, and finishing.

rows.description:
Please find the brief below for your reference:

HERMES BRIEF

Context

Hermès Parfums is preparing a high-level business presentation for the new President of Hermès. The meeting, taking place in Dubai on November 13 but an content draft and design concept must be shared by November 6th, and this presentation will serve as an important first impression between the regional team and the new leadership. The deck will be reviewed by 4–5 top executives, including the President, Director, and Head of Hermès Parfum.

The presentation’s objective is to provide a business review for the MENA, Turkey, and India region, covering commercial, marketing, training, and finance operations.

Current Situation

The client expressed dissatisfaction with:

  • Design: lacking creativity, polish, and standardization.
  • Narrative flow: disjointed storytelling and unclear transitions between sections.
  • Content optimization: excessive charts, redundant data, and poorly framed titles.

They also require data and content to be transferred into a new Hermès-approved theme, maintaining the brand’s signature look and feel while enhancing the overall storytelling quality.

Purpose & Expectations

  • Impress and inform the newly appointed President, emphasizing the region’s strong performance compared to other zones.
  • Deliver a holistic yet numbers-driven overview, balancing storytelling and analytical clarity.
  • Reflect a modern, strategic presentation flow — moving from past performance (2025) into future outlook (2026).
  • Create a seamless, elegant narrative that resonates with Hermès’ design ethos and luxury brand tone.

Key Challenges

  • Audience profile: Highly analytical, results-oriented leadership with limited patience for fluff — prefers sharp, factual communication supported by clear visuals.
  • Content density: Current slides are overloaded with data visualizations, especially in the “Market Facts” section.
  • Flow & grouping: Need to reorganize sections into a more coherent storyline (e.g., from macro context → regional performance → market specifics → strategic outlook).
  • Design cohesion: Align visual hierarchy, ensure layout consistency, and integrate the official Hermès theme while enhancing creative depth.

Suggested Narrative Structure (take with a grain of salt)

  1. Introduction & Context
    • Who we are / Regional scope (MENA, Turkey, India)
    • Overview of commercial, marketing, training, and finance structure
  2. Regional Snapshot
    • Demographics, GDP, and market overview (condensed and visually clean)
  3. Performance Highlights
    • Key numbers, growth vs. other zones, year-on-year improvement
  4. Market Insights
    • Sales through partners and retail channels
    • Consumer product performance
    • Key market (COP JV in UAE, Qatar, Kuwait)
  5. Strategic Outlook (2025–2026)
    • Upcoming goals and initiatives
    • Alignment with annual Hermès global theme
  6. Closing
    • Key takeaways, confidence in regional growth, and call for leadership alignment

Design Direction

  • Use the official Hermès theme and brand color palette.
  • Introduce creative yet minimal visual storytelling — elegant layouts, consistent iconography, and refined typography.
  • Ensure data visualization simplicity — fewer charts, more insight-driven graphics.
  • Apply grouped and modular slide design for smoother transitions and easy updates in future iterations.

Next Steps

  • Send quote and close deal
  • Internal briefing session between BD, CS, and Creative Teams (strategy + design)
  • Schedule a deep dive kickoff with the client to:
    • Validate initial brief
    • Confirm updated data sources.
    • Align on visual approach and key deliverables.
    • Set clear milestones/ timelines/ plan

--------------------

Important dates:

Tomorrow: CS, creative team and strategy team to meet Hermes for kickoff! (The client will confirm the time). We will have another debrief meeting also tomorrow before the call

6th of Nov :  Content Draft and Concept EOD

12th of Nov : Full File EOD

13th of Nov : Edits and iterations

The client agreed to mostly go with a refined/ enhanced look and feel. Amplified for covers/ separators/ content light slides but overall we can go with a refined and clean look.

You can find below the presentation, a template they use for their internal comms and the "Marketing and Commercial Plan _ The Boutique Group" to consider it as a reference: https://prezlab.sharepoint.com/sites/ClientDatabase5.0/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FClientDatabase5%2E0%2FShared%20Documents%2FHerm%C3%A8s%2F2025%2FNovember%2FMEIA%20Business%20Review%20%2D%20Nov%202025%2FMaterial&viewid=87324605%2D829b%2D4ace%2D8d35%2D27d454daedda&p=true&ct=1761034174870&or=OWA%2DNT%2DMail&cid=9afbdf4e%2D000c%2D89f3%2D56e3%2D3260302d2743&ga=1

rows.taskName: S01922 | Ulo x Market Securities – UK Local Authorities Housing Crisis Presentation Brief
rows.description:
Context

Market Securities is working with Hulo, a real estate company offering a fully integrated housing delivery solution tailored for the public sector.

They approached PrezLab to create a visually powerful and professional presentation targeted at UK local authorities—including municipalities, boroughs, and councils—who are currently facing a severe housing crisis.

The goal is to build a deck that communicates urgency, positions Hulo as a turnkey, low-risk solution, and speaks in the institutional tone needed to gain trust and buy-in from government stakeholders.

This presentation is meant to be both presented in meetings and shared as a standalone document for council teams to review internally.

Objective

Create a punchy, institutional-grade presentation aimed at UK local authorities to pitch Hulo's all-in-one housing solution.

The presentation must: (all design aspect as we won't be working on the content)

  • Show that the housing crisis is real and urgent
  • Offer Hulo as the only viable, no-risk solution
  • Reflect a professional, data-driven tone aligned with how councils operate
  • Be visually clear, graph-based, and light on heavy text

Key Themes

  • Housing policy + public real estate pressure
  • Decision-making within local councils
  • Operational ease and funding models
  • Institutional delivery setup
  • Control, accountability, and scale

Target Audience

  • Decision-makers within local councils, boroughs, and municipalities
  • Likely to include chief executives, policy leads, and finance officers
  • Conservative, risk-sensitive, highly institutional in behavior

Tone & Style

  • Serious and professional
  • Visually minimal with strong use of data
  • Zero fluff, no startup-style exaggeration
  • Every slide must feel purposeful and credible

Slide Format Expectations

Each slide should include:

  • A clear, benefit-driven title
  • A custom graph or visual
  • A few concise bullet points (max 3–5)

Deliverables

  • Full presentation in editable PowerPoint
  • Custom infographics and charts
  • Up to 2 rounds of revisions in each stage
  • Optional: Visual direction and light branding (e.g., temporary logo, color suggestions)

rows.taskName: Katana Studios- T1- COAT Pitch Deck
rows.description:
Katana Studio is preparing an investor deck aimed at PIF and key Saudi investors, with the goal of raising funds and building long-term confidence. The deck will serve to introduce COATcreate, highlight strategic partnerships, showcase impactful case studies, and communicate capabilities in immersive and digital twin technologies. The presentation will consist of 26 slides and will be delivered in a PPT Immersive format.

The strategy phase will focus on restructuring the content to ensure clarity, flow, and maximum impact on investors. The objective is to craft a compelling narrative that positions Katana Studio as a high-potential, innovation-driven business.

Key tasks include:

  • Restructuring and enhancing the content to improve clarity and investor appeal
  • Crafting a strong overarching narrative with a clear value proposition
  • Defining the slide structure, covering:
    • Introduction
    • Problem
    • Solution
    • Technological Edge
    • Case Studies
    • Strategic Partnerships
    • Business Model
    • Financials
    • Team
  • Integrating storytelling elements and investor-specific messaging throughout the deck

  • Design Phase (Immersive)
  • The design phase will bring the strategy to life using immersive visuals and dynamic layouts tailored to a high-stakes investor audience. The design will be developed with high visual fidelity, incorporating motion and interactive elements where relevant.
  • Design deliverables include:
    • Execution of the deck based on an approved concept
    • High-quality visuals with animated transitions
    • Use of motion design and cutout visuals, particularly for case studies and technology showcases
    • Creating an engaging, immersive experience aligned with investor expectations 
  • Key Points to Highlight in the Deck
  • The presentation will emphasize Katana Studio’s strategic and technological strengths to position it as both a regional innovator and a global player.
  • Key messaging areas:
    • Integration with NVIDIA and Apple (COATcreate)
    • Leadership in immersive experiences and digital twin technologies
    • High-ROI real-world case studies that demonstrate effectiveness and tech capability
    • A scalable business model with strong regional relevance and global potential
    • Clear positioning of Katana Studio as a cutting-edge innovator ready for investment

rows.description: Format all slides after slide 10, ensuring consistency with the color scheme used in the first eight slides. Adjust alignments and apply minor refinements as needed to enhance overall visual look and presentation quality.

1. General Requirements:
    • The will provide the open file for the images.
    • Slides highlighted in yellow serve as the structural reference; both the structure and content should follow these slides. However feel free to suggest something else.
    • Use dark green as the primary color.
    • Apply Bahij font throughout the presentation.
    • The client will provide the required images and map.
    • No animation is required.
    • Work-in-progress (WIP) updates should be shared for each section.
    • Maintain a mix of light and dark themes.
    • Image selection should be professional and relevant, focusing on cityscapes, environmental inspection, and infrastructure maintenance (avoid overly playful visuals).
  2. Slide-Specific Guidelines:

Slide 1: Riyadh Growth & Urban Expansion

  • Highlight how Riyadh's growth accelerated between 1970 and 1980.
  • Emphasize the areas and population expansion during this period.
  • Visually highlight the years 1970 and 1980 in the design.
  • Focus on the green areas of the map, minimizing white space.
  • Consider an alternative version excluding 1940 and 1950, displaying only 1970 and 1980.
  • Feel free to explore different structural approaches.

Slide 2: Milestones & Achievements

  • Due to Riyadh’s rapid evolution in the 1980s, strategic planning and establishment were necessary.
  • Include a photo of King Salman as a key figure.
  • Highlight 1974 as a major milestone.
  • Display key achievements below this section.

Slide 3: Riyadh’s Role in Transformation

  • Demonstrate how Riyadh was a key pillar in the national transformation program.
  • The slide should be structured into three distinct parts (not necessarily vertical).

Slide 4: The Role of the Royal Commission

  • Emphasize that the Royal Commission's role is broad and spans four main pillars.
  • Highlight its impact across multiple sectors, including tourism (سياحة) and housing (إسكان).

Slide 5: Overlapping Activities of Riyadh Municipality

  • Show how activities overlap across different sectors.
  • There are five main activities, with logos of Amanah and RCRC displayed under them.
  • Harvey balls should represent the significance of the overlap.
  • Include a screenshot of deep-dive analysis.

Slide 6: Project Details

  • Visualize key projects in alignment with the overall design.

Slide 8: Pie Chart Design

  • Ensure the pie chart on the map follows Al Amaneh’s color scheme.

Slide 12: Overview of Services

  • (الخدمة الذكية) applies to both committees.

Slide 14: Supervision & Inspection

  • Maintain clarity in illustrating roles and responsibilities.

Slide 16: Benchmarking

  • Add flags of relevant countries for context.
  • Ensure numbers are aligned to the left, as they represent rankings.

Slide 18: Key Learnings

  • Summarize insights in a structured format.

Slide 20: References

  • Organize reference materials effectively.

Deep Dive Sections:

  1. Title & Action Title
    • Categorize three key areas and showcase the network to develop them.
    • Infographic-style representation using charts or visual elements.
    • Each deep dive should conclude with an impact section.
  2. Alseyaneh (Maintenance)
    • Images on the right should serve as placeholders.
    • Include actual vs. target numbers.
    • Ensure the pie chart aligns with the map's color scheme.
  3. Al Rukhas (Licensing)
    • Make the “Duration and Fee” (حالات عدم تقاطع) stand out.
  4. Al Khidma Al Raqamiyya (Digital Services)
    • Present three key elements of digital solutions.
    • Highlight that both Amanah and the Royal Commission have applications for the same element.
    • Clarify that Smart City Services (خدمات المدن الذكية) use the same applications but serve different geographies.
  5. Al Raqaba wa Al Tafteesh (Supervision & Inspection)
    • Show that both entities are performing the same activities.
    • The right-hand side (RHS) should illustrate how this impacts Wadi deterioration.

Thank You Slide

  • Design a visually appealing closing slide.

Project Overview

We have been engaged by Aramco to support their upcoming GIM Meeting (13–16 October 2025). This meeting occurs multiple times per year and brings together a diverse audience ranging from C-level executives to vendor representatives.

Our role is to ensure a high-impact presentation experience at the event, covering both content structuring and design as well as speaker support prior to the event.

Our Scope

  1. Presentation Design & Structuring
    • Opening Presentation: ~70 slides
    • Closing Presentation: ~30 slides

    • Reference Material - sharing the one drive link in a bit:

  2. Content Support

  3. Speaker Coaching

    • Focus on presentation delivery and confidence-building

Key Dates

  • Event Dates: 13–16 October 2025
  • Kick-off / Discovery Call: Tomorrow at 9:00 AM
  • Onsite Speaker Coaching: To begin 3–5 days before event start

Branding Guidelines

  • Aramco logo must be used
  • Each event may have its own visual theme, but should align with Aramco’s identity - we can reconfirm the branding part
  • Use provided references for style inspiration
