rows.description:
We will need creative storytelling help from strategy, but the project should be fairly simple

DP World (Jafza 40 Years Presentation)

Purpose: Enhancing and amplifying the Jafza 40 Years presentation for an upcoming government delegation

1. Background & Objective

  • The Marketing team from DP World – is leading the initiative for Jafza’s 40-year celebration.
  • The goal is to enhance the existing “40 Years of Jafza” presentationto make it visually compelling and narrative-driven.
  • This presentation will be shown to high-level government delegates by DP World’s COO in the first week of December 2025.
  • The presentation tells the story of Jafza’s evolution — its contribution to Dubai’s growth, multimodal connectivity, global expansion, and future projects.

2. Project Scope – Phase One

  • Concept Creation : referring to the branding guidelines to be provided by the client
  • Amplify the existing 15 slide deck visually and narratively.
  • Content Strategy : Refine the storyline to create smoother transitions and stronger flow from the 1985 beginnings to Jafza’s global footprint today.
  • Design and animation:
    • Amplified PowerPoint deck ( 15 slides).
    • Visual concept and narrative alignment before design. 
    • TBC :executive suitable animations to match the importance of the meeting.
    • Visualize maps, port growth, and expansion sequences dynamically.
    • Replace static images where needed and rebuild charts in editable format.
  • Branding:
    • Must strictly adhere to Jafza’s official branding guidelines (colors, fonts, tone of voice).
    • The client will connect Prezlab with the branding team to provide the guidelines.

3. Client Requirements & Logistics

  • Timeline:
    • Presentation is due before 4th of December 2025. 
    • Marketing contact will support in fast-tracking communication and approvals.
  • Files & Access:
    • Client will share open editable PowerPoint files, charts, and imagery.
    • Some visuals (e.g., maps) may need to be re-created for animation or clarity.

4. Content Direction

  • The presentation must deliver a strong, powerful message, as it will be presented by the COO to Dubai’s senior council.
  • The core narrative should highlight:
    • History – JAFZA as the first and oldest free zone in the UAE.
    • Growth – How the zone has evolved over 40 years.
    • Current Footprint – Their present-day strength and global positioning.
    • Future Vision – This is the most important part; positioning the next 40 years as even more ambitious and transformative.
  • Key message:
    “Imagine what we achieved in the past 40 years — now imagine what we can do in the next 40.”
  • Themes to emphasize:
    • Technology as a major driver.
    • Future of free zones.
    • Sustainability and advanced technologies.
    • Socio-economic impact.
  • No CEO/COO quotes will be included.
  • For data and charts:
    • We are free to redesign all graphs.
    • We should highlight only the key numbers that matter.

5. Design Direction

  • We have complete creative freedom with the overall visual direction, as long as it remains:
    • Corporate, professional, and high-level.
    • Not overly playful or too creatively abstract.
  • AI-generated visuals are allowed, but they must:
    • Look natural and not artificial or “AI-fake.”
  • We can share the output as a PDF.

Hi Team,

Following our discussion with Omar and his team, please find below a summary of the key points and alignments for the Eagle Environmental Services & Pest Control Annual Report for ADPHC, along with slide-specific directions for our work.

 

Key Alignments

  • The report will be developed in English only at this stage.
  • It will serve as the 2025 Annual Report, prepared in the name of Abu Dhabi Public Health Center (ADPHC).
  • The presentation may be used internally and externally.
  • Deliverable: PowerPoint (PPTX) with animations where suitable and purposeful.
  • The report will be presented by senior leadership, including the Department Director.
  • Tone: informative and awareness-focused.

 

Content & Structure

  • Structure: general overview first, then detailed sections; flexible as the story evolves.
  • All elements should be fully editable where possible.
  • Headlines and supporting content may be enhanced or added to improve clarity and flow.
  • Slides may be split or merged where beneficial for clarity, storytelling, or visual impact, while staying aligned with approved content.
  • Data visualization may be Excel-based where required.
  • Avoid negative framing; challenges should be presented positively.
  • Any content reductions or locked elements will be highlighted and communicated.

 

Design & Visual Direction

  • Align with existing brand elements: colors, graphics, fonts (as used in social media).
  • Where applicable, videos and strong visuals will be included for engagement and clarity.

 

Slide-Specific Notes (Initial Direction)

  • Slide 2: Map showing current areas of operation in Abu Dhabi, including project names.
  • Slide 3: Expand on services provided, with added content for clarity.
  • Slide 4: Use totals related to sources for public health pest control projects.
  • Slide 5: Highlight tools available and actively used by the team; content to be updated.
  • Slide 6: Focused slide on smart traps.
  • Slides 7–8: Integrated Pest Management System; consider merging for better flow.
  • Slide 11: Add contextual info; keep crucial info as main points.
  • Slide 15: Residual Painting – highlight this important service.
  • Slides 12–18: Data-driven; use Excel sheet for accuracy.
  • Slides 19–20: Data to be validated with Excel sheet.
  • Slide 23: Remove unused social media platforms.
  • Slide 24: Present challenges positively.
  • Slide 25: Solutions for the challenges presented in previous slide.
  • Slide 26: Present achievements clearly and impactfully.
  • Slide 27: Summary of general laboratory work.
  • Slide 28: Classification of Testing Activities, scientific studies, and research presented under clear classifications.
  • Slide 29: Future outlook.

 

Items Required from Client Side

To proceed efficiently, we will need:

  • Any previous or similar work for reference
  • Templates, brand guidelines, and image assets
  • Updated content and data
  • Links or references to social media presence
  • Any available content videos

Client Brief – Daylmayr Coffee Machines (Dubai & Sharjah Airports)


Overview


Dalmer has self-service coffee machines placed in airports globally. We need to create three design options for machine wraps, one for Dubai Airport and two for Sharjah Airport.



1. Dubai Airport – Machine Wrap Design


Objective


Create a design wrap for a Dallmayr coffee machine that will be placed in Dubai International Airport. The design must visually communicate that the machine serves both hot and cold beverages.


Key Requirements


Visuals:

  •  Show both hot and cold drink concepts.

  •  If showing cold drinks, no ice cubes are allowed.

  •  You can use color cues (ex: red for hot, blue for cold) to differentiate them. (this is just a suggestion)

Branding:

  •  Include the Dalmer logo, kept small and placed subtly (e.g., top corner).

Tone & Feel:

  •  Clean, modern, airport-friendly.



2. Sharjah Airport – Two Design Mockups


Objective


Create two design concepts for a machine that will be placed in Sharjah Airport.


Key Requirements

Brand Colors:

  •  Use Sharjah Airport’s color palette for visual harmony.

        Branding Restrictions:

  •  Cannot include: Sharjah Airport logo

  •  Cannot include: Dallmayr logo

Messaging:

  •  Add a short, friendly travel-themed slogan (not referencing Dallmayr).

  •  Should feel airport-appropriate and welcoming.


Slogan Options (Choose any to inspire the design) (I came up with these options, feel free to add something else)

  •  “Fuel your journey.”

  •  “Your travel companion in every sip.”

  •  “A smooth takeoff starts with coffee.”

  •  “Sip, relax, and enjoy the journey.”

  •  “Café comfort on your way to fly.”

  •  “Grab a sip before you go.”

  •  “Coffee for every destination.”



Deliverables

  •  1 design for Dubai Airport machine wrap.

  •  2 design mockups for Sharjah Airport machine wraps.

Project Background

The Emirates Group is conducting a series of school roadshows to promote its four scholarship programmes for UAE Nationals. These initiatives are part of Rehlaty, the Group’s Emiratisation strategy aimed at developing and retaining Emirati talent.

The scholarships support students in pursuing academic pathways that lead to long-term career opportunities within the Emirates Group.

During each school presentation, the audience is addressed by four key stakeholders:

  • One main presenter, who delivers the core presentation and provides an overview of the Bachelor’s programme.
  • Three Emirates Group employees who are programme graduates:

    • An Engineer
    • An IT Specialist

These employees share personal insights and experiences from their respective career paths within the Group.

x2 concepts

rows.description:
About: Dallmayr is a coffee machine and vending machine provider and is one of the most prominent ones in the world in this industry. They have been working with Dubai Airports to provide vending machines for over 10 years but now their contract renewal is coming up and they need to send a defensive conceptual proposal to the airport

 

Objective: Showcase Dallmayr's capabilities and offerings to prove that they are the right vendor to continue the relationship for another 10 year contract. A conceptual pitch deck is required which will be displayed as a slideshow by Dubai Airports. They require a WOW factor with not too much text and more emphasis on visuals. The exact ask from the client is that it needs to "not look like a PowerPoint"

 

Recommended Approach: Let's go with a story that emphasis YOU more than US especially in the beginning of the deck and then tailors the offerings and capabilities to showcase why Dallmayr is the perfect partner for Dubai Airports. We can also go with a sleek, modern design style with vendine machine mockups and perhaps some AI generated imagery to showcase future vending machine concepts similar to attached inspiration. Some nice morph effects to bring the machines in and out of the screen would be amazing, adding some infographic overlays on top to communicate the key selling points.

 

Some tips on copy: Let's try to go with engaging copy like "Sweet satisfaction for every traveler" or "Taking off together" so we can play on the whole vending machine/ airport/ traveler experience aspect and make it more human-centric

 

SOW:

  • Content restructuring (60 hrs)
  • Immersive concept (20 hrs)
  • Immersive design of up to 20 - 25 slides including machine mockups and some AI generated imagery to showcase futuristic machine concepts (60 hrs) (ask Sanad and marketing team for support here

Timelines:

  • 16th of March:
    • Full content structure + content draft for the slides with basic formatting (just to give an idea about storyline and content without full blow design)
    • 3 - 5 slides designed with the design vision/ direction we are taking with the slides. This shows off the design aesthetic 
  • 23rd of April: Full immersive presentation slideshow with animations, some machine mockups, and for one or two slides we can use AI generation to showcase futuristic/ modern machine concepts

Project Brief – Richemont Procurement Strategy Presentation

 

Overview:

We have an exciting new project for Richemont that differs from the usual townhall presentations. The presentation will showcase the Procurement Strategy Plan to the CEO and ExCo, delivered by the Global Procurement Director (recently joined). This is a high-impact opportunity, as it is the first time in 2 years the procurement team is presenting to the CEO, and the director is presenting in this context for the first time.

 

Currently, Procurement is perceived as a transactional, tactical function. The team wants to reposition Procurement as strategic, unified, and forward-looking.

 

Objective / Outcome:

  • Deliver key messages in a simple, impactful way
  • Gain approval on their requests and strategic initiatives

Audience:

  • CEO (prefers traditional, understated style)
  • ExCo

Presentation Details:

  • Duration: 10 minutes
  • Tone & Design Direction: Elegant, simple, corporate, visually neat without heavy colors or overly fancy elements.
  • Content Support:
    • Improve overall narrative
    • Keep slides clean and minimal text
    • Highlight current achievements and key numbers
  • Design Support:
    • Corporate, professional look (different from townhall style)
    • Include subtle transitions and animations while ensuring printability

Services:

  • Content / Strategy support
  • Presentation Design

Timeline & Milestones:

DateTask
Today – ThursdayWork on content draft
Tomorrow – Friday morningDevelop concept slides
FridayCall with client to review updated content and concept
After approvalBuild full presentation

Wednesday 3rd of Dec (latest)

Deliver final presentation (deadline may shift by 1–2 days if comments are extensive)

 

Assets:

  • [Presentation File]
  • [Brand Guidelines & Previous Template] – I will confirm if we should follow the brand guidelines - the presentation template is just a reference

Notes / Considerations:

  • CEO prefers efficiency and clarity over visual flair
  • Focus on communicating strategy, evolution, and impact
  • Maintain corporate professionalism while keeping slides visually engaging

Thanks a mill team!

1. Project Overview:
    We aim to design the static presentation with a look and feel similar to the Code Blue website to maintain familiarity. This document is intended as a pitch for the committee. Once approved, we’ll discuss the broader scope, which will be part of the full presentation.
  2. Structure of the Pitch:
    • The left side of the pitch will contain the spoken portion (the part you'll present verbally).
    • The right side will display the visual materials that will be shown on the screen once the presentation is approved.
    • The overall design of the pitch and presentation will be static, with the focus primarily on the content shown on the right side (the visual materials).
  3. Slide Details:
    We plan to create 5-6 slides to showcase our work. We can walk you through the slides on Thursday, April 9th, at 11:00 AM KSA.

rows.description:
Design Brief – Emirates IT Recruitment Roadshow Presentation

Overview

Following the success of the Pilots Roadshow presentation, Emirates is expanding this initiative across other departments including IT, Cabin Crew, National Cadets, Engineering, and AMEL.

We will begin with the IT Roadshow presentation, which is unique in its dual role supporting both Emirates and dnata. As such, this deck will follow a hybrid branding approach that reflects elements from both entities.

Project Objective

Create a high-impact, narrative-driven presentation that positions Emirates IT as a competitive, attractive employer - rivaling global tech giants - and appealing to high-caliber talent in the technology sector.

Services Scope

We will support across two key areas:

  1. Content Development & Strategic Messaging
    • Craft compelling and relevant content tailored to tech-savvy candidates.
    • Position Emirates IT as a desirable career destination.
  2. Design Execution
    • Deliver a polished, on-brand presentation with high visual appeal.
    • Create a cohesive story that supports both Emirates and dnata’s positioning.

Content Brief

Audience Insight:

The IT department is often seen as technical and less engaging. The goal is to shift perception and create content that resonates with elite tech talent (e.g., candidates from companies like Google or Meta).

Key Content Focus:

  • Slides 5–17 are the priority. These will be refined slide-by-slide to ensure:
    • Clearer narrative structure.
    • Stronger appeal and engagement.
    • Logical storytelling with a defined value proposition.
  • Emphasis will be placed on:
    • Emirates IT’s culture, growth opportunities, and impact.
    • Unique benefits and “why Emirates over others” messaging.

Secondary Content:

  • Sections like employee benefits are standardized and will receive only light refinement.
  • An About the Authors and Contact Page are to be added (names, bios, contact email).
  • Introduction page from the client has been added—pending value confirmation from stakeholders.

Content Tasks Include:

  • Improving titles for narrative flow.
  • Introducing a content tracker.
  • Rewriting for clarity and skimmability.
  • Optimizing content layout to support visuals.

Design Scope

Deliverables:

  • 2 initial design concepts aligned with Emirates and dnata branding.
  • Up to 35 slides in amplified design.
  • Light animation and transitions to guide storytelling flow.

Visual Direction:

  • A hybrid branding approach blending Emirates and dnata identity.
  • Use of AI-generated and stock imagery to compensate for limited department visuals.
  • Destination-themed visuals for sections like benefits to highlight the travel aspect.

Timeline

  • Roadshow Date: Early August
  • Internal Deadline: July 9th

Reference Material

  • Here you can find the material received (Presentation to work on: IT Recruitment Info Session deck Germany_02062025)
  • IT Careers Website
  • Pilots Video Presentation – Sample Reference

Create visually stunning newsletter layouts, ensuring optimal reader engagement

with design elements that highlight your content (up to 5 pages or one long page)

Design Brief for Dubai Reef Pitch Deck:

  • Project Overview: The pitch deck will consist of 15-20 slides aimed at enhancing visual appeal and better communicating key messaging to C-suite executives, directors, and GMs to help secure sponsorships.
  • Branding: The deck will be DET-branded, but the Dubai Reef logo will be used instead of DET’s logo.
  • Design Style: Formal, clean, sleek, and visually compelling. The design should ensure the content is highly visual, emphasizing clarity and impact. DET images should be incorporated where possible.
  • Adaptability: The deck will need to be adaptable for different audiences, but we'll create one general version that will cover all use cases.
  • Animations: No animations required. Focus will be on creating a polished and professional look.
  • Deadline: The deck is expected to be ready a few days after Eid. We’ll receive content and images from the client once the quotation is approved, so we can proceed accordingly.
  • Next Steps:
    • Start by creating a concept with 3-4 slides using the content shared. Once this is approved, proceed with the full deck design.
    • Ensure that the deck stays visually consistent and follows the guidelines mentioned above.

      Material  https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1LE6BJzOF1Sk3Xy24PzqH_MrxB-mA49kp/edit?slide=id.p4#slide=id.p4

rows.description:
This project is based on the GI4 framework, which is structured around four key pillars: People, Community, Business, and Tech. The information in these slides is categorized under these four pillars.

  • The People slide is straightforward, but the main focus is on the Business pillar. Here, we have the creative freedom to amplify the content and incorporate a compelling storytelling approach and visual style, while ensuring alignment with ALJ guidelines.
  • The Business slide is divided into two sections:
    1. Approved initiatives – Projects that received approval in 2024 to move forward in 2025.
    2. Current projects – Ongoing initiatives that are actively being worked on.

Our goal is to enhance the impact of this presentation by refining the narrative, structuring the content effectively, and applying a strong visual and storytelling approach. We also have the flexibility to restructure and split the content in a way that improves clarity and engagement.

rows.description:
Project Title 

Hermès Parfums EMEA – 2025 Year-End Review Video 

Project Type 

Inspirational recap video (internal audience) with minimal animation 

Objective & Purpose 

Create a motivating and inspiring 3–4 minute video that celebrates Hermès Parfums EMEA 2024 achievements, activations, milestones, and data points. The video will be used internally to recognize success, boost team morale, and set a positive tone for continued excellence in 2025. 

Creative Direction 

  • Visual style should align with Hermès’ existing hand-drawn theme for 2025. 
  • Keep the animation / motion approach simple, elegant, and minimal, leaning into subtle transitions rather than complex motion graphics. 
  • Video will be mostly dominated by images and videos shared by the client for key activations. 
  • Tone should feel premium, artistic, warm, and story-driven, reflecting Hermès’ craft DNA. 
  • Reference material: Client was happy with last year’s video HERE 

Narrative & Content Notes 

  • Structure will remain linear and clean: 
    For example: Intro → Highlights → Activations → Numbers/Impact → Closing Outlook 
  • No voice-over — storytelling relies on on-screen text + visuals + music. 
  • Background music should be engaging, emotional, and uplifting, non-distracting. 
  • Content Strategy to build flow and identify key narrative beats. 
    Copywriter to craft short inspirational statements that appear throughout the video. 

Technical Requirements 

  • Duration: 3–4 minutes 
  • Dimensions: 16:9 
  • Animation level: Light / simple transitions 
  • Delivery: High-resolution MP4 (Final + editable project files) 

Key Considerations / Constraints 

  • Budget-conscious — avoid high-complexity animation, 3D, or heavy compositing. 
  • Visual consistency with Hermès’ brand world is critical. 
  • Motion should support emotion, not overwhelm it. 
  • The video is due on the second week of February. 
  • The content can be sent immediately from the client, but certain activations which are currently running or happening in December will have their content ready end of Dec/ Beginning of Jan. In the meantime, we can start working on the skeleton and keep placeholders for missing content. 

Presentation we worked on for your reference:  

Reference file
